hardGate.title: Verified answers require non-hidden referenced fields
hardGate.requirement: Verified answers won't work if they reference hidden columns in the model, so fields referenced by a verified answer must not be hidden.

hardGate.title: Enable Power BI Q&A on the model (prerequisite for Prep data for AI)
hardGate.requirement: Power BI Q&A must be turned on for the model; if Q&A isn't enabled, the Prep data for AI tabs (AI data schema, AI instructions, verified answers) are disabled.

hardGate.title: AI instructions are limited to 10,000 characters
hardGate.requirement: Keep AI instructions within the 10,000 character limit.

hardGate.title: Cap total data sources per agent at five
hardGate.requirement: A single Fabric data agent can have up to five data sources total (lakehouses, warehouses, Power BI semantic models, KQL databases, ontologies, Microsoft Graph) in any combination.

hardGate.title: Example queries require a question plus valid query; each must pass schema validation
hardGate.requirement: Every example query must include both a natural-language question and its corresponding query; each question should be unique; every example is validated against the selected data source schema and examples that fail validation are not sent to the agent.

hardGate.title: Example queries NOT supported for semantic models or ontologies
hardGate.requirement: Example queries (few-shot) are supported only for Lakehouse, Warehouse, and Eventhouse/KQL data sources; they are NOT supported for Power BI Semantic Models or Ontologies — for semantic models use Verified Answers instead.

hardGate.title: Semantic models: DAX tool uses ONLY Prep for AI + metadata, ignores agent instructions
hardGate.requirement: For Power BI semantic models the DAX generation tool relies solely on the model's metadata and Prep for AI configurations and ignores any instructions added at the data agent level. Therefore do not put semantic-model-specific instructions at the agent level; keep them in Prep for AI. Semantic models also do not support data-source instructions or descriptions at the agent level.

hardGate.title: Paid capacity F2+ or P1+ required
hardGate.requirement: The semantic model/report must reside in a workspace backed by a paid Fabric capacity (F2 or higher) or Power BI Premium (P1 or higher). A Pro or PPU license alone is insufficient; Copilot/Q&A data questions are unavailable without qualifying capacity. The readiness engine should flag/treat capacity tier as a hard prerequisite for any Copilot scoring.

hardGate.title: Trial SKUs and free SKUs not supported
hardGate.requirement: Copilot in Microsoft Fabric is not supported on trial SKUs or trial capacities; only paid SKUs (F2+ or P1+) work. A model living on a trial capacity cannot be scored as Copilot-ready.

hardGate.title: Capacity must be in a supported region
hardGate.requirement: The Fabric F/P capacity must be in a Copilot-supported region (per Fabric region availability). If the capacity is not in a supported region, Copilot cannot be used at all.

hardGate.title: Cross-geo Azure OpenAI tenant setting required outside US/EU boundary
hardGate.requirement: If the tenant or capacity is outside the United States or EU data boundary (docs also phrase as 'US or France'), Copilot is disabled by default unless the tenant admin enables 'Data sent to Azure OpenAI can be processed outside your tenant's geographic region, compliance boundary, or national cloud instance.'

hardGate.title: Tenant admin Copilot setting must be enabled
hardGate.requirement: The tenant-level setting 'Users can use Copilot and other features powered by Azure OpenAI' must be enabled (enabling it only at the capacity/delegated level is insufficient for the standalone and app-scoped experiences). Copilot is on by default but an admin may disable it.

hardGate.title: Sovereign clouds not supported
hardGate.requirement: Copilot is not supported in sovereign clouds due to GPU availability. Models in sovereign/national cloud deployments cannot be Copilot-ready.

hardGate.title: Q&A must be enabled for Copilot data questions
hardGate.requirement: Copilot data questions require Power BI Q&A to be enabled on the model, because Copilot uses the same engine to build queries. For most sources Q&A is on by default. The readiness engine should add a LIMIT/DataAgentConfig rule that fails when Q&A is disabled on the model (matches existing enable_qna MCP tool).

hardGate.title: DirectQuery / Direct Lake models require manual Q&A enablement
hardGate.requirement: For DirectQuery or Direct Lake models, Q&A may need to be enabled manually in semantic model settings (service) or Data Load settings (Desktop). A readiness check should detect DirectQuery/Direct Lake storage and verify Q&A is explicitly enabled, since it is NOT on by default for these.

hardGate.title: Data Agent ignores agent-level instructions for DAX; use Prep for AI
hardGate.requirement: When querying a semantic model, the Data Agent DAX generation tool uses ONLY the model's metadata and Prep-for-AI config (AI data schema, verified answers, AI instructions); instructions added at the agent level are ignored for DAX. Model-specific guidance must live in Prep for AI. The DataAgentConfig category should require AI instructions on the model, not the agent.

hardGate.title: Prep for AI requires Q&A enabled (preview gate)
hardGate.requirement: Using Prep data for AI features requires Power BI Q&A to be enabled on the semantic model. This couples the Prep-for-AI / Approved-for-Copilot readiness path to the Q&A-enablement gate.

hardGate.title: Verified answers must not reference hidden fields
hardGate.requirement: Verified answers do not work if any field they reference (including filter fields) is hidden in the model. A rule should flag any verified answer whose referenced columns/measures/filter fields are hidden, and ensure all fields used in a verified answer are visible in the model and unhidden in schema selection.

hardGate.title: Verified answers must not use report-level measures or unsupported visuals
hardGate.requirement: Verified answers do not work with visuals that contain report measures, and a defined set of visual types is unsupported (textbox, Data Q&A, AI visuals, key influencers, narrative/smart narrative, decomposition tree, forecasting, custom visuals). A rule can flag verified answers built on report measures or unsupported visual types.

hardGate.title: Enable Power BI Q&A as a prerequisite for all Prep-for-AI features
hardGate.requirement: Power BI Q&A must be turned on for the semantic model; if Q&A isn't enabled, the AI data schema, verified answers, and AI instructions tabs in Prep for AI are all disabled. This makes Q&A a gating prerequisite for every Prep-for-AI optimization, strengthening the existing SYN-SCHEMA rule's importance. Use enable_qna when missing.

hardGate.title: A data agent supports at most five data sources
hardGate.requirement: A Fabric data agent supports up to five data sources in any combination (lakehouses, warehouses, KQL databases, Power BI semantic models, ontologies, Microsoft Graph). A data-agent-config rule should flag configurations exceeding five sources.

hardGate.title: Sample question/query example pairs are NOT supported for semantic models
hardGate.requirement: Example queries (few-shot pairs) improve query generation for SQL/KQL sources, but adding sample query/question pairs is NOT currently supported for Power BI semantic model data sources. A data-agent-config rule must NOT recommend example queries for semantic-model sources, and should route all model-specific guidance to Prep-for-AI instead. (Per source, up to 100 example queries for supported source types.)

hardGate.title: RLS and CLS still apply through a data agent; only Read permission is needed
hardGate.requirement: Access to a semantic model through a data agent is governed by Read permission on the model (Build/workspace access not required for agent-driven queries); Row-Level Security (RLS) and Column-Level Security (CLS) still apply and the agent uses the requesting user's credentials. A readiness/governance rule should confirm RLS/CLS are correctly configured since the agent enforces them per-user.

hardGate.title: Data agent enforces read-only access and Microsoft Purview governance
hardGate.requirement: The data agent enforces strictly read-only data connections and respects Microsoft Purview governance controls (DLP and access-restriction policies) on underlying sources; responses may be truncated/blocked and sensitive assets may be inaccessible per policy. Governance precedence: organizational > role-based > developer > user intent. Relevant context for any DataAgentConfig category and governance readiness checks.

hardGate.title: Data agent responses are capped at 25 rows x 25 columns (English-only)
hardGate.requirement: Fabric data agents are designed for conversational insights, not full dataset retrieval: responses are capped at a maximum of 25 rows and 25 columns and may be summarized. Agents currently support English only for questions, instructions, and example queries. Relevant scale/expectation context: readiness messaging should not promise full data dumps and should encourage measures/aggregations over wide raw tables.

hardGate.title: Data agent and source workspace capacities must be co-located (region) and F2+/P1+
hardGate.requirement: A data agent can't execute queries when the data source's workspace capacity is in a different region than the data agent's workspace capacity; it also requires F2+ Fabric capacity (or P1+ Premium with Fabric enabled) and cross-geo AI tenant settings enabled. Relevant pre-flight readiness gate for deploying a model to a data agent / Direct Lake on OneLake.

recommended.title: Human-readable, business-friendly names for tables, columns, and measures
recommended.requirement: Name tables, columns, and measures in human-readable English using clear, business-friendly terms that reflect how users naturally refer to the data; avoid acronyms, abbreviations, codes, and excessive punctuation (e.g. 'Total Revenue' not 'TR_AMT', 'Sales Region' not 'DIM_GEO_01').

recommended.title: Unambiguous, self-explanatory column names (no bare IDs/codes)
recommended.requirement: Make column names unambiguous and self-explanatory; avoid IDs or codes that require further lookup without context (e.g. rename 'ProdID' to 'Product ID' or 'Product Name', 'CustNo' to 'Customer Number').

recommended.title: Measure names should reflect their calculation and purpose
recommended.requirement: Give measures descriptive names that clearly reflect their calculation and purpose rather than abbreviated names (e.g. 'Average Customer Rating' instead of 'AvgRating').

recommended.title: Avoid duplicate / same field names across tables
recommended.requirement: Avoid having fields with the same name across different tables; ambiguous duplicate names cause Copilot to filter the wrong field. Use concise descriptions to distinguish between similarly named fields (e.g. 'Name' in Customer vs 'Name' in Store).

recommended.title: Consolidate or differentiate duplicate / overlapping measures
recommended.requirement: Multiple measures that calculate similar metrics (e.g. Total Sales, Sales Amount, Revenue) create ambiguity; consolidate or clearly differentiate them and exclude duplicates from your AI data schema.

recommended.title: Add descriptions to tables, columns, and measures
recommended.requirement: Add descriptions to every table, column, and measure; in the description property define each element and how you intend to use it. Descriptions are used as grounding and help the LLM understand the purpose of each object. Note only the first 200 characters are used.

recommended.title: Descriptions help disambiguate similarly named fields
recommended.requirement: Use concise descriptions to distinguish between similarly named fields; descriptions help when similar fields exist in the same model (e.g. 'Name' in the Customer table and 'Name' in the Store table). If you can't rename objects, ensure descriptions and synonyms provide the necessary context.

recommended.title: Use calculation group column descriptions to list calculation items
recommended.requirement: Calculation items aren't included in the model metadata, so use the description of the calculation group column to list and explain the calculation items (only the first 200 characters are used).

recommended.title: Description grounding is truncated at 200 characters
recommended.requirement: Keep the load-bearing content of each description within the first 200 characters, because Copilot truncates descriptions after the first 200 characters when using them as grounding data.

recommended.title: Hide columns and measures not meant for consumption
recommended.requirement: Hide columns and measures that aren't intended for Copilot use (e.g. technical/key columns); any hidden measure or column in the model is excluded from Copilot's grounding for the Ask data questions experience.

recommended.title: Hide or rename columns not intended for Copilot to prevent wrong-field answers
recommended.requirement: If a column (e.g. a 'Country' in a Customer table or a 'Birthday' date column) isn't intended to be used by Copilot, hide or rename it so Copilot doesn't filter or group on the wrong field; alternatively disable it from the Q&A field synonyms of the linguistic model.

recommended.title: Create explicit measures instead of relying on implicit measures
recommended.requirement: Avoid relying on implicit measures, which can lead to unpredictable results; create explicit DAX measures for calculations you want users to query, and set the correct default summarization on numeric columns to prevent unintended aggregations.

recommended.title: Provide predefined measures and KPIs users commonly request
recommended.requirement: Include a set of predefined measures and relevant KPIs that users are most likely to request in reports (e.g. YTD Sales, MoM Growth, ROI), defined as explicit measures in the model.

recommended.title: Standardized, clear calculation logic for measures
recommended.requirement: Measures should have standardized, clear calculation logic that's easy to explain and understand, with a description property explaining the calculation.

recommended.title: Exclude helper / intermediate measures from the AI data schema
recommended.requirement: Include only measures that calculate actual business metrics in the AI data schema; exclude helper measures and intermediate objects used only for report interactivity to reduce noise and improve query accuracy.

recommended.title: Define clear relationships with correct cardinality and active/inactive state
recommended.requirement: Ensure all relationships between tables are clearly defined and logical; specify cardinality (one-to-many, many-to-one, many-to-many) and whether relationships are active or inactive (mark role-playing relationships inactive where appropriate).

recommended.title: Use a star schema with clear fact and dimension tables
recommended.requirement: Follow a star schema design with clearly delineated fact tables (measurable quantitative data) and dimension tables (descriptive attributes); avoid flat/denormalized or pivoted/wide structures. DAX is optimized for star schema.

recommended.title: Poor star-schema design produces poor Copilot results
recommended.requirement: Follow common modeling best practices such as a star schema design; a model that doesn't follow them is likely to give poor Copilot results with any consumption tool.

recommended.title: Establish logical hierarchies for drill-down
recommended.requirement: Establish clear hierarchies within the data, especially for dimension tables that could be used to drill down (e.g. Date: Year > Quarter > Month > Day; Geography: Country > State > City).

recommended.title: Apply correct and consistent data types
recommended.requirement: Apply correct and consistent data types for columns across all tables so measures calculate correctly and sorting/filtering works (e.g. SaleAmount as Decimal Number, Date as Date, ProductID as Whole Number).

recommended.title: Mark a date table so Copilot filters time correctly
recommended.requirement: Mark your date table so Copilot filters time-based questions using the marked date table rather than an unrelated date column (e.g. a 'Birthday' column in a Customer table), which otherwise produces incorrect results.

recommended.title: Disambiguate multiple date fields
recommended.requirement: Multiple date columns (Order Date, Ship Date, Due Date, Calendar/FY Quarter, etc.) without clear guidance confuse the AI; remove all but one date field from Q&A, or use Verified Answers and AI instructions to specify which date field to use by default or for specific question types.

recommended.title: Set up linguistic modeling: field synonyms
recommended.requirement: Set up synonyms for fields so Copilot/Q&A understands business-specific terms; Copilot relies on the same linguistic modeling as Q&A, and field synonyms map alternative user terms to model fields. Without synonyms Copilot is more likely to return unexpected results.

recommended.title: Define linguistic relationships (phrasings / verbs)
recommended.requirement: Define linguistic relationships (phrasings, e.g. 'stores sell products') so the engine understands verbs and qualifying phrases that relate terms, beyond simple field-name synonyms.

recommended.title: Define an AI data schema (focused subset of fields)
recommended.requirement: Define an AI data schema in Prep data for AI selecting only the most relevant tables, columns, and measures (clean fields with limited ambiguity); this focused subset is what Copilot prioritizes when generating responses and reduces ambiguity for clearer, more accurate answers.

recommended.title: Include dependent objects when defining the AI data schema
recommended.requirement: When selecting an AI data schema, also include dependent objects (e.g. if a Total Revenue measure references other measures that depend on additional columns, include all those dependent objects).

recommended.title: Add AI instructions for business context, terminology, and analysis rules
recommended.requirement: Add AI instructions to provide business context, terminology definitions, and analysis rules (e.g. define 'top performer', specify which measure to use for a term, default groupings). Be explicit and specific, group related instructions, keep them focused (fewer focused instructions beat many broad ones), and avoid conflicting instructions.

recommended.title: Create verified answers for common or ambiguous questions
recommended.requirement: Create verified answers (trigger phrases mapped to a selected visual, saved at the semantic-model level) for common or complex questions that might otherwise be misinterpreted; use 5-7 trigger questions covering formal and conversational phrasings, configure up to three filters, and use complete/robust questions rather than partial phrases.

recommended.title: Update verified answers when referenced objects are renamed
recommended.requirement: If you rename any table, column, or measure referenced in a verified answer, update the verified answer and save it again for the changes to take effect.

recommended.title: Mark the model as Approved for Copilot when ready
recommended.requirement: After preparing and testing the model, mark it 'Approved for Copilot' (formerly 'prepped for AI') in the semantic model settings; this removes the standalone Copilot 'friction treatment' from answers and marks associated reports as approved.

recommended.title: Reduce model complexity and remove bloat
recommended.requirement: Reduce model complexity: more fields, dependencies, and business logic (and patterns like currency conversion or disconnected/field-parameter tables) increase the chance of incorrect Copilot results. A bloated model with unnecessary columns, tables, and measures creates noise that reduces response accuracy; remove unnecessary objects.

recommended.title: Optimize the model for performance / use BPA and Memory Analyzer
recommended.requirement: Optimize the semantic model for performance and run Best Practice Analyzer and Semantic Model Memory Analyzer to find problems such as incorrect data types, unnecessary columns, high-cardinality columns, and inefficient DAX patterns; a well-optimized model yields faster, more accurate AI responses.

recommended.title: Give report visuals descriptive titles (visual metadata is grounding)
recommended.requirement: Ensure visuals (including hidden visuals and pages) on reports connected to the model have descriptive titles; the DAX generation tool grounds responses using visual metadata such as visual title, table, columns, measures, and filters applied.

recommended.title: Add synonyms to tables and columns
recommended.requirement: For every table and column, add the alternative terms users naturally use to refer to it (e.g. 'revenue', 'net sales' for a Sales field), via the Synonyms box in the Model view Properties pane. This is the single most basic, high-impact Q&A optimization because the engine matches questions against field names and their synonyms.

recommended.title: Make the primary synonym unique to avoid ambiguity
recommended.requirement: Do not assign the same synonym to more than one table or column. When a term is genuinely shared, make the primary synonym (the one used in the Q&A restatement) unique so the restatement alerts the user to ambiguity. Detect and flag synonyms that collide across multiple model objects.

recommended.title: Approve high-value suggested synonyms; remove wrong ones
recommended.requirement: In Q&A setup, promote useful suggested terms to approved synonyms (the + icon). Unapproved suggestions are still used at lower priority and rendered with an orange dotted (low-confidence) underline; remove incorrect suggestions (the x) so they are not used even as low-priority synonyms.

recommended.title: Use Teach Q&A to define unrecognized noun synonyms
recommended.requirement: When a user word maps to no field (red double-underline), use Teach Q&A: for nouns Q&A prompts 'refers to' and you fill in the actual field from the data model. Always map to a real field; mapping to anything other than a model field yields undesirable results.

recommended.title: Use Teach Q&A to define adjective filter conditions
recommended.requirement: Define domain adjectives (e.g. 'popular', 'awesome') as a single condition on a column or measure via Teach Q&A's 'that have' prompt. Only one condition can be defined in tooling; for compound conditions create a DAX calculated column or measure first, then define a single condition over it.

recommended.title: Add custom phrasings to define new terms, resolve ambiguity, and improve restatements
recommended.requirement: Add manual phrasings to the linguistic schema for relationships Q&A cannot guess: (1) to define a new descriptive term (e.g. 'ages indicate how old customers are' so 'oldest customers' works), (2) to disambiguate directional relationships (e.g. 'flights are from departure cities' vs 'flights are to arrival cities'), and (3) to make Q&A restatements explicit and unambiguous.

recommended.title: Set Row label to identify the display column of a table
recommended.requirement: For each table, set the Row label property to the column that best identifies a single row (e.g. Display Name for a Customer table) so Q&A and Copilot plot results by that label instead of treating the table generically.

recommended.title: Add or edit verb/preposition phrasings for relationships
recommended.requirement: For relationships that users describe with verbs or prepositions, add the corresponding phrasings (e.g. 'stores sell products', 'cities are in countries/regions') in the Q&A setup Relationships tab or the linguistic schema, so the natural-language engine maps those words to the right fields. Copilot relies on the same linguistic modeling, including verbs for relationships.

recommended.title: Name tables and columns to accurately reflect their content
recommended.requirement: Choose human-readable table and column names that accurately reflect the data they contain, because Q&A assumes names match content and only does basic word-breaking/plural detection. Avoid names like CustomerSummary, StoreInfo, or Product List where Store/Products is meant.

recommended.title: Use human-readable English names; avoid acronyms, abbreviations, punctuation
recommended.requirement: Name tables, columns and measures in human-readable English without excessive acronyms, abbreviations, or punctuation, and distinguish similarly named fields across tables. Inconsistent/abbreviated names make users mis-prompt and confuse Copilot when it resolves field references.

recommended.title: Avoid duplicate field names across tables
recommended.requirement: Do not have fields with the same name across different tables. Distinguish similarly named fields (e.g. 'Name' in Customer vs 'Name' in Store) by renaming or by adding concise descriptions, so the natural-language engine and Copilot pick the intended field.

recommended.title: Add concise descriptions to tables, columns and measures
recommended.requirement: Add descriptive-sentence descriptions to tables, columns, measures and other objects; only the first 200 characters are used by Copilot. Descriptions are part of the natural-language grounding data and help distinguish similarly named fields. Unlike a linguistic schema, all models benefit from descriptions.

recommended.title: List calculation item names in the calculation group description
recommended.requirement: Calculation items are not included in model metadata, so list and explain the calculation item names inside the calculation group column's description (first 200 chars used) so the natural-language engine and Copilot can reference them.

recommended.title: Add missing relationships so tables can be joined
recommended.requirement: Define all required relationships between tables; without them Q&A and Copilot cannot interpret how to join tables (e.g. 'total sales for Seattle customers' fails if Orders-Customers is unrelated). Relationships are the cornerstone of a model that works for natural language.

recommended.title: Denormalize to eliminate inactive relationships Q&A cannot use
recommended.requirement: Q&A can only use active relationships. Where two paths exist between tables (role-playing dimensions, e.g. SourceCityID and DestinationCityID to Cities) one relationship must be inactive and becomes unusable by Q&A; denormalize the needed columns into the fact table so both directions are queryable in natural language.

recommended.title: Normalize so each concept is exactly one model object
recommended.requirement: Apply the rule that every distinct 'thing' users talk about is represented by exactly one table or column. Split multi-column entities into their own tables, pivot away property bags (one column per property), union partitioned tables/pivoted value columns into a single column, split formatted columns (e.g. Full Address into Address/City/Country, Full Name into First/Last), and split multi-value columns into separate rows. Q&A only works when each column has a single meaning.

recommended.title: Follow star-schema design for natural-language consumption
recommended.requirement: Build the model as a star schema with clearly delineated fact tables and descriptive dimension tables. Models that don't follow star-schema best practices produce poor results with any natural-language consumption tool, including Copilot.

recommended.title: Hide or exclude fields that shouldn't answer natural-language questions
recommended.requirement: Hide columns and measures not meant for consumption, or exclude them from Q&A via 'Include in Q&A' in the Q&A setup. Reduce redundant date columns and foreign keys to one canonical field so the correct field is picked. Hidden fields are excluded from Copilot's grounding for the Ask data questions experience.

recommended.title: Mark private/hidden objects to control Copilot grounding
recommended.requirement: Copilot's Ask data questions experience excludes hidden fields and any table marked as private from grounding data, while including the full linguistic schema and descriptions/data types/format strings/data category. Use hide and table-private settings to keep unwanted fields (e.g. a Birthday column competing with the date table) out of natural-language answers.

recommended.title: Set Summarization to Don't summarize for non-additive numeric columns
recommended.requirement: Set the default Summarization to 'Don't summarize' (or Average) for numeric columns that shouldn't be summed - especially Year, Month, Day and ID columns, and others like Age - so questions like 'total sales by year' don't return nonsensical grand totals of years.

recommended.title: Fix incorrect data types (dates/numbers imported as strings)
recommended.requirement: Ensure date and number columns are not imported as strings; Q&A and Copilot won't interpret string-typed dates and numbers as dates and numbers. Apply correct, consistent data types across all tables.

recommended.title: Set a Data Category for each date and geography column
recommended.requirement: Assign the appropriate Data Category (e.g. City, Country/Region, ZIP code, date/time) to date and geography columns. Q&A uses Data Category both to choose visuals (maps for geo, line/play axes for dates) and to bias language interpretation (e.g. 'when' maps to a date, a place name maps to a city). Data category is part of Copilot's grounding properties.

recommended.title: Linguistic metadata feeds Copilot - the model linguistic schema is grounding data
recommended.requirement: Recognize that Copilot's Ask data questions experience grounds on the full model linguistic schema (and DAX query view grounds on its synonyms), so investment in synonyms and phrasings directly improves Copilot answers, not just the legacy Q&A visual. Linguistic modeling is an explicit Copilot model-prep step.

recommended.title: Create Verified Answers for common questions
recommended.requirement: Use Prep data for AI to set up verified answers: attach trigger phrases/questions/keywords to a specific visual so Copilot returns that configured visual when users ask matching natural-language questions. Add multiple phrasings per answer.

recommended.title: Add AI Instructions to encode business vocabulary and synonyms
recommended.requirement: Use AI Instructions (Prep data for AI) to give Copilot business context, domain logic, and natural-language mappings - e.g. which words ('Leads','Deals','Sales Prospects') refer to which table, term synonyms, and which measure to use for a concept - then iteratively test and refine.

recommended.title: Simplify the data schema for AI (remove ambiguous/unused fields)
recommended.requirement: Use Prep data for AI > Simplify the data schema to deselect fields irrelevant to natural-language analysis (unique-identifier relationship keys, sort-order helper columns) and ensure unique field names, reducing the schema Copilot must reason over and removing ambiguity.

recommended.title: Mark the model Approved for Copilot and set item descriptions
recommended.requirement: After preparing the model, mark it 'Approved for Copilot' to signal it is optimized for AI; unapproved models surface warnings to users. Also set rich report and semantic-model descriptions with distinct keywords, since name and description are equally important for Copilot data discovery.

recommended.title: Limit model complexity for reliable natural-language answers
recommended.requirement: Reduce model complexity for natural-language use: remove unused tables/fields/measures, avoid ambiguous relationships, and limit complex patterns such as currency conversion and disconnected field-parameter tables, which cause unexpected or incorrect Copilot/Q&A results when referenced.

recommended.title: Use descriptive table/column/measure names (no Table1, col1, flag)
recommended.requirement: Ensure data sources, tables, and columns use clear, descriptive, business-friendly names that reflect how users naturally refer to the data (e.g. CustomerOrders, customer_email_address, Total Revenue); avoid vague or generic labels like Table1, col1, status, flag, TR_AMT, F_SLS, DIM_GEO_01.

recommended.title: Scope the agent to a single domain / use case
recommended.requirement: Design data agents focused on a specific domain or use case rather than a broad range of questions; specialized agents with targeted instructions, relevant data sources, and domain-specific terminology are more reliable.

recommended.title: Minimize data source scope; limit to 25 or fewer tables
recommended.requirement: Include only the data sources, tables, and columns necessary to answer expected questions; for optimal results limit the number of tables to 25 or fewer per data source.

recommended.title: State what to do, not just what to avoid
recommended.requirement: In instructions, give clear positive guidance on the correct approach rather than only stating what the agent should not do, to avoid ambiguity in edge cases.

recommended.title: Define business terms, abbreviations, and synonyms in instructions
recommended.requirement: Define ambiguous, organization-specific, or domain-specific terms, abbreviations and acronyms (e.g. fiscal vs calendar year, quarter, SKU, NPS, MAU). Put cross-source definitions in agent-level instructions and dataset-specific definitions in data source instructions.

recommended.title: Keep instructions concise, focused, and current
recommended.requirement: Write clear, focused instructions; include only information needed for accurate responses and avoid vague, outdated, overly broad content (e.g. 'all kinds of questions', references to old systems, 'just do your best') that dilutes focus or adds latency.

recommended.title: Write detailed agent instructions: role, behavior, tone, sources, fallback
recommended.requirement: Agent instructions must clearly describe the agent's role, expected behavior, tone/style, intended use cases, preferred/prioritized data sources, when-to-use-which-source routing, and fallback behavior when information is missing. A new reader should be able to tell which source to use and how.

recommended.title: Write detailed data source instructions (purpose, columns, joins, value formats)
recommended.requirement: Data source instructions should be specific, structured, and descriptive: state the data source purpose, which question types it answers, required columns to include in responses, join logic between tables, and typical value formats. Because the agent cannot see row values before querying, include examples of typical values/formats (e.g. State as 'CA' vs 'California').

recommended.title: Provide example queries (few-shot) for complex/representative logic
recommended.requirement: Add example question-query pairs for common or representative question types, especially where logic involves filtering, joins, aggregations, or date handling; keep them well-formatted in the correct syntax (SQL/KQL). Examples need not match user questions verbatim — they demonstrate intent and structure.

recommended.title: Example query quality: Clarity, Relatedness, Mapping all positive
recommended.requirement: Write example-query questions that are clear and unambiguous with necessary metrics, timeframes and filters (avoid vague/multi-intent like 'Show performance'); the query must match the question's intent, metric, filters and granularity; and every literal (number, date, category) in the question must appear in the query. An example is high quality only when Clarity, Relatedness and Mapping are all positive.

recommended.title: Configure an AI data schema (Prep for AI) and mirror its table selection in the agent
recommended.requirement: In Prep for AI > Simplify data schema, select only the tables, columns, and measures relevant to the agent's scope, then select the SAME tables when adding the semantic model to the data agent. Include all dependent objects (e.g. measures/columns a measure references). This reduces ambiguity, improves accuracy, and lowers latency.

recommended.title: Create Verified Answers for common/complex semantic-model questions
recommended.requirement: Create Verified Answers (stored at the semantic-model level) for your most common or easily-misinterpreted questions; the agent checks for an exact or semantically similar match before generating a new response and uses the visual's columns/measures/filters to guide DAX generation. Use complete, robust questions as triggers.

recommended.title: Verified answer config: 5-7 trigger questions, up to 3 filters, no hidden fields
recommended.requirement: Use five to seven trigger questions per verified answer covering formal and conversational phrasings, configure up to three filters for flexible slicing, do not reference hidden columns (verified answers won't work on hidden fields), and re-save the verified answer after renaming any referenced table/column/measure.

recommended.title: Add semantic-model AI instructions in Prep for AI (terminology, metric/date/source preferences)
recommended.requirement: In Prep for AI > Add AI instructions, provide business terminology, metric preferences, time-period definitions, default groupings, and which date field/source to use by default (e.g. define 'top performer', 'use Contribution_Margin not Gross_Profit', 'analyze by fiscal quarter'). Keep instructions clear and specific rather than complex or conflicting.

recommended.title: Use a star schema, not flat/denormalized/pivoted tables
recommended.requirement: Model data as a star schema with clear fact and dimension tables; avoid flat, denormalized, or wide pivoted tables which make DAX less efficient and harder to generate correctly. Unpivot wide tables so each row is a single observation.

recommended.title: Exclude helper/intermediate and duplicate/overlapping measures from the AI schema
recommended.requirement: Include only measures that calculate actual business metrics in the AI data schema; exclude helper/intermediate measures to reduce noise, and consolidate or clearly differentiate duplicate/overlapping measures (e.g. Total Sales vs Sales Amount vs Revenue) excluding duplicates.

recommended.title: Use explicit measures and correct default summarization, not implicit measures
recommended.requirement: Avoid relying on implicit measures (unpredictable results); create explicit DAX measures for calculations users will query and set the correct default summarization (Sum, Average, None, etc.) on numeric columns to prevent unintended aggregations.

recommended.title: Avoid conflicting instructions vs verified answers
recommended.requirement: Do not write AI instructions that contradict Verified Answer configurations; conflicting guidance creates unpredictable behavior.

recommended.title: Optimize the semantic model for performance / remove bloat
recommended.requirement: Optimize the model (efficient DAX, correct data types, drop unnecessary/high-cardinality columns and tables) before relying on the data agent; a bloated model creates noise that reduces accuracy and slow queries hurt the conversational experience. Use BPA and the Memory Analyzer in a Fabric notebook to find issues.

recommended.title: Define agent scope before selecting objects (scope-first workflow)
recommended.requirement: First define the scope of the data agent (the types of questions it should answer), then select only the relevant tables/columns/measures; do not connect an entire lakehouse or model with all objects.

recommended.title: Q&A index hard limit: first 1,000 model entities only
recommended.requirement: The Q&A index (which Copilot data questions and Fabric Data Agent DAX generation rely on) is built only from the first 1,000 model entities (tables + fields). Models exceeding 1,000 tables+columns+measures risk dropped entities and degraded Copilot/Q&A accuracy. Add a CopilotLimits scale rule that counts indexable entities and warns near/over 1,000.

recommended.title: Q&A index hard limit: 5 million unique values
recommended.requirement: The Q&A index stores a maximum of 5 million unique values; exceeding this drops values and reduces Q&A/Copilot accuracy. High-cardinality columns therefore directly degrade Copilot value matching. A scale/cardinality rule should flag models whose indexed unique values approach this ceiling.

recommended.title: Direct Lake / Lakehouse models not supported by Q&A live connect (Desktop)
recommended.requirement: Q&A live-connect supports SSAS, AAS, and Power BI semantic models, but Direct Lake and Lakehouse Power BI semantic models are explicitly NOT supported in Desktop. A readiness check should warn when the model is a live-connect Direct Lake/Lakehouse target that Desktop Q&A tooling cannot index.

recommended.title: Prep-for-AI authoring in Desktop limited to Import/DirectQuery/Composite
recommended.requirement: In Power BI Desktop, Prep data for AI (AI data schema, verified answers, AI instructions) can be authored only for Import, DirectQuery, and Composite (local) connections; all model types can use Prep for AI in the service. The workbench should know Direct Lake Prep-for-AI authoring must happen in the service, not Desktop.

recommended.title: RLS and OLS are enforced/supported by Q&A and Copilot
recommended.requirement: Row-level security is supported across Q&A configurations and object-level security is supported for models hosted in the Power BI service; Copilot only retrieves data the user is permitted to see. OLS-hidden objects are simply not exposed to Copilot, which is correct governance behavior but also reduces what Copilot can answer.

recommended.title: OLS not supported by Q&A for AAS / on-prem SSAS-hosted models
recommended.requirement: Object-level security is NOT supported by Q&A for models hosted in Azure Analysis Services or on-premises SSAS. If the live-connected model relies on OLS in AAS/SSAS, Copilot/Q&A behavior is unsupported and should be flagged.

recommended.title: Hidden/private fields and tables are excluded from Copilot grounding
recommended.requirement: For Copilot data questions, hidden measures/columns, tables marked private, and hidden report pages are excluded from grounding. Conversely, anything you want Copilot to answer about must be visible and non-private. Verified answers referencing hidden columns will not work. Hide noise; surface answerable fields.

recommended.title: Verified answers fail on hidden columns
recommended.requirement: Verified answers will not work if they reference hidden columns in the model. Any verified answer the workbench creates must use visible fields only.

recommended.title: Model bloat (excess tables/columns/measures) degrades accuracy
recommended.requirement: A bloated model with unnecessary columns, tables, and measures creates noise for the DAX generation tool and reduces response accuracy. A readiness/scale rule should detect unused or excessive objects and recommend trimming or scoping via AI data schema.

recommended.title: High-cardinality columns and inefficient DAX flagged as model problems
recommended.requirement: Microsoft explicitly calls out high cardinality columns, incorrect data types, unnecessary columns, and inefficient DAX patterns as problems to fix (via BPA + Memory Analyzer) before exposing a model to a Data Agent, because they hurt performance and answer quality. Supports a CopilotLimits/Scale rule on cardinality and model efficiency.

recommended.title: Duplicate/overlapping measures create ambiguity
recommended.requirement: Multiple measures computing similar metrics (e.g., Total Sales, Sales Amount, Revenue) create ambiguity that misleads DAX generation; consolidate or clearly differentiate and exclude duplicates from the AI data schema. Complements the existing NAME-DUP rule with a measure-semantic-overlap check.

recommended.title: Helper/intermediate measures should be excluded from AI scope
recommended.requirement: Helper and intermediate measures used only for report interactivity add noise; only measures that calculate actual business metrics should be in the AI data schema. Suggests a rule that flags non-business helper measures that are exposed (not hidden / not excluded from AI schema).

recommended.title: Implicit measures and wrong default summarization degrade results
recommended.requirement: Relying on implicit measures leads to unpredictable results; create explicit DAX measures and set correct default summarization (Sum/Average/None) on numeric columns to prevent unintended aggregations. Complements the existing FMT-SUMMARIZE rule with an implicit-measure / summarizeBy check.

recommended.title: Non-star-schema / flat denormalized models hurt DAX generation
recommended.requirement: Flat, denormalized, or pivoted (wide) tables make DAX harder to generate correctly; DAX is optimized for star schema with clear fact/dimension tables. Supports a relationship/structure readiness rule favoring star schema (complements existing fact/dimension delineation guidance).

recommended.title: Ambiguous multiple date columns confuse Copilot
recommended.requirement: Multiple date columns (Order/Ship/Due Date, calendar vs fiscal) without clear guidance confuse the AI; use a marked date table plus Verified Answers and AI instructions to specify the default date. Complements existing DATE-MARK rule with a multiple-active-date-fields ambiguity check.

recommended.title: Non-descriptive names (codes/abbreviations) block correct query generation
recommended.requirement: Object names like TR_AMT, F_SLS, DIM_GEO_01 provide no context; use clear business-friendly names, and if renaming is impossible, supply descriptions and synonyms for context. Reinforces NAME-MEASURE/NAME-COLUMN and SYN-FIELD rules with a code/abbreviation-pattern detector.

recommended.title: Descriptions truncated after first 200 characters for Copilot grounding
recommended.requirement: Only the first 200 characters of a description are used by Copilot/DAX generation (the DAX query view truncates descriptions after 200 characters; the Copilot-report-generation path states only the first 200 characters are used). Front-load meaning in the first 200 chars. Complements the existing DESC-LONG rule with a 200-char effective-length threshold.

recommended.title: Calculation items are absent from metadata; use group description
recommended.requirement: Calculation items aren't included in the model metadata Copilot sees, so the calculation group column's description must list and explain each calculation item (first 200 chars used). Copilot otherwise struggles to use calculation groups. Add a DataAgentConfig/Descriptions rule requiring calc-group columns to enumerate items in their description.

recommended.title: Model complexity (field params, disconnected tables, currency conversion) reduces reliability
recommended.requirement: More complex models (more fields, dependencies, business logic, disconnected tables like field parameters, currency conversion) are more likely to produce wrong Copilot results; if results aren't consistently correct, advise users not to use Copilot on that model. Supports a complexity/scale risk score.

recommended.title: Linguistic modeling / synonyms required for good Copilot results
recommended.requirement: Copilot relies on the same linguistic modeling as Q&A; missing synonyms for fields and verbs for relationships makes Copilot more likely to return unexpected results. Reinforces SYN-FIELD / SYN-SCHEMA and adds relationship-verb phrasing coverage.

recommended.title: Duplicate field names across tables confuse Copilot; disambiguate
recommended.requirement: Fields with the same name across different tables (e.g., Name in Customer and Name in Store) cause Copilot confusion; hide, rename, or add concise descriptions/synonyms to disambiguate. Complements NAME-DUP with a cross-table same-name detector tied to Copilot grounding.

recommended.title: Meet 7 of 8 data-evaluation criteria, including Consistency
recommended.requirement: Microsoft states report quality is good as long as the model meets at least seven of the eight data-evaluation points, including Consistency. This gives a concrete pass threshold the A-F readiness scoring can mirror for the 'evaluate data' criteria.

recommended.title: AI data schema should scope to relevant tables/columns/measures
recommended.requirement: Define an AI data schema (Prep for AI > Simplify data schema) selecting only the tables, columns, and measures relevant to the agent's scope, and select the same tables in the Data Agent; this reduces ambiguity, improves accuracy, and lowers latency. Include dependent objects of selected measures. Core DataAgentConfig rule.

recommended.title: Mark model 'Approved for Copilot' to remove friction treatment
recommended.requirement: After preparing a model for AI, mark it 'Approved for Copilot' (formerly 'prepped for AI') so the standalone Copilot agent removes its friction treatment from answers; admins can restrict standalone Copilot to only approved items. There is no way to approve a report/dashboard/app directly. A governance/visibility readiness signal.

recommended.title: Live-connect from Pro/PPU still needs F64 Copilot workspace
recommended.requirement: You can use Copilot on a semantic model published to a Pro or PPU workspace, but Power BI Desktop must be configured to consume Copilot from an F64 workspace; the F64 requirement is independent of which model you connect to. Important capacity nuance for the consume-via-live-connection path.

recommended.title: Measures must be in a valid (error-free) state
recommended.requirement: A measure only works with Copilot if it is in a valid state with no errors; broken measures are unusable by Copilot. A readiness rule should fail when the model contains measures with calculation errors.

recommended.title: Configure AI data schema in Prep for AI (scope the model to the relevant subset)
recommended.requirement: Define an AI data schema in Power BI 'Prep data for AI' > 'Simplify data schema' that selects only the tables, columns, and measures relevant to the questions the agent should answer. The DAX generation tool used by Fabric data agent uses this schema (not the data-agent-level table selection) when generating DAX. Detect models that have Q&A on but no AI data schema configured, and flag fields that are noisy/ambiguous and should be excluded.

recommended.title: Include dependent objects when scoping the AI data schema
recommended.requirement: When a measure is included in the AI data schema, also include every object it depends on (referenced measures and their underlying columns). A rule should flag a selected measure whose dependency closure (other measures/columns it references) is not fully included in the AI schema, since the DAX tool needs the full dependency chain.

recommended.title: Configure verified answers for common / important questions
recommended.requirement: Set verified answers in Prep for AI for the most common or complex questions, stored at the semantic-model level so they work across reports and any data agent using the model. For data agents, verified answers steer the DAX generation tool toward the correct query structure. A rule can flag a model that is 'approved for Copilot' / Q&A-enabled but has zero verified answers.

recommended.title: Verified answer trigger-phrase count and limits
recommended.requirement: Each verified answer should use roughly five to seven trigger phrases (formal and conversational variants); the platform allows up to 15 trigger prompts per verified answer, a 500-character trigger limit, up to 3 filters at a time (10 filter permutations), and a maximum of 250 verified answers per model. A rule can flag verified answers with too few trigger phrases (e.g. <5) or that exceed these hard limits.

recommended.title: Re-save verified answers after renaming referenced objects
recommended.requirement: If any table, column, or measure referenced by a verified answer is renamed, the verified answer must be updated and saved again for the change to take effect. A rename operation in the change-plan engine should detect verified answers referencing the renamed object and prompt to re-save them.

recommended.title: Add AI instructions in Prep for AI (not at data-agent level for semantic models)
recommended.requirement: Define business terminology, metric preferences, default groupings, and data-source routing as AI instructions in Prep for AI on the semantic model. The DAX generation tool ONLY reads AI instructions configured in Prep for AI; data-agent-level instructions are ignored for semantic-model querying. A rule should flag a Q&A/Copilot-approved model with empty AI instructions and warn against placing model-specific guidance at the data-agent level.

recommended.title: Keep AI instructions focused; avoid conflicting/complex instructions
recommended.requirement: AI instructions are unstructured guidance the LLM interprets with no guarantee of being followed; fewer, focused instructions outperform many broad ones, and instructions that conflict with verified-answer configurations create unpredictable behavior and add latency. A rule (LLM-judgment) can flag overly long, contradictory, or conflicting instruction sets.

recommended.title: Mark the model 'Approved for Copilot'
recommended.requirement: After preparing the model for AI, mark it 'Approved for Copilot' (formerly 'prepped for AI') in the service settings so the standalone Copilot agent removes friction treatment on its answers. A rule can surface whether a Q&A-enabled, prepped model is approved for Copilot.

recommended.title: Use a star schema; avoid flat/denormalized/pivoted tables
recommended.requirement: Data agents generate DAX best against star schemas with clear fact and dimension tables; flat, denormalized, or pivoted (wide) tables make DAX less efficient and harder to generate correctly. A rule can detect wide/pivoted tables or absence of a clear fact/dimension structure and recommend normalizing into a star schema.

recommended.title: Create explicit measures and set correct default summarization; avoid implicit measures
recommended.requirement: Implicit measures produce unpredictable results; create explicit DAX measures for everything users should query and set the correct default summarization (Sum, Average, None, etc.) on numeric columns to prevent unintended aggregations. This generalizes the existing FMT-SUMMARIZE rule (currently only key/relationship columns) to all numeric columns relied on for implicit aggregation.

recommended.title: Disambiguate multiple date fields with verified answers / AI instructions
recommended.requirement: Multiple date columns (Order Date, Ship Date, Due Date, calendar vs fiscal quarter, etc.) without guidance confuse the AI; specify which date field to use by default via verified answers and AI instructions in Prep for AI. A rule can detect multiple ambiguous date columns and flag the absence of disambiguating instructions. Complements the existing DATE-MARK rule.

recommended.title: Add descriptions to objects included in the AI data schema
recommended.requirement: Add descriptions to tables, columns, and measures so the LLM understands the purpose of each object included in the AI data schema. This reinforces the existing DESC-* rules but scopes priority to objects that are actually in the AI data schema (those the DAX tool sees).

recommended.title: Use clear, business-friendly object names (data-agent NL2DAX grounding)
recommended.requirement: The DAX generation tool relies on metadata to interpret questions; use clear, business-friendly names that reflect how users refer to data (e.g. 'Total Revenue' not 'TR_AMT', 'Sales Region' not 'DIM_GEO_01', avoid Table1/col1/flag). If objects can't be renamed, ensure descriptions and synonyms supply the context. Reinforces NAME-MEASURE/NAME-COLUMN with concrete data-agent rationale.

recommended.title: Optimize model performance / remove bloat before AI use
recommended.requirement: A bloated model with unnecessary columns, tables, and measures creates noise for the DAX generation tool and reduces accuracy, while slow DAX hurts the conversational experience. Use BPA and Memory Analyzer to find incorrect data types, unnecessary columns, high-cardinality columns, and inefficient DAX. A rule can flag unused/high-cardinality columns and model bloat as an AI-readiness concern.

recommended.title: Limit data-agent table scope to 25 or fewer tables per source
recommended.requirement: For a data-agent data source, limit the number of selected tables to 25 or fewer; include only the data sources, tables, and columns necessary for the expected questions. This is a NEW data-agent-specific scale ceiling distinct from the existing Copilot LIMIT-SCALE (500 tables). A rule can flag a semantic-model data source whose selected-table count exceeds 25.

recommended.title: Build specialized, narrowly-scoped domain agents
recommended.requirement: Design data agents focused on a specific domain/use case rather than broad general-purpose agents; narrowing focus improves accuracy and reduces ambiguity. A data-agent-config recommendation rule can flag overly broad scope (too many unrelated tables/sources/topics).

recommended.title: Define business terms, abbreviations, and synonyms at the right level
recommended.requirement: Define ambiguous, organization-specific, or domain-specific terms (e.g. 'fiscal year' vs 'calendar year', 'NPS', 'MAU', 'SKU'). Place cross-source definitions in agent-level instructions; place dataset-specific definitions in data-source instructions (for semantic models, in Prep-for-AI AI instructions). Reinforces SYN-FIELD/SYN-SCHEMA with the data-agent rationale.

recommended.title: Evaluate the data agent against ground-truth Q&A pairs with the Python SDK
recommended.requirement: Programmatically evaluate the data agent using the fabric-data-agent-sdk: load a ground-truth dataset of question/expected_answer pairs (DataFrame or CSV), run evaluate_data_agent, and review accuracy via get_evaluation_summary / get_evaluation_details (results: true/false/unclear). Note the SDK evaluates only; it can't modify Prep-for-AI config. Semanticus could add an evaluation harness or readiness gate around a ground-truth set.

recommended.title: Verify and inspect generated DAX during testing
recommended.requirement: Response accuracy depends on the generated DAX; when testing, review the DAX query in each response to verify it is valid and correctly answers the question, then trace incorrect results back to which configuration (model, AI data schema, verified answers, or AI instructions) needs adjustment. Semanticus already has run_dax / verify_dax_equivalence which maps directly to an evaluation/verification workflow.

recommended.title: RLS/OLS data is not fully protected in verified answers (preview caveat)
recommended.requirement: Row-level and object-level security are NOT fully supported as security features for verified answers; normally an RLS/OLS-protected verified answer is not matched/returned, but data could still be exposed (e.g. via the Git file format). During preview, do not rely on verified answers as a security boundary. A rule should warn when verified answers touch RLS/OLS-protected data.

recommended.title: Use Git integration and deployment pipelines for data-agent and Prep-for-AI ALM
recommended.requirement: Manage data-agent configurations and Prep-for-AI tooling edits with Git integration and deployment pipelines across dev/test/prod. Note: verified answers do NOT support Git integration, and after Git/pipeline deployment, Import models must be refreshed to sync LSDL/tooling changes (DirectQuery and Direct Lake models only once per day). A readiness rule can warn about sync requirements after deployment.

recommended.title: Generate / align a Fabric IQ ontology from the semantic model for shared business vocabulary
recommended.requirement: Fabric IQ (the new enterprise intelligence layer) grounds copilots and agents in an ontology of business entities, relationships, properties, and rules. Ontologies can be generated directly from production Power BI semantic models so terminology and KPIs stay consistent across reports, agents, and apps. A model with clean, well-named, well-described entities and relationships bootstraps a better ontology, a NEW readiness dimension (ontology-readiness) beyond classic Copilot prep.

recommended.title: Prepare report visuals with descriptive titles (visual metadata grounds NL2DAX)
recommended.requirement: The DAX generation tool also uses report-visual metadata (visual title, tables, columns, measures, filters). Review all reports on the model including hidden visuals and pages so visuals have descriptive titles, since this metadata grounds agent/Copilot responses. A readiness check could flag visuals with generic/empty titles on models marked AI-ready.

bestPractice.title: Mark grounding-irrelevant tables as private
bestPractice.requirement: Tables marked as private are excluded from Copilot grounding; mark tables private when they should not be used by Copilot's Ask data questions or DAX query experiences.

bestPractice.title: Format strings are used as grounding metadata
bestPractice.requirement: Set appropriate format strings (and format string expressions) on measures and columns; Copilot uses format strings as semantic model grounding metadata when answering questions and generating DAX.

bestPractice.title: Data category is used as grounding metadata
bestPractice.requirement: Set data categories on columns (e.g. geographic categories); data category is one of the semantic model properties Copilot takes as grounding context for answering data questions and generating DAX.

bestPractice.title: Maintain standardized values within columns
bestPractice.requirement: Maintain standardized values within columns (consistent casing/spelling, e.g. 'Open','Closed','Pending' not mixed case) to ensure consistency in filters and reporting.

bestPractice.title: Set a row label for each dimension table
bestPractice.requirement: Set a row label defining which column best identifies a single row in a table (e.g. 'Display Name' for a Customer table) so Q&A plots a more helpful visual when users ask for data 'by' that table.

bestPractice.title: Remove redundant fields (e.g. extra date/FK columns) from Q&A
bestPractice.requirement: Use the Include-in-Q&A setting to omit confusing columns (e.g. numerous date fields or foreign keys) from Q&A, keeping only the one correct column so the right field is picked for natural-language questions.

bestPractice.title: Tag / endorse models that are ready for Copilot
bestPractice.requirement: Consider using tags to label semantic models as ready for Copilot consumption, or use Copilot readiness as a criterion for endorsing the model to Promoted or Certified status, so consumers can identify models that work well with Copilot.

bestPractice.title: Define security roles (RLS) for sensitive data
bestPractice.requirement: Define security roles for different levels of data access if there are sensitive elements not all users should see, using Manage roles in Model view.

bestPractice.title: Generate and curate measure descriptions
bestPractice.requirement: Use Copilot to generate descriptions for model measures, then review and revise them to be accurate, concise, and helpful; all semantic models benefit from adding descriptions to tables, columns, and measures (unlike a linguistic schema).

bestPractice.title: Add Name phrasings for tables of named entities
bestPractice.requirement: For tables containing named objects (product names, customer names, athlete names), ensure a Name phrasing exists so a name value can refer to a whole row (e.g. 'chai' identifies the product row, not just a cell) and enables 'named' questions. Q&A auto-generates these when name columns follow sensible conventions (e.g. 'Name'/'ProductName' not 'PrdNm').

bestPractice.title: Treat the linguistic schema as a first-class, exportable asset
bestPractice.requirement: The linguistic schema (parts of speech, synonyms, phrasings) is editable as .lsdl.yaml: export it, edit grammatical tagging and phrasings, and re-import. To change auto-generated content remove its 'State: Generated' tag; to suppress it set 'State: Deleted' so it is not regenerated. A polished schema can be reused across similar models by importing the .yaml.

bestPractice.title: Set Sort By Column so natural-language sorting is logical
bestPractice.requirement: Configure the Sort By Column property where display order should follow an underlying key rather than alphabetical order (e.g. Hat Size sorted by size number XS-XL, not alphabetically), so 'sort customers by hat size' returns the intuitive order.

bestPractice.title: Use leading words / syntax fragments to nudge query generation
bestPractice.requirement: In data source instructions, include syntax hints or fragments (SQL/DAX/KQL, e.g. LIKE '%...%') to guide the model toward the correct query format for pattern matches, filters, or joins. (Applies to SQL/KQL sources, not semantic models.)

bestPractice.title: Example queries retrieved via vector similarity (top ~3-4)
bestPractice.requirement: Understand that for each user question the agent runs a vector similarity search and injects the top relevant example queries (typically top 3-4) into the augmented prompt; provide diverse, distinct examples so the right ones are retrieved.

bestPractice.title: Add query comments and avoid overlapping/contradicting examples
bestPractice.requirement: Include comments in example queries (e.g. -- substitute customer_id here) to show where to substitute values or apply logic, highlight complex joins/aggregations, ensure questions clearly map to the query, reflect real user behavior, and keep each example distinct and non-conflicting.

bestPractice.title: Validate and de-conflict example queries with the SDK
bestPractice.requirement: Use the Fabric Data Agent SDK evaluate_few_shots to validate each natural-language/query pair against the schema, review pass/fail and Clarity/Relatedness/Mapping scores, and run conflict detection that flags examples with the same intent referencing different tables or computing the same metric with different aggregation/granularity; iterate to resolve. (SQL-based examples only today.)

bestPractice.title: Give report visuals descriptive titles (semantic-model grounding)
bestPractice.requirement: Review reports connected to the semantic model (including hidden visuals/pages) to ensure visuals have descriptive titles; the DAX generation tool also grounds responses on report visual metadata (title, tables, columns, measures, filters).

bestPractice.title: Verify the generated DAX in each test response
bestPractice.requirement: When testing the agent, review the generated DAX query in each response to confirm it is valid and correctly answers the question; if results are wrong, analyze the DAX to determine which configuration (model, AI data schema, verified answers, or AI instructions) to adjust.

bestPractice.title: Evaluate and iterate against ground-truth Q&A pairs
bestPractice.requirement: Continuously validate and iterate; use the Fabric data agent Python SDK to run automated evaluations against ground-truth question-answer pairs and analyze accuracy metrics, and involve stakeholders/end users in evaluation. (The SDK evaluates only; it cannot modify the model's Prep for AI configs.)

bestPractice.title: Use source control / deployment pipelines for agent configs
bestPractice.requirement: Manage data agent configurations across dev/test/prod using Git integration and deployment pipelines so configuration changes are tested and validated before promotion to production.

bestPractice.title: Q&A indexes only text values under 100 characters
bestPractice.requirement: Only text values of fewer than 100 characters are indexed by Q&A. Long text columns (e.g., notes, descriptions stored as data) won't be matchable by Copilot value lookups. Useful context for value-matching readiness, not a blocker.

bestPractice.title: DirectQuery Q&A index refreshes at most once per day
bestPractice.requirement: For DirectQuery sources, the Q&A index rebuilds at most once per day, so value matching can lag source data. Direct Lake Prep-for-AI/LSDL changes likewise sync only once a day. Relevant to freshness expectations for Copilot answers on DQ/Direct Lake models.

bestPractice.title: Prompt limit of 10,000 characters per prompt
bestPractice.requirement: Copilot for Power BI supports up to 10,000 characters per prompt across all surfaces. Not a model-authoring gate, but relevant context for any AI-instruction / verified-answer text the workbench generates.

bestPractice.title: Q&A setup (linguistic teaching) only for Import and DirectQuery
bestPractice.requirement: The Q&A setup feature used to teach terms/phrasings supports only Import mode and DirectQuery (and is only available from Desktop). For other modes you cannot teach the linguistic model, limiting synonym/phrasing remediation that boosts Copilot.

bestPractice.title: Copilot cannot validate RLS in Test-as-role
bestPractice.requirement: Copilot (and Q&A and Quick Insights visuals) cannot be used inside the 'View as / Test as role' RLS validation experience. Relevant for any workbench RLS-validation workflow: it cannot lean on Copilot to verify role filters.

bestPractice.title: Calculation groups degrade Copilot DAX generation
bestPractice.requirement: Copilot can struggle to use calculation groups in suggested queries; to improve usage, include the calculation item names in the calculation group description. Flags presence of calculation groups lacking item-enumerating descriptions as a Copilot risk.

bestPractice.title: Missing values cause Copilot to fabricate / hallucinate
bestPractice.requirement: Inaccurate Copilot summaries are often caused by missing values, which the AI fills in by fabricating data; removing rows with missing values avoids this. A data-quality readiness signal for completeness.

bestPractice.title: Verified answers: 5-7 trigger questions and up to 3 filters
bestPractice.requirement: Configure 5-7 trigger questions per verified answer (formal and conversational phrasings) and up to three filters per verified answer for flexible slicing without duplicating. Re-save verified answers after renaming any referenced table/column/measure. Concrete VerifiedAnswers rule thresholds.

bestPractice.title: AI instructions must avoid conflicting/overly complex guidance
bestPractice.requirement: AI instructions are interpreted (not guaranteed) by the LLM; conflicting instructions (especially against verified answers) cause unpredictable behavior and complex instructions add latency. Keep instructions focused, specific, and non-conflicting. Rule: detect conflicting/overlong AI instructions.

bestPractice.title: Report visuals need descriptive titles for grounding
bestPractice.requirement: The DAX generation tool also uses report visual metadata (visual title, tables, columns, measures, filters); review reports (including hidden visuals/pages) so visuals have descriptive titles to help grounding. Indirect readiness signal tying report visuals to model answerability.

bestPractice.title: Copilot cannot do forecasting, anomaly detection, or key influencers
bestPractice.requirement: Copilot data questions cannot answer questions requiring new insight generation such as forecasting, anomaly detection, or key influencers. Useful to scope what 'readiness' can promise and to set user expectations, not a model-authoring fix.

bestPractice.title: English-only for Copilot data questions
bestPractice.requirement: Copilot data questions support only English; multilingual use of standalone Copilot/Copilot in apps isn't officially supported. Synonyms/names authored in English maximize matching. Relevant to naming/synonym remediation language.

bestPractice.title: Live-connection hides measure DAX and hidden/private objects from Copilot
bestPractice.requirement: When connected by live connection to a shared semantic model, Copilot can't see measure DAX expressions or any hidden/private objects, and must run the DAX query to validate it. This changes what readiness fixes are even visible to Copilot for live-connected models.

bestPractice.title: Comments and quoted strings in DAX are not used by Copilot
bestPractice.requirement: Comments in a measure's DAX and text inside double quotes in the DAX formula are not used by Copilot (e.g., when generating measure descriptions and as grounding). Don't rely on DAX comments to convey business meaning to Copilot; use the Description property instead.

bestPractice.title: Capacity scale-up takes up to 24 hours to enable Copilot
bestPractice.requirement: When you purchase or scale up capacity, it can take up to 24 hours for Copilot to recognize the change and become available. Relevant to readiness-state freshness when capacity tier was just changed.

bestPractice.title: Copilot cannot be scoped to specific workloads/experiences
bestPractice.requirement: You can't enable/disable Copilot for specific workloads or specific Copilot experiences; enabling Copilot in a capacity for a user/group exposes all workloads and Copilot experiences to them. Governance nuance: readiness for one experience implies exposure across all.

gaps.title: Human-readable, business-friendly names for tables, columns, and measures
gaps.notes: NAME-MEASURE, NAME-COLUMN and NAME-TABLE cover the three named object types with the shared structural-crypticness detector. The remaining question, whether an otherwise tidy name is genuinely business-friendly and accurate, is business judgment and is pinned in residualBuckets.judgment rather than guessed by another name heuristic.
gaps.proposedCheck: Implemented as NAME-TABLE. Do not promote the broad row to covered: semantic accuracy and business-friendliness remain a documented judgment non-goal.

gaps.title: Unambiguous, self-explanatory column names (no bare IDs/codes)
gaps.notes: NAME-COLUMN covers structurally cryptic ID/key/code shapes. NAME-COLUMN-ID adds an exact deterministic population for identifier-shaped names and flags only bare Id/Identifier/Key/Code/Number/Num/No, while a compound name such as Product Code is a clean applicable case. Whether any other well-formed name is self-explanatory still requires business judgment, so the row remains partial and is pinned in residualBuckets.judgment.
gaps.proposedCheck: Implemented as deterministic NAME-COLUMN-ID with FixKind.AiContent. The engine identifies the precise defect; the user's assistant authors the replacement name.

gaps.title: Measure names should reflect their calculation and purpose
gaps.notes: NAME-MEASURE flags structurally cryptic measure names ('AvgRating' is caught by the [a-z][A-Z] camelCase boundary). But the requirement is semantic: does the name reflect the DAX calculation and its purpose? A measure named 'Total' or 'Value' or 'Metric 1' passes the regex (no cryptic token) yet fails the requirement. The current rule has no access to, or comparison against, the measure Expression — it is name-shape only. The plan's GroundingBundle carries Expression, so the data is available but unused for this judgment.
gaps.proposedCheck: Add LlmJudgment rule NAME-MEASURE-PURPOSE: cheap pre-filter selects visible measures whose name is generic/short (e.g. in a stopword set {Total, Value, Amount, Metric, Result, Calc} or length < N words) OR fails IsCrypticName, then hand the user's Claude the name + DAX Expression (via get_grounding) to judge whether the name reflects the calculation, and propose a descriptive rename ('Average Customer Rating'). Fix=AiContent via rename_object.

gaps.title: Consolidate or differentiate duplicate / overlapping measures
gaps.notes: No rule detects semantically overlapping measures (Total Sales / Sales Amount / Revenue). NAME-DUP only catches EXACT same-name columns across tables; it does not look at measures at all and does not do near-duplicate/semantic-overlap detection. This requirement is flagged deterministic:true in the catalog but is really a semantic-similarity judgment over measure names + DAX, and it ties directly into the AI data schema (exclude duplicates from the AI schema) — i.e. it belongs to the empty DataAgentConfig category.
gaps.proposedCheck: Add ModelRule DAC-DUP-MEASURE in ReadinessCategory.DataAgentConfig. Deterministic pre-pass: cluster visible measures by normalized-name tokens and/or by structurally-equivalent DAX (verify_dax_equivalence can confirm two measures compute the same thing — strong, deterministic signal). Candidate clusters go to the user's Claude (LlmJudgment) to decide consolidate-vs-differentiate. Fix is twofold: set_description/rename_object to differentiate the keepers, and exclude the redundant ones from the AI data schema (the future set_ai_data_schema MCP tool). Severity High.

gaps.title: Descriptions help disambiguate similarly named fields
gaps.notes: We DETECT the ambiguity (NAME-DUP) and we have generic missing-description rules, but nothing links the two: there is no rule that says 'this field shares a name with another field AND its description does not distinguish it.' A duplicate-named field that already has SOME description passes every DESC-* rule yet still violates this requirement (the description may not disambiguate). The MS guidance is specifically: when you can't rename, ensure the description/synonyms provide the distinguishing context. We have no targeted check or targeted generation for that.
gaps.proposedCheck: Add ModelRule DESC-DISAMBIG: deterministic pre-filter = the NAME-DUP duplicate-name set; for each member, an LlmJudgment verdict on whether its description meaningfully distinguishes it from its same-named siblings (the user's Claude gets the field + table + sibling table names + sibling descriptions via get_grounding). Violates when description is missing OR judged non-distinguishing. Fix=AiContent: set_description (write a disambiguating ≤200-char description) and/or set_synonyms. Category Descriptions, Severity Medium.

gaps.title: Use calculation group column descriptions to list calculation items
gaps.notes: DAC-CALC-GROUP owns an undocumented calculation group. Once a group or its column is documented, DESC-CALCGROUP-ITEMS deterministically checks that every calculation-item name is mentioned as a whole token. The rules are mutually exclusive per group, keep Applicable at the calculation-group population they actually evaluate, and route the wording fix to AiContent.
gaps.proposedCheck: Implemented and pinned by ReadinessCatalogBatch4Tests.

gaps.title: Description grounding is truncated at 200 characters
gaps.notes: DESC-LONG covers visible measures. DESC-LONG-OBJECT covers visible tables and columns, excluding calculation-group objects owned by the dedicated calculation-group rules. Both deterministically enforce the 200-character grounding limit and use AiContent only for the rewrite.
gaps.proposedCheck: Implemented and pinned across measure, table, column, clean and dormant cases by ReadinessCatalogBatch4Tests.

gaps.title: Hide columns and measures not meant for consumption
gaps.notes: VIS-FK is the only Visibility rule: it deterministically flags visible foreign-key (relationship FromColumn) columns and offers a SafeFix (hide). That covers the most common technical-column case (surrogate/FK keys) but is narrow.
