It does NOT cover: visible measures that are helper/intermediate/base measures (the plan's 'Helper/intermediate measure visible' rule, unimplemented), other technical columns that aren't FK endpoints (audit columns, GUIDs, hash/ETL columns, surrogate keys not in a relationship), or the general 'not intended for Copilot' judgment. The requirement spans both columns AND measures; we only address FK columns.
gaps.proposedCheck: VIS-HELPER-MEASURE: visible measure referenced only by other measures (DISCOVER_CALC_DEPENDENCY — a dmv rule) or name matching /^_|_base|_helper|temp|aux/; Fix=SafeFix hide OR (preferred per plan guardrail) exclude from AI data schema. VIS-TECH-COLUMN: visible columns whose name matches technical patterns (GUID, Hash, ETL, _SK, LoadDate, RowVersion) or are high-cardinality keys not used as labels; deterministic pre-filter + optional Claude judgment; Fix=SafeFix hide. Both via set_column_metadata(isHidden=true). Category Visibility.

gaps.title: Hide or rename columns not intended for Copilot to prevent wrong-field answers
gaps.notes: Nothing covers the 'semantically-misleading visible column' case (e.g. a 'Country' on a Customer table, or a 'Birthday' date column, that Copilot would wrongly filter/group on). VIS-FK is purely structural (FK endpoints) and cannot reason about whether a visible business-looking column is actually a trap. This is inherently an AI-content/judgment requirement — it needs the user's Claude to reason about likely user intent vs the column's real meaning — and it is not addressed anywhere.
gaps.proposedCheck: Add LlmJudgment rule VIS-MISLEADING: pre-filter to visible columns whose name collides with a common query dimension (Country/Region/Date/Name/Type) but lives on an off-topic table, then the user's Claude judges whether Copilot is likely to grab the wrong field. Fix=Proposal (review-gated): hide via set_column_metadata(isHidden=true), rename via rename_object, or disable from Q&A field synonyms via set_synonyms. Category Visibility, Severity Medium. Genuinely non-deterministic — Proposal not SafeFix.

gaps.title: Mark grounding-irrelevant tables as private
gaps.notes: No rule reads or sets table 'private' status for Copilot grounding. Our Visibility rules operate on column IsHidden (VIS-FK) only; there is no table-private concept anywhere in the readiness ruleset, and 'private' is a Copilot/Prep-for-AI grounding flag distinct from IsHidden. This is a DataAgentConfig/Prep-for-AI concern and the DataAgentConfig category is empty, so it falls through entirely. Marked best-practice (not just recommended) in the catalog.
gaps.proposedCheck: Add rule DAC-TABLE-PRIVATE in ReadinessCategory.DataAgentConfig. Deterministic candidate detection: tables that look grounding-irrelevant — bridge/factless/parameter tables, disconnected tables, tables that are entirely hidden columns, or date/utility tables with no measures — surfaced as candidates the user's Claude (or human) confirms should be private. Fix=Proposal/AiContent writing the Prep-for-AI 'private' flag via a new set_table_private (or set_ai_data_schema exclusion) MCP tool. Round-trip-verify since the Prep-for-AI store is undocumented (per plan section 8 guardrail).

gaps.title: Verified answers require non-hidden referenced fields
gaps.proposedCheck: Deterministic, but blocked by a real prerequisite: we have no verified-answers store to read. VIS-FK only hides FK columns; it does the OPPOSITE of this gate (it pushes columns hidden, with no awareness of what a verified answer references). Once DataAgentConfig can read the Prep-for-AI/verified-answer blob, this becomes a deterministic predicate: for each verified answer, parse its referenced fields (measures/columns), and flag any whose IsHidden==true. Severity Critical / hard-gate (Microsoft says verified answers silently break). Fix is a Proposal, not a SafeFix: either unhide the referenced field (set_column_hidden=false) or remove it from the verified answer — both are human-review because unhiding has report-wide effects. Cross-check: this is in direct tension with VIS-FK, so the engine must exclude verified-answer-referenced columns from VIS-FK's hide suggestion.
gaps.notes: No coverage. The verified-answers store is unread (no MCP tool: set_verified_answer was planned in ai-readiness-plan.md section H but never implemented — McpTools.cs has no verified-answer tool). This is exactly the kind of rule the empty DataAgentConfig category should own. Until the Prep-for-AI/LSDL verified-answer blob is reverse-engineered and readable, the rule cannot fire at all.

gaps.title: Create explicit measures instead of relying on implicit measures
gaps.proposedCheck: Implemented as DAC-IMPLICIT-MEASURE over visible numeric non-key/non-endpoint columns that auto-aggregate. The finding is Proposal-only because the explicit DAX measure needs reviewed business intent.
gaps.notes: DAC-IMPLICIT-MEASURE owns the additive implicit-measure population; SUMMARIZE-DIMENSION separately owns canonical non-additive numeric columns so it never suggests a nonsensical explicit Total Year measure.

gaps.title: Provide predefined measures and KPIs users commonly request
gaps.proposedCheck: AI-content / Proposal (RuleKind.LlmJudgment), not deterministic — there is no metadata predicate for 'a KPI a user would want is absent.' Detection: an LLM pass over the fact tables' base measures (e.g. a [Sales] measure with a marked date table present but no YTD / MoM / YoY / running-total sibling) proposes the missing common KPIs. Engine can give a deterministic ASSIST: if a date table is marked and a base additive measure exists but no time-intelligence variant references it, surface candidates. Fix = AiContent via create_measure (and we already ship generate_time_intelligence, which is the concrete mechanism). Category Measures. Should be Proposal severity Medium — suggestive, never auto-applied.
gaps.notes: No coverage. We have the enabling tools (generate_time_intelligence, create_measure, DATE-MARK detects the date table) but no readiness rule that detects the ABSENCE of commonly-requested KPIs and proposes them. Inherently non-deterministic per the catalog (deterministic:false).

gaps.title: Standardized, clear calculation logic for measures
gaps.proposedCheck: Strengthen the existing description coverage with an LlmJudgment rule that checks the description actually EXPLAINS the DAX, not merely that a description exists. DESC-MEASURE (exists, AiContent) flags missing descriptions and DESC-ECHO (exists, LlmJudgment) flags name-echo/trivial descriptions, which partially covers this. The gap: a present, non-echo description that still doesn't explain the calculation logic. New rule: for measures with a non-trivial description, LLM judges whether the description accounts for the measure's Expression (filters, ratios, time-intel); the grounding bundle already carries Expression + ExistingDescription (GroundingBundle in Readiness.cs has both). Fix = AiContent via set_description. Category Descriptions.
gaps.notes: Partially covered by DESC-MEASURE + DESC-ECHO: we ensure measures HAVE meaningful-enough descriptions and ground generation on the DAX expression. What's missing is the semantic check that the description's content matches the calculation logic. The fix tool (set_description) and grounding (Expression) already exist.

gaps.title: Exclude helper / intermediate measures from the AI data schema
gaps.proposedCheck: This is THE core missing DataAgentConfig rule. Two-stage detection. (1) Deterministic candidate filter: a measure whose name matches helper patterns (^_, _base, _helper, temp, aux) OR that is referenced ONLY by other measures and never surfaced (needs DISCOVER_CALC_DEPENDENCY — a Dmv rule kind). (2) the rule's real subject is the AI data schema include-set: flag helper/intermediate measures that ARE included in the Prep-for-AI data schema. Fix is deliberately NOT IsHidden (the plan, line 174, says hiding breaks verified answers) — instead EXCLUDE from the AI data schema via a set_ai_data_schema tool that does not yet exist. Category DataAgentConfig. Severity Medium, Fix=Proposal (exclude-from-schema).
gaps.notes: No coverage and no enabling tool. There is no readiness rule for helper measures, no DISCOVER_CALC_DEPENDENCY wiring, and crucially no set_ai_data_schema MCP tool (planned in section H, absent from McpTools.cs). This is the headline content the empty DataAgentConfig category should hold: an AI-data-schema include-set that excludes helpers/intermediates.

gaps.title: Define clear relationships with correct cardinality and active/inactive state
gaps.proposedCheck: Implemented deterministic checks cover bidirectional filtering, many-to-many cardinality and inactive paths. No automatic structural edit is offered.
gaps.notes: The observable relationship states are covered by REL-BIDI, REL-M2M and REL-INACTIVE. The row remains partial because deciding which cardinality or active path is correct depends on business meaning; that remainder is pinned in residualBuckets.judgment.

gaps.title: Use a star schema with clear fact and dimension tables
gaps.proposedCheck: Implemented for observable graph anti-patterns. The semantic star/flat classification is an explicit judgment non-goal, not a queued heuristic rule.
gaps.notes: REL-BIDI, REL-M2M, REL-SNOWFLAKE and REL-DISCONNECTED cover high-confidence structural anti-patterns. Flat/pivoted classification and whether a table is semantically a fact or dimension require business judgment, so the row remains partial and is pinned in residualBuckets.judgment.

gaps.title: Poor star-schema design produces poor Copilot results
gaps.proposedCheck: Implemented for observable graph anti-patterns. Do not add a composite duplicate score or a guessed semantic classifier.
gaps.notes: The engine catches bidirectional paths, many-to-many edges, snowflake/bridge chains and disconnected tables. It does not pretend those signals classify every non-star design; the semantic remainder is pinned in residualBuckets.judgment.

gaps.title: Establish logical hierarchies for drill-down
gaps.proposedCheck: Implemented deterministic checks find obvious date/geography drill candidates with no hierarchy and hierarchies that contain only one level.
gaps.notes: Date/geography hierarchy absence and single-level stubs are observable and covered. Which additional business attributes form a logical drill path is subjective, so the row remains partial and is pinned in residualBuckets.judgment.

gaps.title: Apply correct and consistent data types
gaps.proposedCheck: Implemented and pinned by ReadinessCatalogBatch4Tests.
gaps.notes: FMT-DATATYPE deterministically finds visible, non-key Text columns whose names tightly imply a date, time, amount, quantity, price, cost, total, count, number, rate or percentage. It stays dormant when no such mismatch exists and remains a Proposal because changing a data type can break refresh or downstream DAX.

gaps.title: Format strings are used as grounding metadata
gaps.proposedCheck: Implemented. FMT-MEASURE evaluates visible measures and accepts either a static FormatString or a dynamic FormatStringExpression. FMT-COLUMN evaluates visible business numeric/date columns, excluding keys, relationship endpoints, non-additive identifiers, row-number columns and calculation-group columns. Missing formats are deterministic Proposal findings because the correct unit/precision is a business choice.
gaps.notes: Covered by FMT-MEASURE and FMT-COLUMN. Applicable is the exact visible business measure/column population, so models without relevant objects keep the rule dormant.

gaps.title: Data category is used as grounding metadata
gaps.proposedCheck: Implemented. CAT-GEO covers City, Country, Continent, County, State/Province, Postal Code, Latitude, Longitude, Address and Place. CAT-URL covers WebUrl, ImageUrl and Barcode candidates. Both use exact candidate populations, and the existing metadata writer applies reviewed categories.
gaps.notes: Covered by CAT-GEO and CAT-URL. Boundary-aware Address, Place and Barcode matching avoids business phrases such as Addressable Market, Workplace Satisfaction and Barcode Adoption.

gaps.title: Mark a date table so Copilot filters time correctly
gaps.notes: DATE-MARK (Readiness.cs/ReadinessRules.cs, category Relationships, Severity.High, Deterministic, FixKind.Proposal) fires when no table has DataCategory='Time' (m.Tables.Any(t => t.DataCategory == 'Time')). It detects the ABSENCE of a marked date table and the fix path is the real mark_date_table MCP tool. What is MISSING is the specific failure the MS requirement names: the WRONG date column being used for time intelligence (e.g. a 'Birthday' column in Customer being picked instead of a Calendar table). DATE-MARK is satisfied the moment ANY one table is marked Time, even if relationships route time questions through an unrelated date column. It also lives in Relationships, not Formatting as the catalog assigns it.
gaps.proposedCheck: Add a Proposal/LlmJudgment rule DATE-AMBIG: deterministic pre-filter finds models where >1 table contains a Date/DateTime column AND either no table is marked Time, or the marked Time table is not the one related (via SingleColumnRelationship) to the fact tables, or a non-Time table's date column (e.g. name matches Birthday/DOB/HireDate) is an active relationship endpoint used for filtering. Fix = mark_date_table on the correct calendar table (+ for the genuinely-confusing extra date columns, hand off to the proposed QNA-EXCLUDE / AI-instructions path below). It is non-deterministic at the 'which column is correct' step, so RuleKind.LlmJudgment with mark_date_table as the deterministic apply.

gaps.title: Disambiguate multiple date fields
gaps.notes: DATE-AMBIGUOUS deterministically detects visible business tables with multiple event-date columns, excludes marked date tables and start/end validity ranges, and proposes the existing mark_date_table, set_column_hidden, or set_ai_instructions routes. It stays dormant when no table has an observable date ambiguity.
gaps.proposedCheck: Implemented as DATE-AMBIGUOUS (ModelRule, FixKind.Proposal). Applicable is the exact population of visible non-date-table business tables with two or more observable event-date columns; no such table means Applicable=0.

gaps.title: Maintain standardized values within columns
gaps.notes: Every existing rule operates on METADATA (names, descriptions, format strings, synonyms, relationships, DataCategory, SummarizeBy). None reads column DATA / distinct values. There is no rule that samples a column's values to detect mixed casing/spelling (Open/open/OPEN, Pending vs Pendng). This is a data-quality check, not a metadata check, so it cannot be done offline from TMDL.
gaps.proposedCheck: DMV/live rule DATA-STDVAL (RuleKind.Dmv), live-connection only. Pre-pass runs EVALUATE TOPN over distinct values of low-cardinality visible text columns (via run_dax / preview_table infra that already exists) and flags columns where normalized-equal values appear with inconsistent casing/whitespace (case-insensitive group size > 1) or near-duplicate spellings (edit distance 1). FixKind.Proposal — the real fix is upstream in Power Query/source, not in the semantic model, so the model engine can only surface it; no MCP write tool resolves it. Degrade to N==0 when no live connection (matches the plan's graceful-degrade contract for dmv rules).

gaps.title: Define linguistic relationships (phrasings / verbs)
gaps.notes: SYN-SCHEMA/SYN-FIELD only cover field-level SYNONYMS (Entities/Terms in the LSDL). Nothing in the codebase reads or detects the LSDL 'Relationships'/'Phrasings' section (verbs like 'stores sell products'). SynonymHelper is synonyms-only, and there is no phrasing detection (grep for phrasing/relationship-linguistic returns nothing) and no MCP tool to author phrasings.
gaps.proposedCheck: ModelRule SYN-PHRASING (category Synonyms), RuleKind.LlmGenerate, FixKind.AiContent. Deterministic detect: parse the existing JSON linguistic Content and check whether the Relationships/Phrasings collection is empty or only auto-generated (analogous to how SYN-FIELD checks Terms state). Applicable only when a fact->dimension relationship pair exists to phrase. Fix needs a new LSDL writer + a new MCP tool (e.g. set_phrasings/add_phrasing) extending the SynonymHelper pattern to the phrasings node, with Claude generating verb phrases from table/relationship names. Until that tool exists, generate-ready-to-paste only.

gaps.title: Set a row label for each dimension table
gaps.notes: No rule and no helper touch a table's row label / 'data column that best identifies a row'. Catalog marks this deterministic:true. Nothing in TOMWrapper usage here reads Culture ObjectTranslation / linguistic table-binding row-label, and there is no set_row_label MCP tool. DataAgentConfig and Synonyms categories both lack any per-dimension-table rule.
gaps.proposedCheck: ObjectRule<Table> or ModelRule SYN-ROWLABEL (category Synonyms), RuleKind.Deterministic, FixKind.Proposal (LLM picks the column). Predicate: for each visible non-date dimension table (has a key/PK, is the 'one' side of relationships) check whether a row label is set in the linguistic metadata; flag if unset. Deterministic candidate selection (the single string column that is most name-like: matches Name/Title/Display/Description, high distinct count, not a key) and the fix authors the row-label binding in the LSDL via a new set_row_label MCP tool. The 'which column' choice can be Deterministic-with-heuristic or LlmJudgment for ties.

gaps.title: Remove redundant fields (e.g. extra date/FK columns) from Q&A
gaps.notes: Partially addressed via a DIFFERENT mechanism. VIS-FK (Visibility, Deterministic, FixKind.SafeFix) finds visible foreign-key columns (relationship FromColumn endpoints) and hides them with set_column_hidden — which does remove them from Q&A as a side effect, covering the 'extra FK columns' half. But the requirement is specifically the Include-in-Q&A setting (keep the column visible in reports yet excluded from Q&A), which the engine cannot express: there is no per-object Q&A-exclusion read or write (no include_in_qna MCP tool), and the 'numerous date fields' half is not detected at all (ties to DATE-MULTI above). Our only lever is the blunt IsHidden hammer, which the plan itself warns against (hiding breaks verified answers / all report consumers).
gaps.proposedCheck: ModelRule QNA-EXCLUDE (category Visibility or Synonyms), RuleKind.Deterministic, FixKind.SafeFix. Predicate: visible columns that are relationship endpoints OR redundant date columns OR cryptic codes that are still included in Q&A. Fix = set the per-field Q&A inclusion flag (LSDL/metadata) rather than IsHidden, via a NEW include_in_qna MCP tool, preserving report visibility. This is the MS-preferred lever and the plan explicitly prefers AI-schema exclusion over IsHidden — so this also overlaps the AI-data-schema requirements below.

gaps.title: Enable Power BI Q&A on the model (prerequisite for Prep data for AI)
gaps.notes: DAC-QNA-DISABLED reads definition.pbism settings.qnaEnabled, reports an explicit false value in DataAgentConfig, and stays dormant when that file-backed switch is unavailable. Q&A is a prerequisite for Prep-for-AI, but it is not an overall-grade gate because disabling it does not block every AI consumer; the observed violation already docks the DataAgentConfig category.
gaps.proposedCheck: Implemented as DAC-QNA-DISABLED. It reads definition.pbism settings.qnaEnabled, uses Applicable=1 only when that switch is observable, and reports false without adding a combined-grade gate.

gaps.title: Define an AI data schema (focused subset of fields)
gaps.notes: This is the core of the empty DataAgentConfig category. The enum member exists (Readiness.cs) and carries weight 0.08 (ReadinessAnalyzer.cs), but ReadinessRuleSet.Default() emits ZERO rules for it, so the weight is inert (present = categories.Where(c.HasRules) drops it from the mean). There is no read of any Prep-for-AI include-set and no set_ai_data_schema MCP tool. The plan flags this as Wave-2 (undocumented storage).
gaps.proposedCheck: DataAgentConfig rule DAC-SCHEMA, RuleKind.LlmJudgment (engine computes candidates, LLM adjudicates 'which sales measure'), FixKind.Proposal/AiContent. Detect: no AI-data-schema include-set defined, OR the include-set contains hidden helper/intermediate measures or duplicate-named fields, OR omits core business measures. Engine deterministically proposes business-metric leaves; LLM adjudicates ambiguity. Fix needs a NEW set_ai_data_schema MCP tool (autoCloseDependencies) writing the Prep-for-AI store — reverse-engineered/round-tripped per the plan before claiming applied; otherwise generate-ready-to-paste.

gaps.title: Include dependent objects when defining the AI data schema
gaps.notes: No dependency-closure logic feeds the (non-existent) AI-data-schema. The engine does not call DISCOVER_CALC_DEPENDENCY anywhere for readiness (the plan lists it as Wave-2 live work), so it cannot detect that a Total Revenue measure in the schema references measures/columns that were left out. Catalog marks this deterministic:true.
gaps.proposedCheck: DataAgentConfig rule DAC-SCHEMA-DEPS, RuleKind.Deterministic (Dmv for the dependency walk), FixKind.SafeFix. Predicate: for every object in the AI-data-schema include-set, compute its full dependency closure (measure->measure and measure->column refs via DISCOVER_CALC_DEPENDENCY or static DAX parse) and flag any dependent object NOT in the set. Fix = auto-add the missing dependents (autoCloseDependencies=true on the proposed set_ai_data_schema tool). Purely deterministic once an include-set and dependency source exist; depends on DAC-SCHEMA landing first.

gaps.title: Add AI instructions for business context, terminology, and analysis rules
gaps.notes: DAC-AI-INSTRUCTIONS reads model-level LSDL CustomInstructions through PrepForAiReader and deterministically checks presence. The content fix is handed to the user's assistant through AiContent; the engine does not run inference.
gaps.proposedCheck: Implemented as DAC-AI-INSTRUCTIONS. PrepForAiReader reads LSDL CustomInstructions; the rule is dormant without a readable legacy linguistic schema and stands down for the unparsed new Copilot Tooling Format. AiContent routes drafting to the user's assistant.

gaps.title: AI instructions are limited to 10,000 characters
gaps.notes: DAC-AI-INSTRUCTIONS-LEN deterministically measures readable LSDL CustomInstructions and flags content over 10,000 characters. It stays dormant when no instructions exist and uses AiContent only for the condensation fix.
gaps.proposedCheck: Implemented as DAC-AI-INSTRUCTIONS-LEN with FixKind.AiContent. The rule is not wired as an overall-grade hard gate; that product decision remains separate from deterministic detection.

gaps.title: Create verified answers for common or ambiguous questions
gaps.proposedCheck: Verified answers are NOT modeled at all. The whole DataAgentConfig category has weight 0.08 but zero rules, and there is no MCP tool to read/write verified answers (the docs/ai-readiness-plan.md H-table 'No verified answers' rule and a set_verified_answer tool were planned but never implemented; PLAN.md/README explicitly defer DataAgentConfig to Wave 2 as undocumented storage). Detection: this is a Proposal/AI-content rule, NOT deterministic — a deterministic predicate can only check the *count* of stored verified answers (== 0 -> finding) once the Prep-for-AI/LSDL store is reverse-engineered and round-trip readable. The richer 'are these the RIGHT 5-7 trigger questions' judgment is LlmGenerate. Fix path: a new set_verified_answer MCP tool (planned, not built) writing trigger phrases + a visual spec + <=3 filters into the model-level Prep-for-AI store, with validation that referenced fields are visible and not report-only measures, the user's Claude authoring 5-7 formal+conversational trigger phrases. This is the core of what DataAgentConfig should contain.
gaps.notes: No rule, no category content, no MCP tool, no storage adapter. DataAgentConfig category is an empty 0.08-weight shell. Blocked on reverse-engineering the Prep-for-AI/verified-answers storage (Wave 2, per PLAN.md). This is the single biggest DataAgentConfig gap.

gaps.title: Update verified answers when referenced objects are renamed
gaps.proposedCheck: Deterministic, but unbuildable today because (a) there is no verified-answers store to read and (b) rename_object (McpTools.cs) auto-rewrites DAX references but does NOT touch verified-answer definitions, and nothing records that a referenced table/column/measure was renamed after a VA was saved. Once VAs are readable, this is a deterministic predicate: for each verified answer, resolve every table/column/measure name it references against the live model; if any referenced name no longer exists -> finding (broken/stale VA). Fix path: re-bind and re-save the verified answer via the same set_verified_answer tool (FixKind.Proposal — needs human confirm of the intended target since a rename may be ambiguous). Could be tightened by having rename_object emit a 'VA may be stale' flag for any VA referencing the old name.
gaps.notes: Deterministic in principle (requirement.deterministic=true) but doubly blocked: no VA store and rename_object has no VA-awareness. Belongs in DataAgentConfig. Same Wave-2 storage dependency as VA-NONE.

gaps.title: Mark the model as Approved for Copilot when ready
gaps.proposedCheck: Not modeled. 'Approved for Copilot' (formerly 'prepped for AI') is a service-side/tenant flag, NOT a TOM property — ai-readiness-plan.md section 5 explicitly notes it 'must be confirmed at runtime, not assumed as TOM annotations'. Detection: deterministic boolean once a capability probe can read the flag (via Fabric REST / model metadata), predicate = approvedForCopilot == false -> finding. Because Semanticus drives a live model over a local/XMLA connection it may not always see the service flag, so degrade to N==0 (not-evaluated) when unreadable, exactly like the plan's DMV-degradation pattern. Fix path: FixKind.Proposal — Semanticus cannot set a service-side flag through TOM; surface an actionable instruction ('mark Approved for Copilot in model settings') plus, if a Fabric REST adapter exists, a one-click call. This is a DataAgentConfig rule and arguably the capstone gate of the whole readiness flow.
gaps.notes: Belongs in DataAgentConfig. Deterministic check but no readable/writable surface today; needs a Fabric REST/service capability adapter. No code, no MCP tool. Should be the final 'you are ready' signal of the scorecard.

gaps.title: Tag / endorse models that are ready for Copilot
gaps.proposedCheck: Not modeled. Endorsement (Promoted/Certified) and tags are workspace/governance metadata living in the Fabric service, invisible to TOM. Detection: not a model-metadata deterministic predicate — it is a best-practice advisory (requirement.strength=best-practice, deterministic=false). Realistic check: once the model's own grade is computed, if grade>=B (or A) AND the model is not endorsed (read via Fabric REST), emit an advisory 'this model qualifies for Promoted/Certified — consider endorsing'. Fix path: FixKind.None/Proposal (advisory) — Semanticus surfaces the recommendation; it cannot endorse through the modeling connection. DataAgentConfig category, advisory severity (Info).
gaps.notes: Best-practice advisory, governance-layer, no TOM surface. Lowest-priority DataAgentConfig item; gate the suggestion on the model's own readiness grade. No code today.

gaps.title: Reduce model complexity and remove bloat
gaps.proposedCheck: Implemented deterministic signals cover hard scale ceilings, live high-cardinality text columns, disconnected business tables, field-parameter complexity and duplicate measures. The row stays partial because deciding that an object is unnecessary or that a currency-conversion design is too complex requires usage evidence and business judgment.
gaps.notes: Batch 6 reconciled the observable complexity and bloat signals. Full semantic unused-object classification remains a judgment non-goal; deletion is never inferred from counts alone.

gaps.title: Optimize the model for performance / use BPA and Memory Analyzer
gaps.ourRuleId: FMT-SUMMARIZE, SCALE-HICARD-COLUMN, BP-DAX-IFERROR, BP-DAX-SUMMARIZE-EXT, BP-DAX-BARE-TABLE-FILTER, BP-DAX-VAR-ALIAS
gaps.proposedCheck: The readiness scorecard now surfaces incorrect summarization, live high-cardinality text and four deterministic inefficient-DAX patterns. Semanticus also ships the separate BPA and live VPAX/performance tools. The row remains partial because running Microsoft's external Memory Analyzer and deciding which objects to remove are workflow and judgment concerns, not model-state predicates.
gaps.notes: Batch 6 records the deterministic readiness findings without pretending that use of an external analysis product is a scored model property. The external-tool workflow is an out-of-band non-goal.

gaps.title: Define security roles (RLS) for sensitive data
gaps.proposedCheck: Not modeled in readiness. There is NO Security category in ReadinessCategory (Readiness.cs enum), and no readiness rule touches roles — though model.Roles IS queryable via TOM (Bpa.cs line 189 maps 'ModelRole' to model.Roles, so the data is reachable). Detection is best-effort, not strictly deterministic (requirement.deterministic=false): a predicate like model.Roles.Count == 0 -> advisory finding is deterministic to compute but can't know whether the model actually CONTAINS sensitive data, so it should be a low-severity advisory/Proposal ('no RLS roles defined — if any data is sensitive, add roles in Manage roles'). A stronger AI-content variant could scan column names/descriptions for PII-like signals (SSN, salary, email) and raise severity. Fix path: FixKind.Proposal — Semanticus has no create-role / set-RLS MCP tool today, so this is advisory + ready-to-paste guidance, not auto-applied. Needs a new Security category (weight rebalanced from the 8 current categories) or fold into Visibility.
gaps.notes: No Security category, no role rule, no RLS MCP tool — but model.Roles is already TOM-reachable so detection is feasible. Advisory only (can't infer sensitivity). Requires adding a Security category to the enum + weights.

gaps.title: Give report visuals descriptive titles (visual metadata is grounding)
gaps.proposedCheck: Not modeled and architecturally hardest: visual titles live in the REPORT layer (PBIR/.pbix report.json), NOT in the semantic model / TOM that Semanticus drives. ai-readiness-plan.md repeatedly warns report-layer bindings 'can't be validated from TOM'. Detection: deterministic IF a report definition is reachable (PBIP folder / Fabric REST) — parse each visual, flag visuals/pages where title is empty or auto-generated/generic -> finding. Without report access it degrades to N==0 (not-evaluated). The 'is this title descriptive' judgment is LlmJudgment. Fix path: FixKind.Proposal/AiContent — would need a report-layer writer Semanticus does not have; realistically generate-ready-to-paste descriptive titles. Belongs in DataAgentConfig (it grounds the data-agent DAX tool), but flag as cross-layer scope expansion beyond the model.
gaps.notes: Out of current scope: report layer, not model/TOM. No reader, no writer, no rule. DataAgentConfig-adjacent. Lowest feasibility without a PBIR/report adapter; should be explicitly scoped as report-layer work.

gaps.title: Add synonyms to tables and columns
gaps.proposedCheck: Implemented as SYN-FIELD plus SYN-TABLE. Both read JSON LSDL through SynonymHelper, stay dormant below compatibility level 1465 or without a readable schema, and route authored terms through set_synonyms.
gaps.notes: SYN-FIELD covers visible columns and measures; SYN-TABLE covers visible business tables and excludes calculation groups. Both stay dormant without readable JSON LSDL or at compatibility levels below 1465, and both hand the AiContent fix to the user's assistant through set_synonyms.

gaps.title: Make the primary synonym unique to avoid ambiguity
gaps.proposedCheck: Implemented. SYN-COLLIDE parses JSON LSDL terms and compares only the highest-weight user-authored primary synonym for each visible table, column and measure in each culture. It excludes Generated, Suggested and Deleted terms plus lower-weight alternatives, then reports every object sharing a primary term.
gaps.notes: Covered by SYN-COLLIDE with a deterministic Proposal fix. The State/Weight-aware population avoids the false positives produced by grouping every generated thesaurus term.

gaps.title: Approve high-value suggested synonyms; remove wrong ones
gaps.proposedCheck: Not modeled. SYN-FIELD treats any synonym string as present and never inspects term STATE — but the LSDL distinguishes authored/approved terms from low-confidence suggestions (the donor SynonymHelper writes Terms with State, and ai-readiness-plan.md notes authored terms must omit State:Generated). Detection (deterministic): parse the culture LSDL JSON and, per field, flag terms whose State is Generated/suggested (the orange-dotted low-confidence terms) so they can be promoted (+) or removed (x) -> finding listing the unapproved terms. SYN-FIELD's use of the comma-joined GetSynonyms string is too coarse for this; needs term-level State parsing. Fix path: FixKind.Proposal — the user's Claude judges which suggestions to approve vs drop, applied by an extended set_synonyms that can set/clear term State (current set_synonyms just joins terms and calls SetSynonyms, so it would need a State-aware overload). Offline-buildable once term-State is parsed.
gaps.notes: Missing: no term-State awareness anywhere (SYN-FIELD reads only the joined string; set_synonyms writes without exposing State). Deterministic detection but needs LSDL term-level parsing + a State-aware set_synonyms variant. Synonyms category.

gaps.title: Use Teach Q&A to define unrecognized noun synonyms
gaps.notes: We have SYN-FIELD (visible field has no synonyms) and SYN-SCHEMA (no linguistic schema), both AiContent via SynonymHelper.SetSynonyms -> set_synonyms MCP. That covers the *forward* direction: enrich a known field with alternate terms. The MS requirement is the *reverse*: a user word that maps to NO field (red double-underline) must be mapped to a real model field via the 'refers to' prompt. We never model the gap from the question side -- we have no corpus of unrecognized terms, and donor SynonymHelper only writes Entities/Binding/Terms onto an existing object, which is in fact the correct storage for a 'refers to' noun mapping. So the storage/fix tool exists but no rule detects the unrecognized-noun case, and there is no guard that synonyms map to real fields (MS warns mapping to a non-field is harmful).
gaps.proposedCheck: AI-content rule. Deterministic part: cannot fully detect offline (needs the user's question log / red-underline events), so detection is best driven from a sampled question set or surfaced reactively when run_dax / Q&A returns 'no field'. Practical offline proxy: flag visible business nouns (table/Name-column values) with zero synonym Terms AND no Name phrasing as 'likely to be unrecognized'. Fix: the user's Claude proposes synonym Terms; engine calls set_synonyms which (correctly) writes a Term bound to a real field via SynonymHelper. Add a deterministic validator that the target objRef resolves to an actual model field before writing (reject free-text targets).

gaps.title: Use Teach Q&A to define adjective filter conditions
gaps.notes: No rule, no storage path. SynonymHelper only writes synonym Terms (Entities/Binding/Terms); it does not author the LSDL 'that have' adjective condition (a phrasing that ties an adjective to a single column/measure condition). enable_qna seeds a minimal LSDL but no code reads or writes adjective phrasings. The compound-condition guidance (build a DAX calc column/measure first, then one condition) maps to our change-plan / set_dax engine but nothing connects them.
gaps.proposedCheck: AI-content + Proposal. Not deterministically detectable from metadata alone (it is about missing domain adjectives), so detection is advisory/AI-judgment: the user's Claude proposes domain adjectives for a table and the single column/measure condition each maps to. Fix path requires a NEW LSDL writer beyond SynonymHelper -- an 'add_phrasing(adjective, condition)' capability writing an adjective phrasing into Culture.LinguisticMetadata. For compound conditions, chain: change-plan creates a calc column/measure (set_dax), then add the single-condition phrasing over it. This belongs in an extended linguistic-writer, not the current synonym-only path.

gaps.title: Add custom phrasings to define new terms, resolve ambiguity, and improve restatements
gaps.notes: Completely uncovered. Our Synonyms category only models synonym Terms; there is no concept of phrasings (descriptive-term phrasings, directional disambiguation like 'flights are from departure cities' vs 'to arrival cities', or restatement quality). SynonymHelper has no phrasing API; EnsureLinguisticCulture seeds only a skeleton LSDL. No rule, no detector, no writer.
gaps.proposedCheck: AI-content (generation) -- not deterministic. Detection is AI-judgment: flag relationships/fields where a single field name participates in multiple roles (e.g. a Date table joined to two date FKs, or two relationships between the same pair of tables) as 'needs directional phrasing'; that subset IS deterministically detectable (count relationships per table-pair / inactive relationships / role-playing dimensions). Fix: a new linguistic-writer authors phrasings into Culture.LinguisticMetadata.Content with State=Authored (omit State:Generated per MS). Requires extending beyond SynonymHelper to emit Phrasings nodes in the LSDL JSON. No existing MCP tool writes phrasings -- needs a new add_phrasing tool.

gaps.title: Add Name phrasings for tables of named entities
gaps.notes: Not covered. We never detect that a table has a name/identity column eligible for a Name phrasing, and SynonymHelper cannot write Name phrasings (it only writes Terms). The requirement (a name value identifies a whole row; Q&A auto-generates when columns are well-named like 'Name'/'ProductName' not 'PrdNm') is highly deterministic to *detect* but we have no rule and no writer.
gaps.proposedCheck: Mostly deterministic detection + Proposal/SafeFix. Predicate: a table with a clearly-named identity column (Name / *Name / Title / Description where it is the row label) that has NO Name phrasing in the LSDL. Cross-checks our existing IsCrypticName regex (a 'PrdNm'-style name column is also a NAME-COLUMN violation, so the two interact). Fix: a new linguistic-writer adds a Name phrasing binding the identity column to the table entity; this is essentially deterministic once the identity column is chosen (LLM only adjudicates which column is the row identifier when ambiguous). Needs a new add_name_phrasing capability + the Row-label property (see next item) as input.

gaps.title: Set Row label to identify the display column of a table
gaps.notes: Not covered, and this one is explicitly deterministic in the catalog. There is no reference to Row label / DefaultLabel anywhere in the readiness ruleset or engine. TOM exposes it as Table.DefaultDetailRowsDefinition is unrelated; the relevant property is the linguistic/Power BI 'row label' (DataColumn.IsDefaultLabel / Table default label). We set DataCategory and SummarizeBy on columns but never the table's row-label column.
gaps.proposedCheck: Deterministic predicate + SafeFix. Predicate: for each non-hidden fact-or-dim table with a clear identity column, the table has no row-label column set (no column flagged as the default label). Fix: deterministically set the identity column as the table's row label via a new set_row_label / extend set_column_metadata MCP tool writing the IsDefaultLabel flag (the catalog marks this deterministic:true). LLM only needed to pick the identity column when multiple candidates tie. This is the cleanest new deterministic SafeFix to add and directly feeds the Name-phrasing rule above.

gaps.title: Add or edit verb/preposition phrasings for relationships
gaps.notes: Uncovered on the linguistic side. We have REL-BIDI and DATE-MARK in the Relationships *category*, but those are about cross-filter direction and date marking -- not about teaching Q&A/Copilot the verbs/prepositions for a relationship ('stores sell products', 'cities are in regions'). SynonymHelper writes Terms only, not relationship phrasings. So every active relationship is a candidate and we emit nothing.
gaps.proposedCheck: AI-content (generation). Detection is deterministic for the population: enumerate active SingleColumnRelationships whose LSDL has no verb/preposition phrasing. Fix: the user's Claude proposes verb/preposition phrasings per relationship (grounded on From/To table+column names); a NEW linguistic-writer authors a relationship phrasing into Culture.LinguisticMetadata (State=Authored). This is the same missing 'add_phrasing' infrastructure as the custom-phrasings and Name-phrasing items -- one writer serves all three. Note dual benefit: serves both Q&A and Copilot (shared linguistic model).

gaps.title: Treat the linguistic schema as a first-class, exportable asset
gaps.notes: Partial. SYN-SCHEMA accepts either JSON or legacy XML as a real schema, and SYN-LSDL-XML now surfaces every XML culture with a fail-loud upgrade/export proposal instead of falsely reporting the schema missing. JSON LSDL remains managed through enable_qna and set_synonyms. The unpublished new Copilot Tooling Format plus non-synonym Generated/Deleted lifecycle and reusable import/export remain in residualBuckets.deferred-schema.
gaps.proposedCheck: SYN-LSDL-XML is implemented as deterministic detection with Applicable equal to the XML-culture population. It deliberately does not rewrite legacy XML or guess the unpublished new tooling schema.

gaps.title: Name tables and columns to accurately reflect their content
gaps.notes: Partially covered. NAME-MEASURE and NAME-COLUMN (LlmJudgment, AiContent, fixed by rename_object) catch cryptic/non-human names via the Cryptic regex (underscores, camelCase, ALL-CAPS acronyms, ID/Key/Cd suffixes, tbl/dim/fact prefixes). That catches 'PrdNm' but NOT the MS case of a *readable-but-misleading* name like 'CustomerSummary', 'StoreInfo', or 'Product List' meaning Store/Products -- those pass IsCrypticName (no underscore/camel/acronym) yet violate the 'name must reflect content' rule. We have no content-vs-name semantic check.
gaps.proposedCheck: AI-content / LLM-judgment (not deterministic -- it needs the data to know the name lies). Detection: LLM-judgment rule that compares the name against a live data sample (EVALUATE TOPN distinct values + rowcount via the live connection) and against the table's columns/measures to decide if the name accurately reflects content. Optional cheap deterministic pre-filter: flag suffix words like 'Summary/Info/List/Detail/Data' on table names as candidates. Fix: rename_object (review-gated), same path as NAME-COLUMN.

gaps.title: List calculation item names in the calculation group description
gaps.notes: DESC-CALCGROUP-ITEMS deterministically checks that every calculation-item name appears as a whole token in the group or group-column description. It takes over only after documentation exists, so it is mutually exclusive with DAC-CALC-GROUP.
gaps.proposedCheck: Implemented with AiContent for the description rewrite and exact calculation-group applicability.

gaps.title: Add missing relationships so tables can be joined
gaps.notes: REL-DISCONNECTED deterministically evaluates visible business tables once at least two are present, excludes calculation groups, field parameters and measure-only holders, and flags each table with no relationship. A valid single-table model stays dormant. The structural fix remains a reviewed Proposal because selecting the key pair requires author intent.
gaps.proposedCheck: Implemented as REL-DISCONNECTED (ModelRule, FixKind.Proposal). Applicable is the exact visible business-table population when a join is relevant; a single-table model uses Applicable=0.

gaps.title: Denormalize to eliminate inactive relationships Q&A cannot use
gaps.notes: REL-INACTIVE deterministically evaluates exactly the inactive relationship population and explains that Q&A and Copilot cannot traverse it. Active-only models stay dormant.
gaps.proposedCheck: Implemented as a Proposal because denormalizing or otherwise exposing the role is structural and requires reviewed author intent.

gaps.title: Normalize so each concept is exactly one model object
gaps.notes: We have nothing that detects un-normalized columns (Full Address, Full Name, property-bag/wide columns, multi-value columns, partitioned/pivoted value columns). NAME-DUP detects the inverse problem (one name on many tables) but not 'one column carrying many concepts'. This is inherently fuzzy and the requirement itself is marked deterministic:false.
gaps.proposedCheck: AI-content rule (RuleKind.LlmJudgment, FixKind.Proposal). A cheap deterministic pre-filter selects candidate columns: name regex hits like 'full name|full address|address line|name$ with comma/space-rich string data', string columns whose sampled distinct values contain delimiters (comma/semicolon/pipe) via the live run_dax/preview_table sample, or very wide tables with one-column-per-property. The user's Claude judges whether the column carries multiple concepts and drafts a split plan. No deterministic auto-fix and no MCP tool today (split-column is a Power Query / structural change outside TOM's safe surface) — emit as Proposal only. Severity Medium. Category Relationships.

gaps.title: Follow star-schema design for natural-language consumption
gaps.notes: REL-BIDI, REL-M2M, REL-SNOWFLAKE and REL-DISCONNECTED cover the observable relationship-graph anti-patterns. Full fact/dimension delineation and flat/pivoted interpretation remain business judgment and are pinned in residualBuckets.judgment.
gaps.proposedCheck: Implemented for observable graph anti-patterns. Full star adjudication remains a judgment non-goal because the model does not declare semantic fact/dimension intent.

gaps.title: Hide or exclude fields that shouldn't answer natural-language questions
gaps.notes: VIS-FK is our only Visibility rule and it covers exactly one slice: foreign-key columns (relationship FromColumn) that are still visible, with a deterministic SafeFix to hide them (set_column_hidden). It does NOT cover the broader requirement: hiding helper/intermediate measures, technical/system columns, GUIDs, or reducing redundant date columns and duplicate foreign keys to one canonical field. 'Exclude from Q&A' (a separate include-in-Q&A flag distinct from IsHidden) is not represented at all.
gaps.proposedCheck: Add VIS-REDUNDANT-KEY (deterministic, SafeFix via set_column_hidden): when several columns are surrogate/foreign keys pointing into the same dimension or several visible date columns exist, keep one canonical and flag the rest. Add VIS-HELPER-MEASURE (LlmJudgment/Deterministic): a visible measure referenced ONLY by other measures (via DISCOVER_CALC_DEPENDENCY over the live connection) or matching /_base|_helper|^_|tmp|aux/ — fix is hide (set_column_hidden has no measure analog yet; needs a set_measure_hidden tool) or prefer exclude-from-AI. Distinguish hide vs Q&A-exclude. Severity Medium. Category Visibility.

gaps.title: Mark private/hidden objects to control Copilot grounding
gaps.notes: Deterministic by spec and only partially met. VIS-FK handles hiding FK columns (a subset). We have no notion of a 'private table' marking, and no rule that flags fields competing with the canonical grounding (e.g. a Birthday column competing with the date table). Our model has IsHidden plumbing (set_column_hidden) but not table-private or measure-hidden, and no rule that reasons about which visible field should be suppressed to protect Copilot grounding.
gaps.proposedCheck: Deterministic VIS-PRIVATE-TABLE: flag utility/staging tables (no relationships AND/OR name pattern stg_/tmp_/param) that are visible, SafeFix = set table IsHidden (needs a set_table_hidden MCP tool — currently only set_column_hidden exists). Plus VIS-COMPETING-FIELD (LlmJudgment): when a non-date table has a date/time column outside the marked date table competing for 'when' questions, propose hiding it. Detection deterministic for private-table; competing-field is AI-content. Fix path: set_column_hidden / a new set_table_hidden. Severity Medium. Category Visibility.

gaps.title: Set Summarization to Don't summarize for non-additive numeric columns
gaps.notes: FMT-SUMMARIZE owns keys and relationship endpoints. SUMMARIZE-DIMENSION owns visible numeric columns whose canonical metadata makes non-additivity deterministic: year/month/quarter/week/day, age, rank/order/sort/sequence, latitude/longitude, PIN/postal/ZIP and identifier suffixes. Both safely set SummarizeBy=None and keep their populations disjoint from DAC-IMPLICIT-MEASURE. The row remains partial because arbitrary business columns can be non-additive without exposing that semantic in model metadata.
gaps.proposedCheck: The deterministic metadata slice is implemented. Full semantic classification needs business judgment, so the catalog does not promote this row to covered.

gaps.title: Fix incorrect data types (dates/numbers imported as strings)
gaps.notes: FMT-DATATYPE checks the metadata mismatch directly: a visible, non-key column is stored as Text while its name tightly implies a date or numeric value. The rule is deterministic and proposal-only; the engine does not silently change a refresh-sensitive type.
gaps.proposedCheck: Implemented and pinned by ReadinessCatalogBatch4Tests.

gaps.title: Set a Data Category for each date and geography column
gaps.notes: CAT-GEO deterministically covers City, Country, Continent, County, State/Province, Postal Code, Latitude, Longitude, Address and Place; CAT-URL separately covers URL, image and barcode candidates. The row remains partial because Power BI has no general Date data-category value and deciding the intended temporal role of an arbitrary date column is semantic judgment.
gaps.proposedCheck: The observable geography slice is implemented with an exact candidate population and safe metadata fix. The residual date-role claim is a judgment non-goal rather than a reason to invent a CAT-DATE value or add an always-pass rule.

gaps.title: Set Sort By Column so natural-language sorting is logical
gaps.notes: FMT-SORTBY deterministically evaluates visible text month/weekday labels and text labels with exactly one matching numeric order companion on the same table, such as Month Name + Month Number or Priority + Priority Sort. It remains a Proposal so an author confirms the intended business order; ambiguous companions do not fire.
gaps.proposedCheck: Implemented as a cross-object ModelRule and pinned across violation, clean and dormant cases by ReadinessCatalogBatch4Tests.

gaps.title: Linguistic metadata feeds Copilot - the model linguistic schema is grounding data
gaps.proposedRuleId: (covered by Synonyms category; DataAgentConfig should NOT duplicate it)
gaps.notes: SYN-SCHEMA detects an absent linguistic schema; SYN-FIELD and SYN-TABLE measure synonym coverage across visible fields and business tables. This grounding belongs in the Synonyms category rather than being duplicated in DataAgentConfig.
gaps.proposedCheck: Implemented by SYN-SCHEMA, SYN-FIELD and SYN-TABLE in the Synonyms category. DataAgentConfig remains reserved for Prep-for-AI artifacts rather than duplicating linguistic scoring.

gaps.title: Create Verified Answers for common questions
gaps.notes: Completely missing, and this is the heart of what the empty DataAgentConfig category SHOULD contain. DataAgentConfig carries weight 0.08 in ReadinessAnalyzer but has ZERO rules tagged ReadinessCategory.DataAgentConfig, so HasRules is always false and the category silently drops out of the weighted mean — the 0.08 weight is dead. There is no verified-answers store, no AI-instructions store, and no MCP tool (set_verified_answer / set_ai_instructions / set_ai_data_schema) in the current surface (McpTools.cs ends at set_synonyms/generate_*/plan tools).
gaps.proposedCheck: This is the concrete fill for DataAgentConfig — three Prep-for-AI rules: (1) DAC-VERIFIED-ANSWERS: model-level, flag when the verified-answers store is empty/0; fix is AI-content (the user's Claude expands top questions into trigger phrases + a visual spec, caps ~250/model, 5-7 triggers, <=500 chars) via a NEW set_verified_answer MCP tool. (2) DAC-AI-INSTRUCTIONS: flag when the Prep-for-AI instructions block is empty (<=10k cap); AI-content fix via a new set_ai_instructions tool, appended into a delimited Semanticus-managed section. (3) DAC-AI-DATA-SCHEMA: flag when the AI include-set is unset or includes helpers/duplicates; deterministic include-closure computed by the engine, AI adjudicates 'which sales measure', via a new set_ai_data_schema tool. All three need the undocumented Prep-for-AI storage reverse-engineered + round-trip-verified before write (per the plan, Open Decision 1), so ship read+generate-ready-to-paste first. Detection is deterministic (store empty?), content is AI-generated, structural risk = Proposal. These three rules are exactly what makes the 0.08 DataAgentConfig weight live. Severity Medium/High. Category DataAgentConfig / VerifiedAnswers.

gaps.title: Add AI Instructions to encode business vocabulary and synonyms
gaps.notes: PrepForAiReader reads model-level LSDL CustomInstructions. DAC-AI-INSTRUCTIONS deterministically detects an empty block, while DAC-GLOSSARY-GAP detects visible field-name codes not defined in the instructions. Both use FixKind.AiContent: the engine does not infer content; the user's assistant drafts it and set_ai_instructions validates and writes it.
gaps.proposedCheck: Implemented using readable LSDL CustomInstructions. DAC-AI-INSTRUCTIONS checks presence; DAC-GLOSSARY-GAP checks undefined field-name codes; set_ai_instructions is the validated writer and AiContent keeps authorship outside the engine.

gaps.title: Simplify the data schema for AI (remove ambiguous/unused fields)
gaps.notes: VIS-FK hides foreign-key/relationship-key columns (deterministic SafeFix via set_column_hidden), and NAME-DUP flags ambiguous duplicate field names across tables — both directly serve 'remove relationship keys / ensure unique names'. But we DO NOT detect sort-order helper columns, unused/never-referenced columns, or model an explicit 'AI data schema include-set' (the Prep-for-AI 'Simplify the data schema' deselect surface). MS's mechanism is deselecting from the AI schema, which we have no representation of; we only have IsHidden, and the plan itself notes excluding-from-AI-schema is preferable to hiding (hiding breaks verified answers and all report consumers).
gaps.proposedCheck: Two rules. VIS-HELPER (Visibility, Deterministic->SafeFix): flag visible columns that are sort-by helper columns (column is the SortByColumn target of another column, or name matches _key/_sort/sortorder and is numeric) or unreferenced by any visible measure/relationship -> fix with set_column_hidden. DAC-AISCHEMA (DataAgentConfig, Deterministic->Proposal): the Prep-for-AI include-set is unset or still includes keys/helpers/duplicates; engine computes business-metric leaves + dependency closure, LLM adjudicates 'which sales measure'; needs a NEW set_ai_data_schema(culture, include[], autoCloseDependencies?) MCP tool (does not exist yet).

gaps.title: Mark the model Approved for Copilot and set item descriptions
gaps.notes: Two sub-parts. (1) 'Approved for Copilot': MISSING — it is a service-side flag, we have no rule and no way to read/set it (confirmed: no ApprovedForCopilot reference anywhere in the repo). (2) Rich item descriptions with distinct keywords: PARTIALLY covered — DESC-TABLE/DESC-MEASURE/DESC-COLUMN catch MISSING descriptions and DESC-ECHO catches trivial/name-echo ones, but we have NO model-level/dataset description rule (DESC-* are per-table/column/measure, not the semantic-model item description) and no 'name vs description use distinct keywords' check.
gaps.proposedCheck: DAC-APPROVED-COPILOT (DataAgentConfig, Deterministic->Proposal): requirement is marked deterministic:true; detect the service-side 'Approved for Copilot' flag is unset (read via Fabric REST/capability detection, NOT a TOM property) and surface it; fix is a Proposal/advisory (or a NEW set_approved_for_copilot tool) since it must be confirmed at runtime, not assumed. DESC-MODEL (Descriptions, LlmGenerate->AiContent): model.Model.Description (the dataset description) is empty or echoes the model name -> author via set_description against the model object. Optionally extend DESC-ECHO to flag description keyword-overlap with the object name (LlmJudgment).

gaps.title: Limit model complexity for reliable natural-language answers
gaps.notes: LIMIT-SCALE covers the documented hard ceilings, REL-DISCONNECTED finds isolated business tables, and DAC-FIELD-PARAM-COMPLEXITY surfaces field-parameter complexity. The row remains partial because unused-object intent, acceptable currency-conversion complexity and overall reliability are business judgments.
gaps.proposedCheck: The deterministic metadata slice is implemented. Do not infer unused objects or an unreliable business design from names and counts; those remaining decisions belong to review and model interviewing.

gaps.title: Scope the agent to a single domain / use case
gaps.notes: Entirely outside our current model-metadata scope. This is an agent-design property (one agent = one domain), not a semantic-model metadata fact we can read from TOM. DataAgentConfig (weight, no rules) is where any agent-level guidance would live, but it is empty. We cannot deterministically see the agent's question breadth from the model.
gaps.proposedCheck: DataAgentConfig, AiContent/Proposal (non-deterministic, advisory). There is no metadata predicate; surface it as advisory guidance inside the AI-instructions authoring flow: when DAC-INSTRUCTIONS generates the instructions block, include a single-domain framing and prompt the user to confirm scope. Optionally a weak heuristic proxy: model spans many unrelated subject-area table clusters (multiple disconnected relationship components) -> Proposal 'consider splitting into domain-scoped agents'. Fix path: set_ai_instructions (new tool) + human decision; no deterministic auto-fix.

gaps.title: Minimize data source scope; limit to 25 or fewer tables
gaps.notes: LIMIT-DATAAGENT-TABLES deterministically counts visible non-calculation-group tables and surfaces a proposal when the model exposes more than 25. The selected-table state of a deployed data agent is out of band, so the model count is explicitly an advisory proxy with Applicable=0; it never inflates or docks the score.
gaps.proposedCheck: Implemented and pinned below/above the threshold plus advisory dormancy by ReadinessCatalogBatch4Tests.

gaps.title: Cap total data sources per agent at five
gaps.notes: Out of scope for a single-model metadata scanner: a Semanticus session is connected to ONE semantic model, so the count of data sources attached to a Fabric data agent (up to 5: lakehouses, warehouses, semantic models, KQL, ontologies, Graph) is not visible from the model we analyze. This is a hard-gate at the AGENT level, category Scale, deterministic — but the data lives in the Fabric agent item, not the model.
gaps.proposedCheck: DataAgentConfig/Scale, Deterministic hard-gate, but only evaluable with Fabric REST access to the agent item (Applicable=0 / not-evaluated otherwise, degrading gracefully like the dmv rules). Predicate: agent.dataSources.Count > 5 -> Critical gate. Fix: Proposal/advisory only (remove a source in Fabric); no MCP tool in our surface touches the agent item. Most likely stays an advisory note in DAC-INSTRUCTIONS rather than a scored rule, since we can't read it from the model.

gaps.title: State what to do, not just what to avoid
gaps.notes: A quality property of the AI-instructions TEXT, which we neither store nor evaluate (DataAgentConfig empty). No rule today reasons about instruction prose. Depends entirely on DAC-INSTRUCTIONS existing first.
gaps.proposedCheck: DataAgentConfig, AiContent/LlmJudgment (non-deterministic). Once an AI-instructions block exists, an LLM-judgment pass scores whether guidance is phrased as positive directives vs only prohibitions ('never', "don't", 'avoid' density) and proposes rewrites. Detection is LLM verdict {pass,reason} over the instructions text; fix is AiContent rewrite applied via set_ai_instructions(append/overwrite). Best implemented as a generation constraint in the DAC-INSTRUCTIONS prompt template plus a judgment rule on existing text.

gaps.title: Define business terms, abbreviations, and synonyms in instructions
gaps.notes: Two channels. (1) Field-level synonyms in the LSDL: COVERED by SYN-SCHEMA (no linguistic schema at all -> enable_qna) and SYN-FIELD (visible field has no synonyms -> AiContent via set_synonyms). (2) Term/abbreviation/acronym definitions in agent/data-source INSTRUCTIONS (fiscal vs calendar year, SKU, NPS, MAU, cross-source vs dataset-specific): MISSING — that lives in the AI-instructions text store we don't have. Category is Synonyms for this requirement; our Synonyms rules only handle the LSDL term-mapping half, not the prose-definitions half.
gaps.proposedCheck: DataAgentConfig (or Synonyms), AiContent. Deterministic detect: AI-instructions block lacks a Terminology/Definitions section (string/section presence check, degraded). Generate: LLM drafts definitions for detected abbreviations/acronyms found in object names (ALL-CAPS tokens, known patterns) and ambiguous business terms; place cross-source defs at agent level, dataset-specific at data-source level. Fix via the new set_ai_instructions tool. Complements (does not replace) SYN-FIELD.

gaps.title: Use leading words / syntax fragments to nudge query generation
gaps.notes: Not applicable to semantic models (the requirement itself scopes to SQL/KQL data sources: LIKE '%...%' hints, join syntax). For semantic-model sources the data-agent DAX tool IGNORES agent-level instructions/example queries and reads only model metadata + Prep-for-AI (the non-obvious MS truth captured in our plan). So this is intentionally out of scope for Semanticus today and arguably should stay an explicit 'N/A for semantic models' note rather than a rule.
gaps.proposedCheck: No rule recommended. If ever supported, it would be a DataAgentConfig data-source-instruction AiContent helper for SQL/KQL sources only — outside our single-semantic-model scope. Best handled as documentation ('N/A: semantic-model DAX tool ignores data-source query hints') rather than a scored predicate, to avoid penalizing models for something that does not apply to them.

gaps.title: Keep instructions concise, focused, and current
gaps.notes: Another quality property of AI-instructions text we don't store or evaluate. DESC-LONG enforces a 200-char cap on DESCRIPTIONS (a different field), and prompt templates are planned to enforce a 10,000-char instructions cap, but there is no RULE that scores instruction conciseness/focus/currency, and no instructions store exists. Depends on DAC-INSTRUCTIONS.
gaps.proposedCheck: DataAgentConfig, mixed. Deterministic part: instructions length > 10,000-char cap, or presence of vague stock phrases ('all kinds of questions','just do your best','as needed') via a stop-phrase regex -> flag. Quality part (focus/currency, references to old systems): LlmJudgment verdict {pass,reason}. Fix: AiContent rewrite -> set_ai_instructions. Pairs with DAC-INSTR-POSITIVE as the two instruction-quality judgment rules over the same managed text block.

gaps.title: Write detailed agent instructions: role, behavior, tone, sources, fallback
gaps.proposedCheck: AI-content/Proposal rule in the (currently empty) DataAgentConfig category. Detection: read the Prep-for-AI 'AI instructions' store on the model/culture (the ≤10k-char block the plan calls the delimited Semanticus-managed LSDL section). Deterministic pre-filter = block is empty/whitespace OR shorter than a floor (~120 chars) -> violation 'No agent/AI instructions'. Quality grading is LlmJudgment: have the user's Claude confirm the block actually names role, expected behavior, tone/style, prioritized/preferred sources, when-to-use-which-source routing, and missing-info fallback; flag each missing facet. Fix path = LlmGenerate the grouped instruction block, then write it via a new set_ai_instructions(culture, instructions, append) MCP tool (not yet implemented; only in the plan's tool table). CAVEAT to encode: for SEMANTIC-MODEL sources the data-agent DAX tool ignores agent-level instructions, so this content must be authored into the MODEL Prep-for-AI layer, never the agent item.
gaps.notes: Not covered at all. DataAgentConfig category exists in the enum (Readiness.cs) and carries weight 0.08 in ReadinessAnalyzer.cs, but ships ZERO rules. Worse, the analyzer excludes any category with no applicable rules from the weighted mean (ReadinessAnalyzer.cs lines 35 + 53 'HasRules'), so the 0.08 weight is currently inert/dead and silently re-normalizes onto the other 7 categories — the model gets no credit-or-penalty for agent-instruction quality today. There is also no set_ai_instructions MCP tool implemented yet; it lives only in docs/ai-readiness-plan.md section 4. This is the single largest gap in the DataAgentConfig story.

gaps.title: Write detailed data source instructions (purpose, columns, joins, value formats)
gaps.proposedCheck: AI-content/Proposal rule in DataAgentConfig. Detection: for non-semantic-model sources, read the per-data-source instruction text and flag empty/thin instructions and missing facets (source purpose, answerable question types, required response columns, join logic, typical value formats e.g. State 'CA' vs 'California'). Deterministic floor = empty/short; LlmJudgment grades whether join logic + value-format examples are present. Fix = LlmGenerate structured source instructions, grounded by the engine's relationship graph (join logic) and a live distinct-value sample via run_dax/pivot EVALUATE TOPN (to supply real value formats), applied via a set_data_source_instructions MCP tool (to-be-built). HARD-GATE to encode: Power BI semantic models do NOT support data-source instructions/descriptions at the agent level, so for the semantic-model profile this rule must be EXCLUDED (Applicable=0) and the equivalent signal redirected to Prep-for-AI table/column descriptions (which we DO cover via DESC-TABLE/DESC-COLUMN).
gaps.notes: No rule reads or scores data-source instructions. Note the strong overlap with our existing DESC-TABLE and DESC-COLUMN: for the semantic-model consumer the doc explicitly says agent-level source instructions are unsupported and the substitute is rich table/column metadata — which our description rules already drive. So for semantic models this requirement is partially served by DESC-TABLE/DESC-COLUMN; for Lakehouse/Warehouse/KQL sources it is fully missing. Our engine already has the join graph (m.Relationships) and a live connection (run_dax/pivot) to source real value formats, so the grounding is feasible — only the read/write of the agent-source-instruction store and the rule are absent.

gaps.title: Provide example queries (few-shot) for complex/representative logic
gaps.proposedCheck: Needs a NEW category. The requirement is tagged category 'VerifiedAnswers', which does NOT exist in our ReadinessCategory enum (Readiness.cs only has Naming/Descriptions/Synonyms/Relationships/Visibility/Formatting/CopilotLimits/DataAgentConfig). Add a VerifiedAnswers category (and give it a weight, splitting the current 0.08 DataAgentConfig allocation). Rule = Proposal/AI-content: detect example-query count==0 for the source and propose few-shot pairs for representative filtering/join/aggregation/date logic. Fix = LlmGenerate question+query pairs (correct SQL/KQL syntax), then a set_example_query MCP tool. HARD-GATE: only emit Applicable>0 for Lakehouse/Warehouse/Eventhouse-KQL sources; for semantic models this rule is N/A (Applicable=0) and the Verified-Answers rule applies instead (see VA-PRESENT below).
gaps.notes: Completely missing and structurally unrepresentable today: there is no VerifiedAnswers category, no example-query store reader, and no example-query MCP tool. Semanticus is positioned around semantic models (its live connection is run_dax/verify_dax_equivalence over DAX), where few-shot example queries are NOT supported anyway — so for our primary consumer this maps to Verified Answers, not example queries. The catalog's VerifiedAnswers tag is the tell that this whole block belongs in a new category we don't have.

gaps.title: Example queries retrieved via vector similarity (top ~3-4)
gaps.proposedCheck: New VerifiedAnswers category. This is best-practice/advisory (how retrieval works: per question the agent vector-searches and injects top ~3-4 examples), so the actionable rule is 'ensure examples are diverse/distinct so the right ones retrieve'. Detection = LlmJudgment over the example set: flag near-duplicate or redundant examples that would crowd out the top-k slots; deterministic helper = embedding/Jaccard similarity clustering to surface clusters. Fix = Proposal to diversify (LlmGenerate replacements). Only applies to non-semantic-model sources (same hard-gate as VA-EXAMPLES-PRESENT).
gaps.notes: Missing. Pure advisory about retrieval mechanics; only becomes a rule as a diversity/de-dup check on example sets we don't store or read. Same as above: belongs in a VerifiedAnswers category we don't have, and is N/A for the semantic-model path that Semanticus actually drives.

gaps.title: Example queries require a question plus valid query; each must pass schema validation
gaps.proposedCheck: New VerifiedAnswers category; this one is DETERMINISTIC (hard-gate). Predicate per example: non-empty natural-language question AND non-empty query AND question is unique across the set AND the query validates against the selected source schema (and parses). Examples failing validation are not sent to the agent -> flag them. This is the closest analogue to our EXISTING live-DAX validation primitive verify_dax_equivalence / run_dax: we already validate DAX against the live model, so the engine pattern exists — it just isn't pointed at an example-query store (which we don't have for the semantic-model path). Fix = Proposal: drop/repair invalid examples. Applies only to Lakehouse/Warehouse/KQL sources.
gaps.notes: Missing as a rule, but the validation MACHINERY partially exists: our live connection already does query validation (verify_dax_equivalence, run_dax) for DAX. The gap is the example-query artifact itself plus SQL/KQL schema validation, neither of which exists. Deterministic and gateable once an example store is modeled.

gaps.title: Example queries NOT supported for semantic models or ontologies
gaps.proposedCheck: Deterministic guard/meta-rule (hard-gate). Predicate: if source kind == Power BI Semantic Model or Ontology, then example-query few-shot is unsupported -> any example-query rule must be EXCLUDED and the model steered to Verified Answers instead. In our codebase Semanticus only ever has a semantic model loaded, so this gate is effectively ALWAYS TRUE for us: it should suppress all VA-EXAMPLE* rules (Applicable=0) and surface a single advisory 'use Verified Answers, not example queries'. No fix action; it's a routing/applicability constraint that protects the score from penalizing the wrong artifact.
gaps.notes: Missing as an explicit guard, though implicitly true: because Semanticus targets semantic models, every example-query requirement above is N/A for us and should hard-gate to Verified Answers. Encoding this guard is what prevents the proposed VA-EXAMPLE* rules from ever wrongly firing on a semantic model. Cheap deterministic predicate on source type.

gaps.title: Example query quality: Clarity, Relatedness, Mapping all positive
gaps.proposedCheck: New VerifiedAnswers category; AI-content (LlmJudgment) with a deterministic Mapping helper. Per example score three axes: Clarity (question is unambiguous, has metric/timeframe/filters, not vague/multi-intent like 'Show performance'), Relatedness (query matches question intent/metric/filters/granularity), Mapping (every literal — number, date, category — in the question appears in the query; this axis is partly DETERMINISTIC via literal extraction + substring/token match). High quality only when all three positive. Fix = Proposal/LlmGenerate rewrite. Semantic-model-source hard-gate applies (N/A for us, redirect to Verified-Answer trigger quality).
gaps.notes: Missing. The Mapping axis is a deterministic predicate (literal presence) we could implement cheaply; Clarity/Relatedness need LLM judgment, which fits our LlmJudgment RuleKind pattern. No example store exists, so nothing to score today, and it is N/A for the semantic-model consumer (maps instead to Verified-Answer trigger-phrase quality).

gaps.title: Add query comments and avoid overlapping/contradicting examples
gaps.proposedCheck: New VerifiedAnswers category; best-practice. Two sub-checks: (a) deterministic — example query contains no comment lines (regex for -- / /* */ depending on dialect) where substitution/complex-join guidance is expected -> advisory to add comments; (b) LlmJudgment/clustering — detect overlapping or contradicting examples (same intent, different tables or same metric with different aggregation/granularity). Fix = Proposal to add comments and resolve conflicts (LlmGenerate). Semantic-model hard-gate applies.
gaps.notes: Missing. Overlaps conceptually with our existing NAME-DUP idea (ambiguity detection) but operates on example queries we don't store. The conflict-detection sub-check mirrors the SDK evaluate_few_shots conflict pass (next item).

gaps.title: Validate and de-conflict example queries with the SDK
gaps.proposedCheck: New VerifiedAnswers category; best-practice, partly deterministic. This is the external Fabric Data Agent SDK evaluate_few_shots workflow: validate each NL/query pair against schema, surface pass/fail + Clarity/Relatedness/Mapping, and run conflict detection (same intent -> different tables, or same metric -> different aggregation/granularity). Our analogue: a rule that EITHER invokes the SDK (out-of-process, SQL-only today) OR reimplements its checks via VA-EXAMPLE-VALID + VA-EXAMPLE-QUALITY + VA-EXAMPLE-COMMENTS-DEDUP above. Fix = Proposal: iterate to resolve flagged conflicts. SQL-source only; N/A for semantic models.
gaps.notes: Missing. This is essentially the umbrella/tooling rule over the four VA-EXAMPLE* checks; it depends on an external SDK (SQL-only) and on an example store we don't have. Lowest priority for Semanticus given our semantic-model focus; would only matter if we add Lakehouse/Warehouse source support.

gaps.title: Semantic models: DAX tool uses ONLY Prep for AI + metadata, ignores agent instructions
gaps.proposedCheck: This is a ROUTING CONSTRAINT, not an object check, and our whole architecture already honors it: every implemented rule (DESC-*, NAME-*, SYN-*, VIS-FK, FMT-*, CAT-GEO, REL-BIDI, DATE-MARK, LIMIT-SCALE) targets the MODEL metadata / Prep-for-AI layer, and the MCP fix tools (set_description, set_synonyms, enable_qna, set_measure_format, mark_date_table, set_data_category, set_relationship_crossfilter, rename_object) all write the model — never an agent-level item. So the spirit is COVERED by design. What is missing is making it EXPLICIT: a DataAgentConfig advisory DAC-PREP-FOR-AI-LAYER (LlmJudgment/advisory) that, for the semantic-model profile, states 'agent-level instructions/example queries are ignored; author into Prep for AI' and EXCLUDES (Applicable=0) any agent-level rule. The docs/ai-readiness-plan.md already records this MS truth (section 2.H note + section 4 note) — it just isn't encoded as a rule.
gaps.notes: Partial: behaviorally satisfied (all our rules and MCP tools target model + Prep-for-AI, exactly as the doc demands), and the constraint is written down in the plan, but there is no rule/flag that asserts it or that gates agent-level requirements off for semantic-model sources. Adding the explicit guard is what turns 'we happen to comply' into 'we enforce the routing'. Note also that the Prep-for-AI WRITE layer itself (AI instructions store) is not yet implemented — only first-class TOM props (Description/FormatString/etc.) are written today — so the 'Prep for AI' half is aspirational in code.

gaps.title: Configure an AI data schema (Prep for AI) and mirror its table selection in the agent
gaps.proposedCheck: DataAgentConfig rule, D->J (deterministic core + LLM adjudication). Detection: read the Prep-for-AI 'Simplify data schema' include-set; flag when (a) it is unset, or (b) it includes helper/intermediate or duplicate objects, or (c) it omits dependent objects a selected measure references. Deterministic part: the engine computes business-metric leaves + dependency closure via DISCOVER_CALC_DEPENDENCY (our live connection can run this) and diffs against the include-set; LlmJudgment adjudicates ambiguous 'which sales measure' choices. We ALREADY have adjacent signals: VIS-FK (hide FK noise) and FMT-SUMMARIZE reduce ambiguity, and the plan lists this as rule H.'No AI data schema'. Fix = set_ai_data_schema(culture, include[], autoCloseDependencies:true) MCP tool (to-be-built) plus an emitted exact table list to mirror in the agent.
gaps.notes: Missing as a scored rule. The dependency-closure machinery (DISCOVER_CALC_DEPENDENCY) is available via our live connection and is described in the plan (sections 4 step 3, 5, 7) but not implemented, and there is no Prep-for-AI include-set reader/writer. Partially adjacent to VIS-FK/FMT-SUMMARIZE (both reduce schema noise) but neither addresses the include-set selection or the agent-table mirroring. This plus DAC-AGENT-INSTRUCTIONS are the two concrete things the empty DataAgentConfig category should hold first.

gaps.title: Create Verified Answers for common/complex semantic-model questions
gaps.proposedCheck: New VerifiedAnswers category (or, pragmatically, place under DataAgentConfig until the category exists). This is THE verified-answers rule for the semantic-model consumer Semanticus actually targets, so it is the highest-value item in this whole block for us. Detection: verified-answers store count == 0 (or thin) -> AI-content violation. Deterministic caps from the doc to validate against: <=250/model, 5-7 trigger phrases, <=500 chars each, <=3 filters; trigger fields must be VISIBLE and not report-only measures (we can validate visibility with the same predicates VIS-FK / IsHidden checks use). Fix = LlmGenerate: expand the top/most-misinterpreted questions into complete robust trigger phrases + a visual spec (columns/measures/filters), grounded by run_dax/pivot to confirm the DAX returns, then a set_verified_answer(def) MCP tool (in the plan's tool table, not yet built). The agent checks for an exact/semantically-similar match before generating, so triggers must be complete.
gaps.notes: Missing, and this is the gap that matters most for Semanticus: Verified Answers are the semantic-model substitute for example queries, stored at the semantic-model level — squarely in our wheelhouse (we have the live model + run_dax + verify_dax_equivalence to validate the visual spec).
