Yet there is no rule, no VerifiedAnswers category, and no set_verified_answer tool implemented (plan-only). The visibility/non-report-measure validation it needs is already expressible with our existing IsHidden/VIS-FK predicates. Recommend implementing VA-PRESENT first within the empty DataAgentConfig weight before splitting out a full VerifiedAnswers category.

gaps.title: Verified answer config: 5-7 trigger questions, up to 3 filters, no hidden fields
gaps.proposedCheck: Mostly deterministic (validation) once a verified-answer artifact exists, but there is NO storage/tool for it today. The plan (DataAgentConfig section, doc line 99) calls for a Proposal/AiContent rule: detect verified-answers store == 0 (model-level finding, FixKind.Proposal) and, when present, deterministically validate each answer: 5-7 trigger phrases, <=3 filters, <=500 chars, and that every referenced table/column/measure resolves AND is NOT IsHidden (verified answers silently fail on hidden fields), plus a stale-reference check after renames. Fix path requires a NEW set_verified_answer MCP tool writing the Prep-for-AI/LSDL verified-answers blob (does not exist yet) + reverse-engineered storage adapter; the user's Claude authors trigger phrasings (formal+conversational) and the visual spec. Today this is 100% uncovered: no rule, no storage, no tool.
gaps.notes: No verified-answer rule, storage, or MCP tool exists anywhere (confirmed: 'verified' in the engine only refers to verify_dax_equivalence DAX-rewrite proofs, not data-agent verified answers). DataAgentConfig category carries 0.08 weight but zero rules. The hidden-field guard is the high-value deterministic check and overlaps our VIS-FK/set_column_hidden plumbing, but nothing wires it to verified answers.

gaps.title: Add semantic-model AI instructions in Prep for AI (terminology, metric/date/source preferences)
gaps.proposedCheck: Model-level rule, FixKind.AiContent (RuleKind.LlmGenerate). Deterministic presence check: the Prep-for-AI AI-instructions store is empty/absent (or under a Semanticus-managed delimiter). The user's Claude drafts grouped sections (business terminology e.g. 'top performer', metric preferences e.g. 'use Contribution_Margin not Gross_Profit', time-period/fiscal definitions, default groupings, default date field/source), <=10,000 chars, clear/non-conflicting. Fix path needs a NEW set_ai_instructions MCP tool (append into a delimited managed section) writing the LSDL/Prep-for-AI blob - this tool and storage do NOT exist today. This is the single most important thing DataAgentConfig should contain.
gaps.notes: Completely uncovered. No AI-instructions storage, rule, or tool. This is the headline of the empty DataAgentConfig category.

gaps.title: Use a star schema, not flat/denormalized/pivoted tables
gaps.proposedCheck: Implemented for observable graph anti-patterns. Flat/denormalized/pivoted intent remains a judgment non-goal and structural remediation stays Proposal-only.
gaps.notes: The relationship graph now has deterministic checks for bidirectional, many-to-many, snowflake/bridge and disconnected structures. Classifying a wide table as intentionally flat, denormalized or pivoted still needs business judgment, so the row remains partial and is pinned in residualBuckets.judgment.

gaps.title: Exclude helper/intermediate and duplicate/overlapping measures from the AI schema
gaps.proposedCheck: NAME-DUP catches duplicate VISIBLE *column* names across tables (ambiguity), which is adjacent but NOT the requirement: it does not cover measures, near-duplicate/overlapping measure semantics (Total Sales vs Sales Amount vs Revenue), or helper/intermediate-measure exclusion. Propose DAC-AI-SCHEMA-EXCLUDE in DataAgentConfig: (a) deterministic detection of helper measures - a visible measure referenced ONLY by other measures (needs DISCOVER_CALC_DEPENDENCY via the live connection / run_dmv) or matching /_base|_helper|^_|temp|aux/; (b) LLM-judgment for overlapping/near-duplicate business measures. Fix path = exclude from the AI data schema (preferred over hiding, which breaks verified answers) via a NEW set_ai_data_schema MCP tool + Prep-for-AI store - neither exists. Until then it degrades to set_column_hidden/IsHidden, which is the wrong lever.
gaps.notes: NAME-DUP is the only adjacent rule and it is column-only ambiguity, FixKind.Proposal. No helper-measure detection, no overlapping-measure judgment, no AI-data-schema include/exclude storage or tool. Dependency-closure detection requires DISCOVER_CALC_DEPENDENCY (Wave-2 live-grounded).

gaps.title: Use explicit measures and correct default summarization, not implicit measures
gaps.proposedCheck: Implemented as a disjoint partition: FMT-SUMMARIZE owns keys/endpoints, SUMMARIZE-DIMENSION owns canonical non-additive numeric dimensions, and DAC-IMPLICIT-MEASURE owns the remaining visible numeric columns that auto-aggregate.
gaps.notes: Together the three deterministic rules evaluate the visible numeric surface without cross-category double-counting. Safe metadata fixes set non-additive/key columns to None; additive implicit measures use Proposal because an explicit DAX measure needs reviewed business intent.

gaps.title: Avoid conflicting instructions vs verified answers
gaps.proposedCheck: Depends entirely on two artifacts we don't store yet (AI instructions + verified answers), so it is fully missing. Propose DAC-INSTRUCTION-CONFLICT in DataAgentConfig, RuleKind.LlmJudgment, FixKind.Proposal: once DAC-AI-INSTRUCTIONS and DAC-VERIFIED-ANSWERS storage exist, cross-read both and have the user's Claude flag contradictions (e.g. an instruction 'analyze by fiscal quarter' vs a verified answer filtered on calendar quarter; 'use Contribution_Margin' vs an answer referencing Gross_Profit). No deterministic predicate is feasible; it is pure LLM adjudication over the two managed JSON blobs. Fix = surface the conflict for human edit of one side (no auto-fix).
gaps.notes: Blocked on the missing AI-instructions and verified-answers stores; inherently LLM-judgment. Nothing today reads either artifact, so 0% coverage.

gaps.title: Give report visuals descriptive titles (semantic-model grounding)
gaps.proposedCheck: Out of scope for a TOM/model-only engine as built: report visual metadata lives in the report layer (.pbir/PBIR/Power BI report), not in the Tabular model our rules iterate (m.Tables/AllMeasures/AllColumns/Cultures). All five DESC-* rules operate on model objects only. Propose DESC-VISUAL-TITLE (Descriptions, FixKind.Proposal, advisory) ONLY if/when the engine can read connected report definitions (PBIR JSON or Fabric REST) - parse visuals incl. hidden pages/visuals, flag generic/blank titles ('Chart','Visual 1'); the user's Claude drafts descriptive titles, but applying them requires writing the report layer, which Semanticus explicitly cannot validate from TOM (plan guardrail). Realistically advisory-only.
gaps.notes: No report-layer access exists; the readiness engine is model-metadata only. The plan itself flags report-layer bindings as unvalidatable from TOM. Best treated as an advisory Proposal, not an auto-fix.

gaps.title: Optimize the semantic model for performance / remove bloat
gaps.ourRuleId: LIMIT-SCALE, SCALE-HICARD-COLUMN, FMT-SUMMARIZE, BP-DAX-IFERROR, BP-DAX-SUMMARIZE-EXT, BP-DAX-BARE-TABLE-FILTER, BP-DAX-VAR-ALIAS
gaps.proposedCheck: Implemented readiness signals cover hard ceilings, live high-cardinality text, incorrect summarization and four deterministic inefficient-DAX patterns. Live cardinality stays dormant when no measurement exists. The row remains partial because unnecessary-object selection and exhaustive performance optimization require workload evidence and judgment.
gaps.notes: Batch 6 replaces the stale claim that no live cardinality rule exists. Semanticus detects objective risk signals but does not treat performance optimization as complete model-state proof.

gaps.title: Verify the generated DAX in each test response
gaps.proposedRuleId: (no readiness rule - capability, not a model-state predicate)
gaps.proposedCheck: This is a testing-workflow practice, not a static model property, so it cannot be a coverage rule over model metadata. But our engine ALREADY provides the underlying capability: run_dax executes a generated query, pivot_measure/preview_table sanity-check values, and verify_dax_equivalence proves a rewrite is behavior-preserving across a group-by matrix. So the human-in-the-loop 'verify the DAX' step is supported by existing tools (partial = capability present, no rule). No new rule is warranted; if anything, document it as a DataAgentConfig workflow affordance. No model predicate, no fix path beyond the existing live-connection tools.
gaps.notes: Not a deterministic model-state requirement - it is per-response testing behavior. Existing live tools (run_dax, pivot_measure, verify_dax_equivalence) cover the mechanics; there is nothing to 'score' on the model itself.

gaps.title: Evaluate and iterate against ground-truth Q&A pairs
gaps.proposedRuleId: (no readiness rule - external SDK evaluation loop)
gaps.proposedCheck: Out of scope for a model-metadata readiness rule: this is the Fabric data-agent Python SDK running automated eval against ground-truth Q&A pairs (the SDK evaluates only, cannot modify Prep-for-AI). There is no model property to assert and no MCP tool that runs the SDK. Not a candidate for a deterministic/AiContent/Proposal rule. At most, DataAgentConfig could surface an advisory 'no evaluation set found' if we chose to store ground-truth pairs as a managed artifact, but that is a product feature, not a readiness predicate. Recommend leaving uncovered with a documented rationale rather than inventing a rule.
gaps.notes: Pure external evaluation workflow; nothing in the model to score, no fix tool. Correctly absent from the ruleset.

gaps.title: Define agent scope before selecting objects (scope-first workflow)
gaps.proposedCheck: This is the AI-data-schema include-set requirement (don't connect the whole model; select only relevant tables/columns/measures), and we have no AI-data-schema store or rule. Propose DAC-AI-SCHEMA-SCOPE in DataAgentConfig, RuleKind.LlmJudgment+Deterministic, FixKind.Proposal: deterministic part = the Prep-for-AI include-set is unset or equals 'all objects' (no scoping); LLM part = given a stated scope/question-set, the user's Claude proposes the minimal relevant table/column/measure include-set (business-metric leaves + dependency closure). Fix path needs the NEW set_ai_data_schema MCP tool + Prep-for-AI storage (do not exist). Shares storage with DAC-AI-SCHEMA-EXCLUDE; together they are the 'AI data schema' that DataAgentConfig is missing.
gaps.notes: No AI-data-schema storage/rule/tool. This and DAC-AI-SCHEMA-EXCLUDE are two faces of the same missing 'AI data schema' artifact. Scope definition itself is human input the LLM helps shape; the include-set is the storable, validatable part.

gaps.title: Use source control / deployment pipelines for agent configs
gaps.proposedCheck: Out of scope for a model-state readiness predicate: Git integration + Fabric deployment pipelines for dev/test/prod promotion are CI/CD concerns outside the TOM model. There is no model property to assert and no MCP tool to enforce it. Our contribution is adjacent but real - because Prep-for-AI configs (AI instructions / data schema / verified answers) live IN the model's LSDL/TMDL, once DAC-* writers exist those configs become diffable TMDL that naturally version-controls in the PBIP folder (the plan persists a JSON sidecar + annotation history for exactly this). So no rule; the enabling work is making the DataAgentConfig artifacts serialize to reviewable TMDL/sidecar so they CAN be Git-managed. No deterministic/AiContent/Proposal rule warranted.
gaps.notes: External DevOps practice; no model predicate. The indirect value is that writing Prep-for-AI config into TMDL/LSDL (once those writers exist) makes it Git-diffable - the plan already calls for a JSON sidecar + Semanticus_AIReadiness_History annotation. Correctly absent as a rule.

gaps.title: Paid capacity F2+ or P1+ required
gaps.notes: No capacity/SKU awareness exists anywhere in the codebase. The engine operates on TMDL/TOM model metadata (plus an optional read-only XMLA/local connection); it never sees the hosting workspace's capacity tier. LIMIT-SCALE (CopilotLimits) is the only 'hard gate' and it only counts model objects (tables>500, cols>10000, measures>5000, rels>2000, name>256) — it has nothing to do with capacity. The DataAgentConfig category that should hold environment prerequisites has weight 0.08 but zero rules, so it is dropped from the weighted mean entirely.
gaps.proposedCheck: Proposal / prerequisite-attestation rule, NOT deterministic on model metadata. Detection: requires environment context the model does not carry. When connected via connect_xmla to a powerbi:// endpoint, resolve the workspace and query the Fabric/Power BI Admin or Capacities REST API (GET /v1.0/myorg/admin/capacities or /groups/{id}) to read the capacity SKU; flag when SKU is below F2/P1 or absent (Pro/PPU only). Offline (TMDL-only) it must surface as an un-evaluable 'prerequisite — confirm capacity is F2+/P1+' proposal rather than a scored predicate. No MCP fix tool exists or is appropriate (capacity is provisioned in Fabric/Azure, outside Semanticus). Category: DataAgentConfig (environment prerequisites).

gaps.title: Trial SKUs and free SKUs not supported
gaps.notes: Same gap as capacity: no SKU/trial detection. Nothing in ReadinessRules.cs, ReadinessAnalyzer.cs, or the engine reads SKU type or trial state. The model carries no signal of its host capacity.
gaps.proposedCheck: Proposal / prerequisite-attestation, not deterministic on metadata. Detection: with a live workspace connection, read the capacity SKU string via the Capacities REST API and flag Trial/FT* SKUs and free SKUs as Copilot-ineligible. Offline it is an un-evaluable prerequisite the scorecard should list ('confirm capacity is a paid SKU, not Trial'). Could fold into ENV-CAPACITY as the same admin-REST probe (paid F2+/P1+ AND non-trial). No fix MCP tool (provision a paid capacity in Fabric). Category: DataAgentConfig.

gaps.title: Capacity must be in a supported region
gaps.notes: No region awareness. The engine has no workspace/capacity region. Catalog itself files this under DataAgentConfig, which currently has weight but no rules — exactly the empty bucket.
gaps.proposedCheck: Proposal / prerequisite, not deterministic on model metadata (deterministic=false in catalog). Detection: with a live connection, read the capacity's region via the Capacities REST API and compare against a pinned static allow-list of Copilot-supported Fabric regions (maintained as data in the rule); flag if the region is unsupported. Offline: un-evaluable prerequisite surfaced as a checklist item. No fix MCP tool (move workspace to a supported region, an admin action). Category: DataAgentConfig.

gaps.title: Cross-geo Azure OpenAI tenant setting required outside US/EU boundary
gaps.notes: No tenant-setting awareness. Nothing reads tenant admin settings or geographic/compliance boundary. Belongs in the empty DataAgentConfig bucket.
gaps.proposedCheck: Proposal / prerequisite-attestation, not deterministic (deterministic=false). Detection: read the tenant home region (Admin REST, e.g. /admin/capacities or tenant metadata) and, when it is outside the US/EU(France) data boundary, require the tenant setting 'Data sent to Azure OpenAI can be processed outside your tenant's geographic region...' to be enabled. Tenant-setting state is generally not machine-readable to a non-admin, so realistically this is an attestation/checklist item gated on detected home region. No Semanticus fix MCP tool (tenant admin toggles it in the Fabric/Power BI admin portal). Category: DataAgentConfig.

gaps.title: Tenant admin Copilot setting must be enabled
gaps.notes: No tenant-admin-setting detection. 'Users can use Copilot and other features powered by Azure OpenAI' is not visible to the model or the read-only connection. Belongs in the empty DataAgentConfig bucket.
gaps.proposedCheck: Proposal / prerequisite-attestation, not deterministic (deterministic=false). Detection: only a tenant admin can read this via the Admin tenant-settings REST API; for non-admins it is an un-evaluable prerequisite surfaced as a checklist item ('confirm the tenant Copilot setting is enabled; note it must be at tenant level, not only capacity/delegated'). No Semanticus fix tool — admin toggles it in the portal. Category: DataAgentConfig.

gaps.title: Sovereign clouds not supported
gaps.notes: No cloud-instance awareness. Nothing detects national/sovereign clouds. Catalog files this under DataAgentConfig — the empty weighted bucket.
gaps.proposedCheck: Proposal / prerequisite, not deterministic (deterministic=false). Detection: infer the national cloud from the live XMLA endpoint host (e.g. analysis.usgovcloudapi.net, *.chinacloudapi.cn, *.cloudapi.de patterns vs commercial api.powerbi.com) when connected; flag any sovereign/national cloud as Copilot-unsupported (no GPU availability). Offline it is an un-evaluable prerequisite. No fix tool — a hard environmental block. Category: DataAgentConfig.

gaps.title: Q&A must be enabled for Copilot data questions
gaps.notes: DAC-QNA-DISABLED reads the actual file-backed qnaEnabled switch instead of treating linguistic-schema presence as a proxy. An explicit false value is a DataAgentConfig violation; an unavailable switch is dormant rather than assumed enabled.
gaps.proposedCheck: Implemented as DAC-QNA-DISABLED against definition.pbism settings.qnaEnabled. The rule is dormant when the actual switch is unavailable and does not substitute linguistic-schema presence as a proxy.

gaps.title: DirectQuery / Direct Lake models require manual Q&A enablement
gaps.notes: DAC-QNA-STORAGE inspects effective partition modes and surfaces a non-scoring verification advisory for DirectQuery/Direct Lake only when the on-disk Q&A switch cannot be read. When definition.pbism is readable, DAC-QNA-DISABLED evaluates the actual qnaEnabled value instead, so the two rules never double-flag the same model.
gaps.proposedCheck: Implemented and pinned across Import dormancy, unknown Direct Lake, and enabled DirectQuery states by ReadinessCatalogBatch4Tests.

gaps.title: Q&A index hard limit: first 1,000 model entities only
gaps.notes: LIMIT-QNA-INDEX deterministically counts visible tables, columns and measures in the Q&A-indexable surface, stays dormant below the documented threshold, and surfaces a Proposal above 1,000 entities.
gaps.proposedCheck: Implemented as LIMIT-QNA-INDEX. It is not an overall-grade hard gate because entities beyond the ceiling are a degraded index surface rather than proof that every AI use is blocked.

gaps.title: Q&A index hard limit: 5 million unique values
gaps.notes: SCALE-QNA-INDEX is a live Dmv ModelRule that sums measured cardinality only across visible string columns and flags totals above 5,000,000. It now uses Applicable=0 when no indexed-column statistics are available, so a failed or unavailable live measurement cannot create an always-pass CopilotLimits rule.
gaps.proposedCheck: Implemented as SCALE-QNA-INDEX. The offline scan omits live rules; an empty live-stat set also keeps this rule dormant.

gaps.title: Q&A indexes only text values under 100 characters
gaps.notes: Still open after completion batch 6. This requirement concerns maximum DATA-value length, not metadata description length. The readiness-only lane cannot supply the needed statistic; it requires a new live Engine query and a ReadinessLiveStats field.
gaps.proposedCheck: Deterministic but live-only (RuleKind.Dmv, best-practice/Info). Detection: for visible string columns, sample via run_dax (e.g. MAXX over LEN of distinct values, or EVALUATE TOPN of long values) and flag columns whose typical/text values are >=100 chars as 'not value-matchable by Copilot/Q&A'. Offline -> Applicable=0 (not evaluated). Category Scale, best-practice. Fix is advisory (these columns won't be matched by value lookups); optionally set_column_hidden if the column is noise. No deterministic auto-fix.

gaps.title: DirectQuery Q&A index refreshes at most once per day
gaps.notes: Not modeled, and depends on the same missing storage-mode detection as requirement 8. The engine cannot tell a DirectQuery/Direct Lake model from Import, so it cannot attach the once-per-day freshness caveat.
gaps.proposedCheck: Advisory/informational, not deterministic (catalog deterministic=false). Detection: reuse the QNA-DQ-DIRECTLAKE storage-mode predicate (TOM Partition.Mode == DirectQuery/DirectLake); when true, emit an Info finding noting the Q&A value index rebuilds at most once/day (Direct Lake Prep-for-AI/LSDL likewise syncs daily) so Copilot value matching can lag source data. Category Scale, best-practice. No fix — pure freshness-expectation context; surface in the scorecard's notes, not as a scored violation. Could be folded into QNA-DQ-DIRECTLAKE as an extra advisory line.

gaps.title: Prompt limit of 10,000 characters per prompt
gaps.notes: DESC-LONG enforces a 200-char cap on MEASURE descriptions only (Readiness.cs / ReadinessRules.cs line 134), which is the Copilot description-truncation limit, NOT the 10,000-char prompt limit. We generate no AI-instruction / verified-answer text today (DataAgentConfig has no rules and no MCP writer), so there is currently no generated text against which to enforce 10,000 chars. This is a validator-side guardrail, not a model-authoring gate; the ai-readiness-plan.md already references '<=10,000 char cap' for AI instructions but nothing implements it.
gaps.proposedCheck: Deterministic predicate, but it belongs in the GENERATE/VALIDATE pipeline (step 5 of make_model_ai_ready) rather than as a scored finding: when the workbench authors an AI-instructions block or verified-answer trigger text, reject/trim any single prompt-bound string > 10,000 effective chars before it is sent or written. Pair it with the existing 200-char description validator. Fix path: the future set_ai_instructions MCP tool (does not exist yet) validates length and truncates/front-loads; no remediation against the user's model is needed since this is a best-practice context limit, not a model defect.

gaps.title: Direct Lake / Lakehouse models not supported by Q&A live connect (Desktop)
gaps.notes: MODE-DIRECTLAKE-QNA deterministically inspects effective partition storage mode and surfaces a Direct Lake routing advisory. Applicable remains 0 because mode context is not a model-quality pass or failure.
gaps.proposedCheck: Implemented as MODE-DIRECTLAKE-QNA with FixKind.None and service-authoring guidance.

gaps.title: Q&A setup (linguistic teaching) only for Import and DirectQuery
gaps.notes: MODE-DIRECTLAKE-QNA deterministically detects effective Direct Lake partition mode and explicitly says that Desktop Q&A setup linguistic teaching is unavailable. CFG-PREP-MODE routes Prep-for-AI authoring for the same model to the Fabric service. Both are non-scoring advisories with Applicable=0, so mode context never inflates or docks the grade.
gaps.proposedCheck: Implemented by the existing storage-mode advisories rather than a duplicate Synonyms rule. Import and DirectQuery remain clean; Direct Lake is routed to service authoring.

gaps.title: Prep-for-AI authoring in Desktop limited to Import/DirectQuery/Composite
gaps.notes: CFG-PREP-MODE deterministically detects Direct Lake and routes AI data schema, verified answers and AI instructions authoring to the Fabric service. It is a non-scoring advisory with Applicable=0.
gaps.proposedCheck: Implemented as CFG-PREP-MODE; Import and DirectQuery models remain clean and dormant.

gaps.title: OLS not supported by Q&A for AAS / on-prem SSAS-hosted models
gaps.notes: SEC-OLS-AWARENESS now detects OLS from model metadata, but no host-type signal distinguishes AAS / on-prem SSAS from the Power BI service. The unsupported hosted combination therefore remains uncheckable until live connection metadata supplies that boundary.
gaps.proposedCheck: Deterministic predicate (requirement is deterministic:true): combine (a) OLS-present detection from Model.Roles TablePermission/ColumnPermission MetadataPermission==None, with (b) host detection from the live connection (server type / ServerMode — AAS or on-prem SSAS vs Power BI Premium/Fabric). Violate when OLS is present AND host is AAS/SSAS. Category Security, Severity High/recommended. FixKind.None/Proposal — it is unsupported behavior to warn about, not auto-remediate; message advises moving the model to the service or removing OLS reliance. Host detection requires the live-connection foundation (server properties), so this is a Wave-2 live rule; on a pure offline TMDL scan it returns Applicable=0.

gaps.title: Copilot cannot validate RLS in Test-as-role
gaps.notes: Semanticus has no RLS-validation / View-as-role workflow at all, and no Security category. This is a workflow constraint (Copilot/Q&A/Quick Insights are unavailable inside Test-as-role), not a model-metadata defect, so there is nothing in the deterministic ruleset that could or should encode it as a per-object finding.
gaps.proposedCheck: Not a scored rule (requirement deterministic:false, best-practice). Best handled as documentation/guidance attached to any future RLS-validation feature: if/when Semanticus adds a 'View as / Test as role' helper, surface a static note that Copilot cannot be used to verify role filters inside that experience. No predicate over the model, no MCP fix; if represented at all it is an advisory annotation in the Security category, not a coverage-scored finding.

gaps.title: Hidden/private fields and tables are excluded from Copilot grounding
gaps.notes: Our Visibility category contains only VIS-FK (line 189), which HIDES foreign-key columns to remove noise — that correctly leverages 'hidden = excluded from grounding' for FKs. But the converse half of the requirement ('anything you want answered must be visible/non-private') is unenforced: we have no rule that detects a business-relevant measure/table that is hidden and therefore silently invisible to Copilot, and no 'table marked private' concept. Many description/synonym rules already pre-filter on !IsHidden, so we implicitly treat hidden as out-of-scope, but we never flag a likely-answerable field that is hidden.
gaps.proposedCheck: Hybrid. Deterministic candidate selection: a hidden measure/column whose name is business-readable (not cryptic per IsCrypticName, not a key/FK, not a helper '_'/'temp'/'aux' name) — i.e. it looks like something a user would ask about yet is excluded from grounding. Then LlmJudgment confirms it is a genuine business field vs intentional plumbing (avoids false positives on staging measures). Category Visibility, Severity Medium, FixKind.Proposal (un-hiding affects all report consumers, so it must be reviewed — per the plan's 'prefer AI-schema exclusion over IsHidden' guardrail). Fix path: there is currently NO MCP set_hidden/unhide tool — set_column_metadata(isHidden) is in the plan but not implemented in McpTools.cs — so today this is review-only; add an unhide affordance to close the loop.

gaps.title: Verified answers fail on hidden columns
gaps.notes: We do not author or read verified answers at all (DataAgentConfig is empty; no set_verified_answer MCP tool exists in McpTools.cs — only set_description, set_synonyms, enable_qna, set_measure_format, mark_date_table, set_data_category, set_relationship_crossfilter, rename_object, get_grounding, make_model_ai_ready, ai_readiness_scan are wired). So there is no verified-answer artifact whose field references we could validate against visibility.
gaps.proposedCheck: Deterministic predicate, enforced at VALIDATE time of a future set_verified_answer tool: parse each verified answer's referenced fields and reject/flag any that resolve to an IsHidden column (verified answers fail on hidden columns). Implementation needs (1) a verified-answers reader for the Prep-for-AI store and (2) the new set_verified_answer MCP writer — both currently absent. Category DataAgentConfig, Severity recommended. Fix path: validator blocks the verified answer or proposes either using a visible equivalent field or un-hiding (review-gated). Until the verified-answer store is read/written, this rule has Applicable=0.

gaps.title: Model bloat (excess tables/columns/measures) degrades accuracy
gaps.notes: Hard ceilings, live high-cardinality text, disconnected business tables, field-parameter complexity and duplicate measures are observable bloat signals. The row remains partial because whether a below-limit object is unused or unnecessary is a workload and business judgment.
gaps.proposedCheck: The deterministic candidate signals are implemented. Full unused-object classification stays a judgment non-goal because metadata cannot prove workload usage or business value, and the analyzer must never recommend deletion from a count heuristic.

gaps.title: High-cardinality columns and inefficient DAX flagged as model problems
gaps.notes: SCALE-HICARD-COLUMN deterministically flags measured visible text columns above its high-cardinality threshold and stays dormant without live evidence. Four token-path DAX rules surface representative inefficient or unreliable DAX patterns without matching comments, strings or names.
gaps.proposedCheck: Implemented. Coverage means the two named problem families are represented by deterministic findings; it does not claim exhaustive workload tuning or substitute for the separate BPA and Memory Analyzer workflows.

gaps.title: Duplicate/overlapping measures create ambiguity
gaps.notes: NAME-DUP (line 147) detects EXACT duplicate visible field NAMES across tables (string equality, OrdinalIgnoreCase) — a syntactic collision. It does NOT detect SEMANTIC overlap: measures with different names computing the same metric (Total Sales vs Sales Amount vs Revenue). That is the gap the requirement calls out, and it explicitly says to complement NAME-DUP with a measure-semantic-overlap check.
gaps.proposedCheck: AI-content / LlmJudgment (requirement deterministic:false). Deterministic pre-filter clusters candidate measures by signal (similar DAX expression skeletons, shared base columns/dependency overlap, fuzzy name similarity on a synonym set like sales/revenue/turnover), then the user's Claude judges which clusters are genuine duplicates vs legitimately distinct metrics. Category Measures (new) or Naming, Severity recommended. Fix path: review-gated Proposal — consolidate or differentiate via clearer names (rename_object), disambiguating descriptions/synonyms (set_description/set_synonyms), and/or exclude the duplicate from the AI data schema (future set_ai_data_schema). Cache the LLM verdict by (measure refs + expression hash) per the plan's anti-cost guidance.

gaps.title: Helper/intermediate measures should be excluded from AI scope
gaps.notes: No rule detects helper/intermediate measures. Our only Visibility rule is VIS-FK (foreign-key columns), and SafeFixes only hides FK columns + sets SummarizeBy=None + geo categories. The plan (ai-readiness-plan.md section E, 'Helper/intermediate measure visible') describes this exact rule but it was never implemented. Nothing flags a visible measure that is referenced only by other measures. Note DESC-MEASURE/NAME-MEASURE/SYN-FIELD/FMT-MEASURE all skip x.IsHidden measures, so a helper that IS hidden is already out of grounding — the gap is the EXPOSED helper.
gaps.proposedCheck: AI-content/judgment (LlmJudgment) gated by a deterministic pre-filter. Deterministic candidate signal: visible measure whose name matches /^_|_base$|_helper$|temp|aux/ OR (live) is referenced only by other measures and never by a relationship/visual — needs run_dmv DISCOVER_CALC_DEPENDENCY to build the reverse-reference graph (Wave-2, since offline TOM can't see report usage). LLM confirms it is plumbing not a business metric to spare legit measures matching the name pattern. Fix path: set_column_hidden has no measure analogue today, so fix is the existing rename_object is wrong here — we'd remediate via hiding the measure (Measure.IsHidden) which is NOT yet an MCP tool; prefer plan's 'exclude from AI data schema' but that store/tool doesn't exist either. So: add a set_measure_hidden MCP tool (or fold into a generic set_hidden) as the SafeFix/Proposal target. FixKind=Proposal (hiding can break verified answers).

gaps.title: Implicit measures and wrong default summarization degrade results
gaps.notes: The visible numeric surface is partitioned deterministically: FMT-SUMMARIZE evaluates key/relationship columns, SUMMARIZE-DIMENSION evaluates canonical non-additive dimensions, and DAC-IMPLICIT-MEASURE evaluates the remaining auto-aggregating numeric fields. A column belongs to exactly one population, so the score does not double-count the same defect.
gaps.proposedRuleId: FMT-IMPLICIT (and FMT-SUMMARIZE-NUMERIC for the year/geo non-endpoint names)
gaps.proposedCheck: Implemented by DAC-IMPLICIT-MEASURE, FMT-SUMMARIZE and SUMMARIZE-DIMENSION with violation, clean and dormant tests for every population.

gaps.title: Non-star-schema / flat denormalized models hurt DAX generation
gaps.notes: The deterministic graph slice is implemented: bidirectional, many-to-many, snowflake/bridge and disconnected structures are findings. Flat/denormalized intent is not declared in model metadata, so that remainder is pinned in residualBuckets.judgment.
gaps.proposedCheck: Implemented for observable graph anti-patterns. The semantic flat/denormalized classification remains a judgment non-goal; no guessed REL-STAR rule is queued.

gaps.title: Ambiguous multiple date columns confuse Copilot
gaps.notes: DATE-AMBIGUOUS deterministically flags a visible non-date table with multiple event DateTime columns while excluding marked date tables and Start/End or Effective/Expiry validity ranges. The fix remains a reviewed Proposal because choosing the intended default date is business intent.
gaps.proposedCheck: Implemented as DATE-AMBIGUOUS with exact table-level applicability and validity-range precision guards.

gaps.title: Descriptions truncated after first 200 characters for Copilot grounding
gaps.notes: DESC-LONG covers visible measures and DESC-LONG-OBJECT covers visible tables and columns. Both deterministically flag descriptions over 200 characters, remain dormant on undocumented models, and route the front-loaded rewrite to the user's assistant through AiContent.
gaps.proposedCheck: Implemented and pinned across all three object types by ReadinessCatalogBatch4Tests.

gaps.title: Calculation items are absent from metadata; use group description
gaps.notes: DAC-CALC-GROUP flags a calculation group with no table/column description. DESC-CALCGROUP-ITEMS takes over once documentation exists and checks every item name as a whole token. The mutually exclusive populations prevent one calculation group from being counted twice.
gaps.proposedCheck: Implemented and pinned across missing, incomplete, complete and dormant states by ReadinessCatalogBatch4Tests.

gaps.title: Calculation groups degrade Copilot DAX generation
gaps.notes: DAC-CALC-GROUP deterministically detects calculation groups with no table or group-column description. Once documentation exists, DESC-CALCGROUP-ITEMS owns completeness, so the same group is not counted twice.
gaps.proposedRuleId: LIMIT-CALCGROUP (or fold into DESC-CALCGROUP as the same finding)
gaps.proposedCheck: Implemented as the mutually exclusive DAC-CALC-GROUP and DESC-CALCGROUP-ITEMS pair.

gaps.title: Model complexity (field params, disconnected tables, currency conversion) reduces reliability
gaps.notes: LIMIT-SCALE covers hard ceilings, REL-DISCONNECTED finds isolated business tables, and DAC-FIELD-PARAM-COMPLEXITY identifies field-parameter models. Currency-conversion intent, dependency-depth tolerance and an overall reliability verdict remain semantic judgment.
gaps.proposedCheck: The objective metadata signals are implemented. Do not synthesize a composite reliability score from arbitrary heuristics; the remaining currency-conversion and acceptable-complexity decisions belong to model review and interview evidence.

gaps.title: Linguistic modeling / synonyms required for good Copilot results
gaps.notes: SYN-SCHEMA (no linguistic schema at all, CL>=1465 gated) and SYN-FIELD (visible field has no synonyms, via SynonymHelper.GetSynonyms) cover the FIELD-synonym half well, and SYN-SCHEMA is even a soft-gate candidate. What's missing is the RELATIONSHIP-VERB phrasing the requirement also calls out: synonyms for verbs that describe relationships (e.g. 'Customer buys Product') so Copilot/Q&A can phrase joins. SYN-FIELD only iterates measures+columns, never relationships, and there's no verb/phrasing coverage in the LSDL.
gaps.proposedCheck: Detection deterministic over the parsed LSDL: for each active relationship, check whether the linguistic schema has a relationship phrasing/verb entry binding the two tables; violation = none. Reuse the SynonymHelper/LSDL JSON parser already in play for SYN-FIELD. Fix = AiContent: the user's Claude proposes verb phrasings; needs an extension to set_synonyms (or a new set_relationship_phrasing) to upsert relationship phrasings into the LSDL, since today set_synonyms targets a single measure/column/table only. FixKind=AiContent, Synonyms category, Severity Medium.

gaps.title: Meet 7 of 8 data-evaluation criteria, including Consistency
gaps.notes: This is the heart of the empty DataAgentConfig category: weight 0.08 (set in ReadinessAnalyzer.Weights) but ZERO rules, so in the analyzer it has HasRules=false and is dropped from the weighted mean entirely (present = categories.Where(c=>c.HasRules)). There is no representation of Microsoft's 'evaluate data' 8-criteria / 7-of-8 pass threshold, no Consistency check, and no AI-instructions / AI-data-schema / verified-answers rules. Also note: no MCP write tool exists for any of these (no set_ai_instructions, set_ai_data_schema, set_verified_answer in McpTools.cs), so even detection has no fix path today.
gaps.proposedRuleId: DAC-EVAL8 (umbrella) backed by DAC-INSTRUCTIONS, DAC-DATASCHEMA, DAC-VERIFIED, DAC-CONSISTENCY
gaps.proposedCheck: What DataAgentConfig SHOULD contain: (1) DAC-INSTRUCTIONS — model-level AI-instructions store empty (<=10k cap) → AiContent, fix via a new set_ai_instructions tool. (2) DAC-DATASCHEMA — AI data-schema include-set unset or includes helpers/duplicates/FKs → Deterministic detect (engine computes business-metric leaves + dependency closure) + Proposal fix via set_ai_data_schema; this is also where the helper-measure exclusion (item 1) and FK exclusion land. (3) DAC-VERIFIED — zero verified answers (caps <=250/model) → AiContent via set_verified_answer. (4) DAC-CONSISTENCY — the named 8th criterion: deterministic checks that the same concept is modeled consistently (e.g. duplicate/competing measures for the same metric, inconsistent format strings or summarization for like-named fields). Then DAC-EVAL8 mirrors the 7-of-8 pass threshold: map our criteria to MS's 8 'evaluate data' points and require >=7 passing, surfacing which point fails — a deterministic threshold the A-F banding can echo (e.g. <7 caps the Copilot/data-agent profile). IMPORTANT caveat already in PLAN.md/README: the AI-instructions/verified-answer/data-schema STORAGE is undocumented (Wave-2), so until reverse-engineered these ship as detect + generate-ready-to-paste, not auto-write. Until rules exist here, the 0.08 weight is effectively dead and the category should either get rules or have its weight redistributed.

gaps.title: Missing values cause Copilot to fabricate / hallucinate
gaps.notes: Still open after completion batch 6. This is a live data-quality signal about NULL/blank values, not model metadata. The readiness-only lane cannot supply it; it requires a new live Engine query and per-column null/blank statistics.
gaps.proposedCheck: DMV/live rule (RuleKind.Dmv, FixKind.Proposal). For each visible column in the AI-schema scope, run a live DAX probe: COUNTROWS(FILTER(ISBLANK(col))) / COUNTROWS(table); flag columns whose null-fraction exceeds a threshold (e.g. >5%). Deterministic predicate once stats are fetched, but degrade to N==0/not-evaluated when offline (same graceful-degrade contract the plan specifies for dmv rules). Fix is advisory/Proposal only — Semanticus does not edit data; surface 'remove/handle missing rows upstream' guidance. No MCP write tool fixes this (it is an ETL fix, not a model-metadata fix); could optionally draft an AI-instructions caveat once set_ai_instructions exists.

gaps.title: Data Agent ignores agent-level instructions for DAX; use Prep for AI
gaps.notes: DataAgentConfig has weight 0.08 but ZERO rules, so it is dropped from the overall average (analyzer only includes categories where HasRules). There is no model-level AI-instructions store read or written anywhere — McpTools.cs has no set_ai_instructions tool. The plan (section H, line 104) explicitly captures this MS truth ('the DAX tool ignores agent-level instructions; automation must target the model/Prep-for-AI layer') but it was deferred to Wave 2 and never implemented. This is the core hard-gate the DataAgentConfig category should enforce.
gaps.proposedCheck: Model-level rule (ModelRule). Detection: read the Prep-for-AI / LSDL AI-instructions store off the default culture; deterministic predicate = store empty or only the Semanticus-managed delimiter present. Severity High. FixKind AiContent (RuleKind.LlmGenerate) — the user's Claude drafts grouped sections (Terminology, Date logic, Default groupings, ≤10k chars). Requires a NEW MCP tool set_ai_instructions(culture, instructions, append) plus a reverse-engineered storage adapter (round-trip verified) — neither exists today. Until the storage adapter lands, ship as generate-ready-to-paste (FixKind.Proposal).

gaps.title: AI data schema should scope to relevant tables/columns/measures
gaps.notes: No AI-data-schema concept exists in the code. VIS-FK hides FK columns and the plan mentions 'prefer AI-schema exclusion over IsHidden', but there is no rule that reads or builds an include-set, and no set_ai_data_schema MCP tool. Plan section H line 98 specifies it ('engine computes business-metric leaves + dependency closure') but it is unimplemented. Core DataAgentConfig rule that is entirely absent.
gaps.proposedCheck: ModelRule. Detection: AI-data-schema include-set is unset, OR it includes hidden/helper/duplicate objects, OR a selected measure's dependency closure (DISCOVER_CALC_DEPENDENCY) is not fully included. Mostly deterministic predicate over the include-set + dependency graph; LLM (FixKind.AiContent) only adjudicates ambiguous picks ('which sales measure'). Fix = engine computes business-metric leaves + dependency closure via a NEW set_ai_data_schema(culture, include[], autoCloseDependencies) MCP tool. Requires the same Prep-for-AI storage adapter as DAC-AI-INSTRUCTIONS; generate-only until reverse-engineered. Severity High.

gaps.title: Verified answers: 5-7 trigger questions and up to 3 filters
gaps.notes: This requirement's category in the catalog is VerifiedAnswers, which does not even exist in our ReadinessCategory enum (Naming, Descriptions, Synonyms, Relationships, Visibility, Formatting, CopilotLimits, DataAgentConfig). No verified-answers store is read/written; no set_verified_answer MCP tool. Plan line 99 specifies the thresholds (≤250/model, 5-7 triggers, ≤500 char each, ≤3 filters) but nothing is built.
gaps.proposedCheck: Add the thresholds as DETERMINISTIC structural checks once a verified-answers store can be read: per answer require 5-7 trigger questions and ≤3 filters, ≤500 chars each, ≤250/model; also flag verified answers referencing a renamed table/column/measure (re-save needed). Deterministic predicate for the count/length checks; FixKind.AiContent (LLM expands top questions into formal+conversational trigger phrasings + visual spec, validating field visibility and not-a-report-measure). Needs a NEW set_verified_answer MCP tool + storage adapter. House under DataAgentConfig (rename the catalog's VerifiedAnswers into our DataAgentConfig category, or add a VerifiedAnswers category) since neither exists in code.

gaps.title: AI instructions must avoid conflicting/overly complex guidance
gaps.notes: Depends on the AI-instructions store, which we neither read nor write. No rule inspects instruction content for length or conflicts. DESC-LONG enforces a 200-char cap but only on measure DESCRIPTIONS, not AI instructions — different store, different cap (10k). So this is missing, not partial-via-DESC-LONG.
gaps.proposedCheck: Two-part rule on the AI-instructions blob. Deterministic part: flag overlong instructions (approaching the 10k cap) — pure length predicate. AI-content/judgment part (RuleKind.LlmJudgment): the user's Claude detects instructions that conflict with each other or with verified answers, and over-complex multi-clause guidance that adds latency. Fix = FixKind.AiContent rewrite into focused, specific, non-conflicting lines via set_ai_instructions(append:false on the managed section). Severity Medium. Requires the Prep-for-AI read adapter + DAC-VERIFIED-ANSWERS read to cross-check conflicts.

gaps.title: Report visuals need descriptive titles for grounding
gaps.notes: Entirely out of current scope: we operate on the semantic model (TOM/TMDL) only; report visuals live in the report layer (.pbir/.pbix) which TOM cannot see — the rename tool description in McpTools.cs even warns 'report-layer bindings outside the model can't be updated'. No rule reads visual metadata. Catalog category VerifiedAnswers does not exist in our enum.
gaps.proposedCheck: ReportRule that parses report-layer JSON (PBIR/visual.json) for visuals — including hidden visuals/pages — lacking a descriptive title (deterministic: title null/blank/auto-generated like 'Clustered column chart'). FixKind.Proposal/AiContent advisory: LLM suggests a grounded title from the visual's bound tables/columns/measures. NO existing MCP tool writes the report layer; would need a new report-layer reader at minimum (write is risky/out of model scope). Likely advisory-only. Lowest priority of the DataAgentConfig set because it needs a whole new (report) data source.

gaps.title: Mark model 'Approved for Copilot' to remove friction treatment
gaps.notes: Catalog category is Visibility, but our Visibility category contains only VIS-FK (hide foreign-key columns) — nothing about the service-side 'Approved for Copilot' flag. The plan (line 217) explicitly notes 'Approved for Copilot is a service-side flag' that must be confirmed at runtime via Fabric REST, not a TOM annotation. We have no Fabric REST integration and no MCP tool to read/set it. Missing.
gaps.proposedCheck: Governance rule (ModelRule, Severity Medium, FixKind.Proposal). Detection is deterministic IF reachable: query Fabric REST for the item's Approved-for-Copilot / prepped-for-AI status; flag when the model is otherwise AI-ready (good grade) but not approved. Degrade to not-evaluated when the Fabric API is unreachable (offline/PBIP). Fix is a governance action (set the flag via Fabric REST or guide the user) — there is no model-metadata MCP tool today; needs a new fabric_approve_for_copilot tool. Pair with the Q&A gate below (approval path requires Q&A enabled).

gaps.title: Prep for AI requires Q&A enabled (preview gate)
gaps.notes: DAC-QNA-DISABLED is scoped to DataAgentConfig and evaluates the actual qnaEnabled switch when the PBIP source is readable. It reports the Prep-for-AI prerequisite directly and stays dormant when the switch cannot be observed.
gaps.proposedCheck: Implemented as a DataAgentConfig rule over the actual qnaEnabled switch. The rule activates only when that switch is readable; no cross-consumer overall-grade cap is added.

gaps.title: Copilot cannot do forecasting, anomaly detection, or key influencers
gaps.notes: Our only CopilotLimits rule is LIMIT-SCALE (table/column/measure/relationship/name-length ceilings → hard gate capped at D). It says nothing about analytical capability limits. This requirement is explicitly NOT a model-authoring fix ('useful to scope what readiness can promise … not a model-authoring fix') — so it is informational, not a violation we remediate. It is genuinely missing as content but arguably belongs as static expectation-setting copy rather than a scored rule.
gaps.proposedCheck: Advisory-only rule (RuleKind.Deterministic, FixKind.None, Severity Info) in CopilotLimits that ALWAYS emits one informational finding listing the unsupported question types (forecasting, anomaly detection, key influencers). Deterministic (no detection logic — it is constant guidance). No MCP fix tool; it never lowers the score (Applicable could be 0 so it doesn't dilute), purely to set user expectations in the dashboard. Lowest value; could instead live as static UI text rather than a rule.

gaps.title: English-only for Copilot data questions
gaps.notes: Catalog category Naming. Our Naming rules (NAME-MEASURE, NAME-COLUMN cryptic-name; NAME-DUP duplicate names) check readability/uniqueness but never language. The cryptic-name regex is language-agnostic. Nothing flags non-English names/synonyms, and our generation prompts (plan line 165 'business-friendly English names') aim for English but there is no DETECTION rule. Missing as a rule.
gaps.proposedCheck: Detection is inherently non-deterministic (language ID) → RuleKind.LlmJudgment, FixKind.AiContent, Severity Info/Medium. Cheap pre-filter: non-ASCII letters or known non-English tokens in visible names/synonyms; the user's Claude confirms the name/synonym is non-English. Fix = rename_object (for names) or set_synonyms (add English synonyms so users' English questions match) — both MCP tools already exist, so once detection is added the fix path is fully wired. House in Naming.

gaps.title: Live-connect from Pro/PPU still needs F64 Copilot workspace
gaps.notes: Catalog category Scale, which does not exist in our enum (closest is CopilotLimits). LIMIT-SCALE only checks object-count ceilings, not capacity/SKU. We have a live connection (connect_xmla/connect_local/connection_status) but read no workspace SKU/capacity info, so we cannot detect F64. This is a capacity/environment nuance, not a model-metadata fix. Missing.
gaps.proposedCheck: Advisory rule (FixKind.None, Severity Recommended/Medium) in CopilotLimits. Deterministic IF capacity metadata is reachable via Fabric REST (workspace SKU == F64+); otherwise not-evaluated/advisory. Because the F64 requirement is on the CONSUMING Desktop/workspace and independent of the model, this can never be auto-fixed by a model edit — surface as guidance ('configure Desktop to consume Copilot from an F64 workspace'). Needs Fabric REST capacity lookup we don't have; no MCP fix tool applicable.

gaps.title: Live-connection hides measure DAX and hidden/private objects from Copilot
gaps.notes: PARTIAL via the SPIRIT of our description/synonym rules. The MS point is: over a live connection Copilot can't see measure DAX or hidden objects, so the metadata (descriptions, synonyms, names) is the ONLY grounding — which is exactly what DESC-MEASURE/TABLE/COLUMN, SYN-FIELD, NAME-* already push toward (DESC-MEASURE message literally says 'Copilot/Q&A ground on these'). But there is no rule that DETECTS the live-connection condition or specifically warns that hidden-but-referenced objects / DAX-only logic are invisible. VIS-FK hides FKs (good), but no rule flags business logic that exists only in hidden helper measures' DAX with no description. So the remediation intent is covered; the live-connection-specific detection is not.
gaps.proposedCheck: ModelRule, Severity Medium. Deterministic detection: a HIDDEN measure that is referenced by visible measures (DISCOVER_CALC_DEPENDENCY) and carries meaningful logic but has no description, OR a visible measure whose meaning lives only in DAX it depends on — since live-connected Copilot sees neither the DAX nor the hidden object. Fix = FixKind.AiContent: the user's Claude writes a description on the VISIBLE measure capturing the hidden logic (set_description), or excludes from AI schema. Complements DESC-MEASURE rather than duplicating it. Could also drive a connection-aware advisory banner when connection_status reports a live (shared-model) connection.

gaps.title: Comments and quoted strings in DAX are not used by Copilot
gaps.notes: DESC-MEASURE (Descriptions, High, LlmGenerate, AiContent) fails any visible measure with no Description, which addresses the positive half of the guidance ('use the Description property'). But nothing detects the failure mode the requirement actually warns about: a measure that HAS business meaning encoded in DAX comments (// or /* */) or in a double-quoted literal yet has an empty or thin Description — i.e. the author is relying on DAX comments that Copilot ignores. DESC-ECHO catches name-echo/too-short but not 'meaning lives only in the DAX comment'. There is no parse of measure.Expression for comments or string literals anywhere in ReadinessRules.cs.
gaps.proposedCheck: Deterministic-detect + AiContent-fix. Scope AllMeasures where !IsHidden. Applies when the measure's Expression contains a non-trivial DAX comment (regex over //... and /* ... */, stripped of trivial tokens) OR a meaningful double-quoted literal, AND (Description is empty OR materially shorter/less informative than the comment text, e.g. comment length > Description length). Violation message: 'business meaning lives in a DAX comment/string literal Copilot ignores — move it to the Description.' Fix path: get_grounding (it already returns Expression) -> the user's Claude reads the comment as grounding and authors a Description -> set_description. Best-practice severity (Medium). This is the partial-to-full upgrade: reuse the existing AiContent description pipeline, just change the trigger to 'has hidden meaning in DAX'.

gaps.title: Capacity scale-up takes up to 24 hours to enable Copilot
gaps.notes: Pure tenant/capacity-state freshness concern; nothing in the model metadata expresses capacity tier or a recent scale-up, and Semanticus scores model metadata only. LIMIT-SCALE handles the opposite case (model too big -> Copilot disabled) but says nothing about capacity-side propagation delay. No rule, no advisory.
gaps.proposedCheck: Not a deterministic model-metadata predicate and not AI-content — implement as an advisory/Proposal (FixKind.None) note surfaced in the scorecard, not a scored rule (so it never moves the grade). It would fire only when capacity context is known (e.g. a service-side annotation or user-supplied 'capacity changed at <ts>' input) and the change is < 24h old, emitting 'Copilot may not yet reflect the recent capacity scale-up (up to 24h to enable).' No MCP fix tool — purely informational; if anything it belongs to a future readiness-state/freshness banner alongside the LIMIT-SCALE gating banner rather than the rule engine. Category Scale per the catalog, but our enum has no Scale category, so it would sit under CopilotLimits as advisory.

gaps.title: Measures must be in a valid (error-free) state
gaps.notes: No rule inspects measure validity/error state. ReadinessRules.cs never reads any error/state property, and the live connection (run_dax / verify_dax_equivalence) that COULD prove a measure errors is not wired into any readiness rule. This is the highest-value gap because it is deterministic, hard-failing for Copilot, and we already have the live plumbing to detect it.
gaps.proposedCheck: Deterministic predicate, Critical, category Measures (catalog) -> map to CopilotLimits or a new Measures category. Offline path: there is no reliable TOM 'IsError' for measures, so detect via the live connection — for each visible measure issue a probe EVALUATE { <measure ref> } (or ROW("v", [Measure])) through the existing run_dax channel and flag measures whose evaluation returns an error. Applicable population = visible measures (when connected); degrade to Applicable=0 when offline so it never skews the score (matches the dmv graceful-degrade pattern in the plan). Fix path: there is NO safe auto-fix — surface as Proposal/None with bpa_get_fix_prompt-style guidance routing to update_measure so the user's Claude repairs the DAX. Should also act as a hard gate (broken measures are unusable by Copilot) capping the grade, mirroring the LIMIT-SCALE gate.

gaps.title: Copilot cannot be scoped to specific workloads/experiences
gaps.notes: Governance/exposure nuance with no model-metadata signal. Nothing in our rules or scorecard communicates that enabling Copilot exposes all experiences. This is the kind of thing the catalog files under DataAgentConfig but it is informational, not a scored deficiency.
gaps.proposedCheck: Not deterministic over metadata and not AiContent — an advisory note (FixKind.None), surfaced once in the DataAgentConfig / governance section of the scorecard, never scored. Text: 'Readiness for one experience implies exposure across all Copilot experiences in this capacity; you cannot scope Copilot per workload.' No MCP fix tool. It belongs in the DataAgentConfig category as a non-scoring informational item so the user understands the blast radius of the other DataAgentConfig fixes.

gaps.title: Configure AI data schema in Prep for AI (scope the model to the relevant subset)
gaps.notes: DataAgentConfig category exists with weight 0.08 but contains ZERO rules, so it is excluded from scoring (HasRules=false drops it from the weighted mean). No AI data schema is read or written. README/PLAN explicitly defer Prep-for-AI storage as undocumented (Wave 2). This is exactly the kind of rule DataAgentConfig is supposed to hold.
gaps.proposedCheck: Deterministic-detect (presence) + Proposal-fix, DataAgentConfig. Detect: model is Q&A/Copilot-enabled (a linguistic culture exists, reusing the SYN-SCHEMA culture probe) but no AI data schema is configured in the Prep-for-AI store. Because the Prep-for-AI storage keys are undocumented (per PLAN section 8), v1 ships as detect + generate-ready-to-paste rather than auto-write: flag the model, and have the engine compute the candidate business-metric leaf set + dependency closure while the user's Claude adjudicates ambiguous fields. A second sub-rule (DAC-AI-SCHEMA-NOISE) flags noisy/ambiguous/high-cardinality columns that should be EXCLUDED. Fix path: a future set_ai_data_schema(culture, include[], autoCloseDependencies) MCP tool (does not yet exist); until then, Proposal-only output. This is the primary thing DataAgentConfig should contain.

gaps.title: Include dependent objects when scoping the AI data schema
gaps.notes: Deterministic and fully computable from the TOM dependency graph, but no rule exists and there is no AI-schema include-set to validate against. DataAgentConfig has no rules. This is the most tractable DataAgentConfig rule because it is a pure closure check once a schema include-set exists.
gaps.proposedCheck: Deterministic predicate, DataAgentConfig, recommended. For each measure marked included in the AI data schema, compute its dependency closure (referenced measures and their underlying columns) and flag any dependency not also included. Detection: parse measure DAX / use DISCOVER_CALC_DEPENDENCY (live) to build the closure; compare against the include-set. Applicable = included measures (0 until an AI schema exists, so it self-excludes from scoring until DAC-AI-SCHEMA-MISSING is resolved). Fix path: set_ai_data_schema with autoCloseDependencies=true (proposed tool) auto-adds the missing dependents; until that tool exists, emit the exact missing-object list as a Proposal.

gaps.title: Configure verified answers for common / important questions
gaps.notes: DAC-VERIFIED-ANSWERS uses PrepForAiReader to count file-based verified-answer definitions beside the model. It stays dormant when the storage capability is unavailable and surfaces a Proposal when Q&A is enabled but no verified answer exists.
gaps.proposedCheck: Implemented as DAC-VERIFIED-ANSWERS in DataAgentConfig. Entry-level hidden-field, filter and trigger validation remains in the partial-schema residual bucket.

gaps.title: Verified answers must not reference hidden fields
gaps.notes: Hard-gate per the catalog, fully deterministic, but no verified-answer objects exist in our model representation to validate, and no VerifiedAnswers category exists. VIS-FK hides FK columns (which could itself BREAK a verified answer referencing that column — a cross-rule hazard worth noting), but nothing checks the inverse.
gaps.proposedCheck: Deterministic predicate, hard-gate (Critical), VerifiedAnswers category. For each verified answer, resolve every referenced column/measure/filter field and flag any with IsHidden=true. Requires the verified-answer store to be parsed first (Wave 2). Fix path: unhide the field (the existing set_column_metadata/IsHidden path, or a generic visibility setter) OR adjust the verified answer — Proposal, because unhiding has report-wide effects. Importantly this rule must coordinate with VIS-FK so the safe-fix that hides FKs does not silently break a verified answer that references one.

gaps.title: Verified answer trigger-phrase count and limits
gaps.notes: Deterministic count/limit checks, but no verified-answer objects and no VerifiedAnswers category. Nothing in the engine enforces the 5-7 trigger guidance or the 15/500-char/3-filter/250-per-model hard caps.
gaps.proposedCheck: Deterministic predicate, VerifiedAnswers. Per verified answer: flag <5 trigger phrases (recommended) and HARD-fail >15 triggers, any trigger >500 chars, >3 filters / >10 filter permutations; per model HARD-fail >250 verified answers. Pure arithmetic over the verified-answer store once parsed. Fix path: the user's Claude adds/trims trigger phrases via the future set_verified_answer tool; over-limit cases are Proposal (need human pruning). Requires Wave-2 verified-answer storage.

gaps.title: Verified answers must not use report-level measures or unsupported visuals
gaps.notes: Hard-gate, deterministic, but inherently report-layer: report-level measures and visual types live in the .pbix/PBIR report, which Semanticus' TOM view does not see (the plan repeatedly notes report-layer bindings are invisible to TOM). So this is missing AND only partially detectable from the model.
gaps.proposedCheck: Deterministic predicate where report metadata is reachable, otherwise advisory. For each verified answer, flag if it is built on a report-level measure or an unsupported visual type (textbox, Q&A, AI visuals, key influencers, narrative/smart narrative, decomposition tree, forecasting, custom visuals). Detection needs the report layer (PBIR/Fabric REST), which the engine currently cannot read from TOM — so where the report is unavailable, downgrade to a Proposal/advisory ('verify the source visual is supported'). Fix path: rebuild the verified answer on a supported visual with model (not report) measures — Proposal, no auto-fix. VerifiedAnswers category, hard-gate.

gaps.title: Re-save verified answers after renaming referenced objects
gaps.notes: We HAVE the rename engine: rename_object (McpTools) -> LocalEngine.RenameObjectAsync does TOM AutoFixup that rewrites all DAX references, and the change-plan Plan.cs models a 'rename' op with Risk='rename'. But that fixup covers in-model DAX only; it has NO awareness of verified answers, and there is no verified-answer store to scan. The requirement's specific hook — on rename, detect verified answers referencing the renamed object and prompt to re-save — does not exist. So the rename mechanism is present (partial) but the verified-answer coupling is missing.
gaps.proposedCheck: Deterministic predicate wired into the change-plan/rename path, recommended. When RenameObjectAsync (or the plan engine's 'rename' op) touches a table/column/measure, scan the verified-answer store for any answer referencing the old name and surface a 'these verified answers must be re-saved' warning in the rename result/preview. Detection is a string/ref match over the parsed verified-answer definitions. Fix path: re-save each affected verified answer via the future set_verified_answer tool (the docs say the platform requires an explicit re-save; AutoFixup does not propagate into verified answers). This is the concrete extension of our existing rename_object to close the loop; blocked only on the Wave-2 verified-answer store.

gaps.title: Add AI instructions in Prep for AI (not at data-agent level for semantic models)
gaps.notes: DAC-AI-INSTRUCTIONS reads Prep-for-AI CustomInstructions from the model's LSDL, becomes applicable only with a readable legacy linguistic schema, and flags an empty block. The AiContent fix is authored by the user's assistant and applied through set_ai_instructions; no engine inference is involved.
gaps.proposedCheck: Implemented as DAC-AI-INSTRUCTIONS over LSDL CustomInstructions. It stays dormant without a readable legacy linguistic schema, stands down for the unparsed Copilot Tooling Format, and routes drafting through set_ai_instructions with FixKind.AiContent.

gaps.title: Keep AI instructions focused; avoid conflicting/complex instructions
gaps.proposedCheck: AI-content / LlmJudgment. There is NO rule, and DataAgentConfig has weight 0.08 but zero rules (catEvals.Count is always 0, so the category is excluded from the overall average via HasRules=false). Detection requires reading the Prep-for-AI AI-instructions blob (model/LSDL metadata, undocumented storage — Wave 2 in PLAN.md/README). Deterministic pre-filter: instructions length > ~10,000-char cap, empty, or >N bullet sections; then an LLM-judgment verdict {pass,reason} flags contradictory/overlapping/overly-broad instruction sets that conflict with verified answers. Fix path: a future set_ai_instructions tool drafts a focused, delimited managed section. No such MCP tool exists today (only set_description/set_synonyms/enable_qna/etc. exist), so this is generate-ready-to-paste until the Prep-for-AI write adapter lands.
gaps.notes: Completely uncovered. Belongs in the empty DataAgentConfig category. Cannot be detected or fixed without the undocumented Prep-for-AI instructions store, which Semanticus deliberately defers to Wave 2. This is the canonical 'DataAgentConfig should contain this' item: an LLM-judgment rule over the AI-instructions blob.

gaps.title: Enable Power BI Q&A as a prerequisite for all Prep-for-AI features
gaps.proposedCheck: Implemented as DAC-QNA-DISABLED. An observed false switch docks DataAgentConfig and explains the Prep-for-AI prerequisite; an unknown switch remains dormant, with no combined-grade cap.
gaps.notes: DAC-QNA-DISABLED detects the real file-backed Q&A switch in DataAgentConfig and reports that all Prep-for-AI features depend on it. No combined-grade cap is added: Q&A being disabled blocks Prep-for-AI but does not block every AI consumer, so the violation affects its applicable category rather than redefining every scorecard.

gaps.title: Mark the model 'Approved for Copilot'
gaps.proposedCheck: Deterministic detection in principle, but the signal is a SERVICE-SIDE flag, not a TOM property. No rule exists. The plan (sec 5) explicitly notes 'Approved for Copilot' must be confirmed at runtime via the Fabric/Power BI service, not assumed as a TOM annotation. Proposed VIS-APPROVED-COPILOT (Visibility category per the catalog) would, when a live service connection is available, query the model's 'Approved for Copilot' setting and flag a Q&A-enabled, otherwise-prepped model that isn't approved. Fix path: there is NO MCP tool for this today and TOM cannot set it; remediation is advisory (FixKind.None / link to service settings) or a future Fabric-REST adapter. Degrades to not-evaluated when offline (matches the DMV graceful-degrade pattern in the plan).
gaps.notes: Uncovered. Not detectable from TOM/TMDL at all — it lives in the Power BI service. Our engine is currently offline-metadata-first and has no Fabric-REST path, so this can only be an advisory rule gated on a live service connection. No rename_object/set_* tool applies; remediation is a human action in service settings.

gaps.title: Use a star schema; avoid flat/denormalized/pivoted tables
gaps.proposedCheck: Implemented for observable graph anti-patterns. Wide/pivoted interpretation remains a judgment non-goal and structural remediation stays Proposal-only.
gaps.notes: The deterministic graph slice is implemented by REL-BIDI, REL-M2M, REL-SNOWFLAKE and REL-DISCONNECTED. Whether a wide table should be remodeled is subjective and structural, so the row remains partial and is pinned in residualBuckets.judgment.

gaps.title: Create explicit measures and set correct default summarization; avoid implicit measures
gaps.proposedCheck: Implemented as three disjoint deterministic populations: additive implicit numerics, keys/relationship endpoints, and canonical non-additive numeric dimensions. Violation, clean and dormant cases pin each population.
gaps.notes: DAC-IMPLICIT-MEASURE detects visible additive numerics that still rely on implicit aggregation; FMT-SUMMARIZE owns keys and relationship endpoints; SUMMARIZE-DIMENSION owns canonical non-additive numeric dimensions. Their populations are partitioned so each applicable column is evaluated for the right remediation without double-counting.

gaps.title: Disambiguate multiple date fields with verified answers / AI instructions
gaps.proposedCheck: Deterministic detection + Ai-content/Proposal fix. DATE-MARK only checks that SOME table is marked DataCategory='Time'; it says nothing about multiple competing date columns (Order/Ship/Due Date, calendar vs fiscal). Proposed REL-DATE-AMBIGUITY: deterministic scope = count visible DateTime columns / multiple relationships into the date table (inactive relationships are a strong signal of role-playing dates); flag when >1 and no disambiguating guidance exists. Fix: the user's Claude adds an AI-instruction line ('default to Order Date') and/or a verified answer — but those writers (set_ai_instructions/set_verified_answer) DO NOT EXIST yet (Prep-for-AI Wave 2). Interim fix path within current tooling: set_synonyms / set_description on the date columns to clarify intent, plus mark_date_table for the primary calendar. Cross-listed as a DataAgentConfig concern because the real fix lives in the AI-instructions store.
gaps.notes: Partial. DATE-MARK covers a different, adjacent thing (existence of a marked date table) and complements but doesn't satisfy this. The ambiguity detection is missing, and the documented remediation (verified answers / AI instructions) depends on the un-built Prep-for-AI writers — so today only a weaker description/synonym workaround is available via existing MCP tools.

gaps.title: Add descriptions to objects included in the AI data schema
gaps.proposedCheck: AI-content generation; refinement of existing rules' SCOPE. DESC-MEASURE / DESC-TABLE / DESC-COLUMN already flag missing descriptions (scope = not IsHidden) and the user's Claude writes them via set_description — strong coverage. The gap vs the requirement is precision: it asks to prioritize objects actually IN the AI data schema (the set the DAX tool sees), which is a tighter set than 'all visible'. We have no concept of the AI-data-schema include-set (that's the unbuilt DAC layer). Proposed DESC-INSCHEMA-PRIORITY: once an AI-data-schema include-set exists, up-weight/Critical-ize missing descriptions on in-schema objects and down-weight purely-hidden/excluded ones. Detection = intersect DESC-* violations with the include-set; fix unchanged (set_description). Until the include-set exists, 'not IsHidden' is our best proxy.
gaps.notes: Largely covered for the generic case (DESC-MEASURE High + DESC-TABLE/DESC-COLUMN, all wired to set_description). Partial only because we can't yet scope to the AI data schema specifically — visibility is a proxy. The refinement depends on the missing AI-data-schema include-set in DataAgentConfig.

gaps.title: Optimize model performance / remove bloat before AI use
gaps.ourRuleId: LIMIT-SCALE, SCALE-HICARD-COLUMN, REL-DISCONNECTED, DAC-FIELD-PARAM-COMPLEXITY, MEAS-DUP-EXPR, FMT-SUMMARIZE, BP-DAX-IFERROR, BP-DAX-SUMMARIZE-EXT, BP-DAX-BARE-TABLE-FILTER, BP-DAX-VAR-ALIAS
gaps.proposedCheck: The readiness scorecard now combines hard ceilings, live high-cardinality text, disconnected and field-parameter complexity, duplicate measures, incorrect summarization and four deterministic DAX anti-patterns. It remains partial because proving an object is unused or a model is fully optimized needs workload evidence and business judgment.
gaps.notes: Batch 6 moves this row from missing to partial. Live cardinality remains dormant without measurements, and no deletion or full-optimization claim is inferred from model metadata.

gaps.title: Limit data-agent table scope to 25 or fewer tables per source
gaps.proposedCheck: Deterministic, data-agent-specific. Distinct from LIMIT-SCALE's 500-table Copilot ceiling — this is a 25-table-per-source data-agent recommendation. No rule exists; this is core to the empty DataAgentConfig category. Detection needs the data-agent's selected-table count per source, which is a Fabric data-agent-item config NOT present in the semantic-model TOM. Proposed DAC-TABLE-SCOPE-25: if a data-agent source selection is reachable (Fabric REST/Git), flag selected-tables > 25; absent that integration, a proxy is to flag a semantic model whose visible/in-AI-schema table count exceeds 25 as 'too broad for a single data-agent source.' Fix: Proposal/advisory (narrow the selection or split sources) — no MCP tool sets data-agent selection today. Deterministic count comparison once the source list is available.
gaps.notes: Out-of-band non-goal for the model analyzer. The exact selected-table count lives in the deployed Fabric data-agent source, not semantic-model metadata. LIMIT-DATAAGENT-TABLES remains an explicitly non-scoring proxy for a broad visible model, but the catalog no longer calls that proxy full coverage of per-source selection.

gaps.title: A data agent supports at most five data sources
gaps.proposedCheck: Deterministic hard-gate, data-agent-config. No rule exists. This is purely a property of the Fabric data-agent item (count of attached lakehouses/warehouses/KQL/semantic-models/ontologies/Graph), which Semanticus does not currently model — Semanticus edits ONE semantic model and has no representation of the data-agent's source list. Proposed DAC-MAX-FIVE-SOURCES (DataAgentConfig, Severity.Critical, FixKind.None gate): when data-agent config is reachable via Fabric REST, count sources and flag > 5; treat as a hard gate like LIMIT-SCALE (cap the data-agent profile sub-score). Fix is advisory only (remove sources). Requires a Fabric data-agent-config adapter that does not yet exist; not detectable from model metadata at all.
gaps.notes: Missing and currently undetectable. It concerns the data-agent item's source inventory, entirely outside the semantic-model TOM. Belongs in DataAgentConfig as a hard-gate, mirroring LIMIT-SCALE's gating mechanics, but needs a Fabric-REST integration Semanticus doesn't have.

gaps.title: Build specialized, narrowly-scoped domain agents
gaps.proposedCheck: AI-content / LlmJudgment recommendation, data-agent-config. No rule exists. Overlaps the table-scope and five-source rules but is a softer semantic judgment about whether the agent's scope spans unrelated domains. Proposed DAC-DOMAIN-FOCUS: deterministic signals (number of disconnected relationship sub-graphs / clusters in the model, table count, mixing of unrelated subject areas) feed an LLM-judgment verdict that the agent is too broad and should be split into domain-specific agents. Fix: Proposal/advisory only (split into focused agents) — no MCP tool applies; it's a design recommendation surfaced to the human + Claude. Deterministic clustering pre-filter (relationship connected-components) is computable offline from TOM even though the agent-scope itself isn't.
gaps.notes: Missing. A DataAgentConfig recommendation (not a hard gate). Partially computable offline via relationship-graph clustering as a proxy for 'unrelated subject areas', but the true scope judgment is LLM-driven and the remediation is purely advisory (no fix tool). Together with DAC-TABLE-SCOPE-25 and DAC-MAX-FIVE-SOURCES it defines what the weighted-but-empty DataAgentConfig category should hold.

gaps.title: Sample question/query example pairs are NOT supported for semantic models
gaps.proposedCheck: Deterministic + advisory hard-gate, no model predicate needed (it's source-type-aware messaging, not an object scan). Today DataAgentConfig is an empty category (weight 0.08, zero rules) so this guidance is nowhere. New rule sits in DataAgentConfig and fires unconditionally for our source type: Semanticus only ever operates on a Power BI semantic model (we connect via run_dax/XMLA, never SQL/KQL), so 'example query/question pairs' must NEVER be recommended. The rule's job is to actively suppress any example-query suggestion and route few-shot intent to the Prep-for-AI layer instead (verified answers / AI instructions / synonyms). Fix path is guidance-only — no MCP write; the remediation text points the user at enable_qna/set_synonyms/set_description and (future) verified-answer authoring rather than agent-level example queries.
gaps.notes: DataAgentConfig category exists with weight 0.08 but contains NO rules (confirmed: ReadinessRuleSet.Default adds nothing for it; ReadinessAnalyzer excludes empty categories via the Applicable>0 filter, so the 0.08 weight is silently redistributed). Nothing in our ruleset, McpTools, or engine references example queries at all. This is the load-bearing 'non-obvious MS truth' the ai-readiness-plan.md section H already calls out ('the data-agent DAX tool ignores agent-level instructions/example queries for semantic-model sources') but it was never implemented as a rule.

gaps.title: Define business terms, abbreviations, and synonyms at the right level
gaps.proposedCheck: AI-content rule (RuleKind.LlmGenerate/LlmJudgment) in DataAgentConfig that complements the existing SYN-FIELD/SYN-SCHEMA presence checks. Detection: heuristically flag ambiguous/domain/abbreviation tokens in visible field names and descriptions (e.g. NPS, MAU, SKU, 'fiscal' vs 'calendar', all-caps acronyms via the existing IsCrypticName acronym branch) that lack an explanatory description AND lack synonyms; the user's Claude judges which are genuinely ambiguous org/domain terms. Fix path: cross-source/agent-level term definitions belong in a future AI-instructions block (no MCP tool yet — gap), dataset-specific terms map to set_synonyms + set_description on the field. So the deterministic-presence half is covered; the 'right level' routing (agent-level vs Prep-for-AI) is the missing half.
gaps.notes: SYN-FIELD (per-field synonym presence) and SYN-SCHEMA (linguistic schema exists) cover the mechanical 'has synonyms' check and are fixed by set_synonyms/enable_qna. What's missing is the data-agent rationale and the 'place at the right level' distinction (agent-level instructions vs dataset/Prep-for-AI). We have no AI-instructions storage or set_ai_instructions MCP tool, so the agent-level half cannot be authored today.

gaps.title: Evaluate the data agent against ground-truth Q&A pairs with the Python SDK
gaps.proposedCheck: Not a per-object readiness predicate — it's a Proposal/advisory DataAgentConfig rule plus a new capability.
