Detection is deterministic+trivial (presence of a stored ground-truth Q&A set / last-evaluation annotation): fire when no ground-truth evaluation set is attached to the model. Fix path: Semanticus can't call the fabric-data-agent-sdk (that's Python/service-side and evaluate-only), so the rule is advisory and points the user to author a ground-truth question/expected_answer set. The actionable Semanticus angle is a LOCAL eval harness built on our existing live connection: run_dax + verify_dax_equivalence already evaluate candidate answers/DAX side-by-side, so a ground-truth set could be replayed through run_dax and scored. No MCP write tool fixes the model here; the 'fix' is producing/attaching the eval set.
gaps.notes: No evaluation harness, ground-truth storage, or accuracy gate exists. run_dax/pivot_measure/verify_dax_equivalence give us the execution primitives to build one, but nothing wires them to a question/expected-answer corpus today.

gaps.title: Verify and inspect generated DAX during testing
gaps.proposedCheck: The verification PRIMITIVES are fully covered by existing MCP tools (run_dax executes EVALUATE and returns columns/rows; verify_dax_equivalence proves a candidate rewrite is behavior-preserving across a SUMMARIZECOLUMNS group-by matrix; pivot_measure sanity-checks a measure by category/date). What's missing is a DataAgentConfig readiness affordance that frames this as the 'inspect the generated DAX, trace a wrong answer back to which config (model / AI data schema / verified answers / AI instructions) to adjust' loop. Proposed rule is advisory/Proposal: when the model is marked data-agent-targeted, surface a checklist that links each verification result back to the responsible config layer. No new model predicate; it reuses run_dax/verify_dax_equivalence as the fix path.
gaps.notes: We have the strongest tooling of any requirement here — run_dax and verify_dax_equivalence map almost 1:1 to 'review and verify the generated DAX.' The gap is purely that this is an ad-hoc tool capability, not surfaced as a scored DataAgentConfig readiness step that ties an incorrect result to the config layer needing adjustment.

gaps.title: RLS and CLS still apply through a data agent; only Read permission is needed
gaps.proposedCheck: Deterministic predicate, but needs a NEW category — there is no Security category in ReadinessCategory (only Naming/Descriptions/Synonyms/Relationships/Visibility/Formatting/CopilotLimits/DataAgentConfig). The TOM exposes the surface: Model.Roles, role.TablePermissions[t].FilterExpression (RLS), and Column.ObjectLevelSecurity[role]/MetadataPermission (CLS/OLS) — all reachable in our TOMWrapper build. Rule: if the model has zero Roles, or roles exist but have empty/whitespace FilterExpression on tables that look sensitive, warn that the agent enforces RLS/CLS per requesting-user credentials so they must be correctly configured. Fix path: governance/advisory (Proposal) — there is no set_rls/set_ols MCP tool today (a gap), so remediation is review-only guidance, not an auto-apply.
gaps.notes: No rule, category, or MCP tool touches Roles/RLS/CLS. Detection is feasible (TOM Roles/TablePermissions.FilterExpression/ObjectLevelSecurity confirmed in the donor TOMWrapper), so this is missing-but-buildable. Would require adding a Security category to the ReadinessCategory enum and the Weights table in ReadinessAnalyzer.

gaps.title: RLS/OLS data is not fully protected in verified answers (preview caveat)
gaps.proposedCheck: Not deterministic-clean and currently un-buildable because we have NO verified-answers storage/model at all. Proposed as an AI-content/Proposal rule in a new Security category: when a verified answer references (or its trigger/visual touches) a table or column that is under an RLS FilterExpression or has ObjectLevelSecurity, warn that during preview verified answers are not a security boundary (data can leak e.g. via the Git file format). Detection requires (a) reading verified answers — not implemented — and (b) cross-referencing RLS/OLS via Model.Roles/TablePermissions/ObjectLevelSecurity. Fix path: advisory warning; remediation is removing/scoping the verified answer (no MCP tool exists to author or edit verified answers yet).
gaps.notes: Double-missing: neither verified-answers nor RLS/OLS exist anywhere in Semanticus. Requires both a Security category AND a verified-answers layer before this rule can be written. The RLS/OLS half shares detection with SEC-RLS-CLS-CONFIGURED.

gaps.title: Data agent enforces read-only access and Microsoft Purview governance
gaps.proposedCheck: Largely informational and NOT deterministically checkable from model metadata — Purview DLP/access policies, read-only enforcement, and governance precedence (organizational > role-based > developer > user intent) are service/tenant-side, invisible to TOM. Proposed as an advisory DataAgentConfig (or new Security category) note rather than a scored predicate: surface that the agent is strictly read-only and Purview-governed, so authors should not assume responses are complete (may be truncated/blocked) and should not encode security in model content. No model predicate, no MCP fix path — it's guidance bundled into DataAgentConfig messaging.
gaps.notes: Nothing in our code references Purview, DLP, or read-only governance. This is context/expectation-setting that belongs in DataAgentConfig advisory text; it cannot be a deterministic rule because the signals live in the Fabric/Purview tenant, not the .NET TOM model we analyze.

gaps.title: Data agent responses are capped at 25 rows x 25 columns (English-only)
gaps.proposedCheck: Deterministic, but our only scale rule (LIMIT-SCALE, category CopilotLimits) checks MODEL-SIZE ceilings (>500 tables, >10000 columns, >5000 measures, >2000 relationships, >256-char names) — it says nothing about the 25x25 RESPONSE cap or English-only. Proposed DataAgentConfig rule: detect very wide raw tables with many visible columns and few/no explicit measures, and advise that agent responses are capped at 25 rows x 25 columns and may be summarized — so authors should favor measures/aggregations over wide raw-column retrieval and not promise full data dumps. Detection is deterministic (count visible columns per table vs explicit-measure coverage; reuse FMT-SUMMARIZE's implicit-measure signal). Fix path: advisory + nudge toward explicit measures (no single MCP tool; relates to update_measure/set_measure_format).
gaps.notes: LIMIT-SCALE covers Copilot model-scale gating but is a different limit class (model size vs response shape). The 25x25 response cap and English-only constraint are entirely absent. Best home is DataAgentConfig as expectation-setting messaging plus a wide-table nudge.

gaps.title: Use Git integration and deployment pipelines for data-agent and Prep-for-AI ALM
gaps.proposedCheck: Deterministic-but-contextual DataAgentConfig advisory. There is no ALM/Git/refresh awareness anywhere in Semanticus. ai-readiness-plan.md already records the load-bearing caveat (after Git/pipeline LSDL edits the service needs a model refresh to sync; Direct Lake/DQ once per day; verified answers don't support Git). Proposed rule fires whenever we APPLY Prep-for-AI/LSDL-layer changes (synonyms, AI instructions, future verified answers): emit a post-apply status that the change won't take effect until a refresh, and that verified answers are excluded from Git integration. Detection: tied to which storage layer a change touched (LSDL/Prep-for-AI vs first-class TOM), not a model scan. Fix path: status/messaging only — the 'applied' result of enable_qna/set_synonyms (and future set_ai_instructions) must say 'needs refresh', not 'instant'.
gaps.notes: No Git/pipeline/refresh-sync logic exists. The right implementation is not a standalone object rule but a post-apply advisory attached to LSDL/Prep-for-AI writes (the plan's section 5 'applied status must communicate refresh requirement, not claim instant effect'). Verified-answers-excluded-from-Git half also depends on the still-missing verified-answers layer.

gaps.title: Generate / align a Fabric IQ ontology from the semantic model for shared business vocabulary
gaps.proposedCheck: This is a NEW readiness dimension (ontology-readiness) that largely SUPERVENES on rules we already have: a clean ontology needs well-named (NAME-MEASURE/NAME-COLUMN/NAME-DUP), well-described (DESC-*) entities and clean relationships (REL-BIDI bidirectional avoidance, DATE-MARK marked date table). So coverage is partial-by-composition: the underlying signals exist but there is no aggregate 'ontology-readiness' score or dimension. Proposed rule/dimension: an LLM-judgment roll-up (AI-content) over entity naming/description/relationship cleanliness that grades how well the model would bootstrap a Fabric IQ ontology; could be a per-consumer profile (reweighting existing rules) rather than brand-new predicates. Fix path: the same MCP tools that fix the underlying rules (rename_object, set_description, set_relationship_crossfilter, mark_date_table).
gaps.notes: No Fabric IQ / ontology concept exists in code, but the constituent quality signals (naming, descriptions, relationships, date table) are already rules. Cheapest path is a reweighted 'ontology' profile over existing rules plus a roll-up score, not a new detector set. Maps naturally to the per-consumer-profile idea in ai-readiness-plan.md section 3.

gaps.title: Prepare report visuals with descriptive titles (visual metadata grounds NL2DAX)
gaps.proposedCheck: Detectable in principle but OUT OF SCOPE for our current model: visual titles live in the report layer (.pbir/.pbix/report.json), not in the TOM semantic model that Semanticus analyzes. All our rules scope over Model/Tables/Columns/Measures/Relationships/Cultures; we never read report definitions. Proposed rule (DESC-VISUAL-TITLE in Descriptions): if report definitions are reachable, flag visuals (including hidden visuals/pages) with empty or generic titles ('Chart', 'Table1') on AI-ready models, since visual metadata grounds NL2DAX. Detection: deterministic string check over report visual titles. Fix path: advisory/Proposal only — report-layer bindings are explicitly NOT editable from TOM (our own rename_object description and plan.md guardrails call this out), so there is no MCP write tool; remediation is manual in the report.
gaps.notes: Entirely report-layer; Semanticus today has no report-definition reader and all readiness rules are TOM-only. ai-readiness-plan.md repeatedly flags that report-layer (.pbix/PBIR) bindings can't be seen or validated from TOM, which is exactly why this is missing and only partially buildable (read-if-reachable, never auto-fixable).

gaps.title: Data agent and source workspace capacities must be co-located (region) and F2+/P1+
gaps.proposedCheck: NOT deterministically checkable from model metadata — region co-location, F2+/P1+ capacity SKU, and cross-geo AI tenant settings are Fabric service/tenant facts, invisible to the TOM model. Could become deterministic only with a Fabric REST/capacity API call (which Semanticus does not make today). Proposed as a DataAgentConfig (or new Scale category) pre-flight advisory gate: before recommending the model for data-agent deployment, warn that source and agent workspace capacities must be co-located in-region and on F2+/P1+ with cross-geo AI enabled, else the agent can't execute queries. Fix path: external/advisory — no model predicate, no MCP tool; if a Fabric capacity API is later wired in, it could become a true deterministic pre-flight gate.
gaps.notes: No capacity/region/SKU awareness anywhere in Semanticus; there is also no Scale category in the enum (the requirement's 'Scale' category doesn't exist in our code — only CopilotLimits). This is environmental governance that lives outside the model file, so it belongs as DataAgentConfig advisory unless/until a Fabric REST capacity probe is added.

launchReadiness.exitRule: The 199 requirement rows remain a research ledger. The historical 1.0 cutoff is preserved, while the next-release completion lane may add high-quality deterministic model-visible rules in partitioned batches. Rows assigned to residualBuckets are documented non-goals until their named boundary changes.

criticMissed.area: Row label and advanced linguistic phrasings
criticMissed.why: The legacy LSDL carries row labels/phrasings, but there is no safe typed writer and the new Copilot Tooling Format schema is not authoritatively published. Do not guess at storage for 1.0.

criticMissed.area: AI data-schema dependency closure and verified-answer hidden/stale references
criticMissed.why: Presence, empty-schema, verified-answer presence, AI-instruction presence/length and model writers ship. Exact dependency/ref validation needs a confirmed per-entry storage schema; keep the existing fail-loud advisory when the new tooling format is present.

criticMissed.area: Capacity, tenant, region, sovereign-cloud and Approved-for-Copilot state
criticMissed.why: These are service/environment facts, not semantic-model metadata. They belong in a connection/deployment checklist and must never move the model's readiness grade.

criticMissed.area: Report visual titles and hidden page/visual grounding
criticMissed.why: The model analyzer cannot see report metadata unless a PBIR/report is explicitly supplied. Keep this as an honest blind spot rather than inferring it from model fields.

criticMissed.area: Subjective semantic quality (overlapping intent, standardized values, domain scope, whether RLS is required)
criticMissed.why: These cannot be determined safely from names or object counts. They are suited to the Model Interview, workflows and human review, not deterministic scoring rules.

criticVerdict: Enough for 1.0. Semanticus ships 81 offline and 2 live built-in readiness rules covering the high-value model-visible path: naming/descriptions/synonyms, visibility, formats and sort order, relationship/star-shape risks, Prep-for-AI Q&A/schema/instructions/verified answers, helper and duplicate measures, calculation-group/field-parameter complexity, implicit measures, deterministic DAX practice and Q&A scale/cardinality limits. The next-release completion ledger records 62 covered, 50 partial and 87 missing rows. Its 138 residual non-goals remain explicit because they depend on unpublished linguistic storage, partial Prep-for-AI storage, service/report state, or subjective business judgment. Excluding covered, rejected and bucketed rows leaves two objective gaps that need new live Engine statistics: maximum text length for the Q&A index and null/blank profiling.

rejected.title: Harmful/problematic phrases in metadata break Copilot entirely
rejected.note: MISMATCH. The supportingQuote provided ('When a question is related to data in the semantic model, Copilot will use the semantic model...otherwise it may answer from the LLM's general knowledge') IS on the page, but it is about semantic-model-vs-LLM answering — it does NOT support the requirement's actual claim that 'harmful or problematic phrases in schema/metadata break Copilot and all prompts error out' or that names/descriptions/synonyms should be scanned for content-filter terms. The page (this FAQ) contains nothing about harmful/problematic metadata phrases breaking Copilot. The quote does not back the stated requirement; supported=false.

rejected.title: Data Agent requires read access and qualifying capacity
rejected.note: NOT SUPPORTED by the cited quote. The supportingQuote 'When a user asks a question, the data agent generates a DAX query and runs it against your model.' is on the page, but it only describes query execution. It does NOT state any F2+/P1+ capacity requirement, read-access prerequisite, or write-for-Prep-for-AI rule. Those prerequisite claims are not backed by this page (they would need a different source). Page is current, but the requirement is not supported here.

rejected.title: Mark the model 'Approved for Copilot' when ready
rejected.note: NOT OFFLINE-DETECTABLE (batch 2). 'Approved for Copilot' (formerly 'prepped for AI') is a SERVICE-SIDE item flag set in the semantic-model settings — it is NOT stored in the model file (TMDL/.bim/PBIP) that Semanticus reads offline (confirmed in CLAUDE.md's Prep-for-AI notes). The TOM Model.Description is a distinct, ambiguous signal (a model description, not the approval state), so it can't stand in. This is an out-of-band governance checklist item, not a metadata rule. Marked deterministic:true in the catalog, but only against SERVICE metadata we don't have offline.

rejected.title: Measures must be in a valid (error-free) state
rejected.note: NOT OFFLINE-DETERMINISTIC (batch 2). A measure's calculation-error state is only known after the engine evaluates/recalculates the model; the offline metadata (TOM over a .bim/TMDL that hasn't been processed) carries the DAX text but NOT an error flag. Detecting a broken measure therefore needs a LIVE connection (the analyzer's ReadinessLiveStats/LiveRules path), which is out of scope for this offline batch. Deterministic given a live engine, but not from offline model metadata alone.
